title: Indonesia’S President Confirms Ongoing Zero‑Tariff Negotiations With The United States
body: President Prabowo Subianto Said At The Apec Summit That Talks With The United States On Zero‑Percent Import Tariffs For Indonesian Commodities Such As Palm Oil, Cocoa, And Rubber Remain Underway, With A View To Mirror Malaysia’s Recent Tariff‑Reduction Agreement. Coordinating Minister Airlangga Hartarto Noted That Critical‑Mineral Discussions Are Proceeding Separately, While Acting Director General Putu Juli Ardika Expressed Confidence That A Similar Deal Would Level The Playing Field For Indonesian Exports.

title: Garuda Indonesia Reports Loss Of Usd 131.22 Million For First Nine Months Of 2025
body: Garuda Indonesia Posted A Net Loss Of Usd 131.22 Million For The First Nine Months Of 2025, A 39% Increase From The Same Period Last Year, While Total Operating Revenue Fell 6.64% To Usd 2.39 Billion. Operating Expenses Dropped To Usd 2.28 Billion, But The Company’s Equity Declined To A Negative Usd 1.53 Billion And Total Liabilities Rose To Usd 8.28 Billion.

title: Bayan Resources Reports Q3 2025 Net Income Of Usd 522.15 Million, Down 15.9% Yoy, With Revenue Slightly Lower At Usd 2.43 Billion
body: Bayan Resources Posted A Net Income Of Usd 522.15 Million For The Quarter Ended 30 September 2025, A 15.89% Decline From Usd 620.8 Million A Year Earlier, While Revenue Fell 1.61% To Usd 2.43 Billion. The Company’s Equity Rose To Usd 2.4 Billion, But Total Assets Fell To Usd 3.04 Billion And Liabilities Dropped Sharply To Usd 599.91 Million.

title: PT Timah Reports A 33.71% Drop In First Nine Months Of 2025, With Revenue Falling 20% To Rp6.6 Trillion And Net Profit Per Share Declining To Rp81
body: PT Timah's Revenue Slid 20% From Rp8.25 Trillion To Rp6.6 Trillion, While Gross Profit Fell From Rp2.2 Trillion To Rp1.53 Trillion. Net Profit Per Share Dropped To Rp81 From Rp122, And Total Assets Rose To Rp13.69 Trillion, Though Liabilities Increased To Rp6.08 Trillion.

title: Impack Pratama Posts 15.5% Yoy Profit Rise In First Nine Months Of 2025 As Harimas Tunggal Perkasa Sells 500 Million Shares
body: Impack Pratama Reported A Net Profit Of Rp465.76 Billion For The First Nine Months Of 2025, Up 15.53% Year‑On‑Year, With Revenue Of Rp3.02 Trillion And Eps Increasing To Rp8.58. On 30 October 2025, Harimas Tunggal Perkasa Disposed Of 500 Million Shares At Rp2,000 Each, Raising Rp1 Trillion And Reducing Its Stake To 22.62 Billion Shares (41.22% Of The Company).

title: Adaro Andalan Indonesia Reports 45.8% Decline In Net Profit For The Year Ended 30 September 2025
body: The Company Posted A Net Profit Of Usd 587.32 Million, Down 45.79% Year‑Over‑Year, With Earnings Per Share Falling To Usd 0.07542 From Usd 0.15335. Revenue Decreased 10.89% To Usd 3.6 Billion, While Total Assets Rose To Usd 6.11 Billion And Equity Increased To Usd 3.79 Billion.

title: Astrindo Per Posts Usd5.41 Million Loss For 30 Sept 2025 And Winsome Investment Ltd Acquires 5.19% Stake
body: Astrindo Per Reported A Net Loss Of Usd5.41 Million For The Period Ended 30 September 2025, A 251.54% Decline From The Prior‑Year Surplus, With Revenue Falling 53.39% To Usd194.82 Million And Earnings Per Share Turning Negative To Usd0.000085. On 29 October 2025, Winsome Investment Ltd Purchased 3.3 Billion Shares (5.19% Of The Company) From Cgs International Securities Singapore Pte Ltd, Leaving Cgs With 8.09 Billion Shares (12.71%) And The Deal Was Executed Through Danatama Makmur Sekuritas And Cgs International Sekuritas Indonesia.

title: Intiland Development Reports Steep 93.6% Drop In Net Profit For Q3 2025
body: For The Quarter Ended 30 September 2025, Intiland Development Posted A Net Profit Of Idr 18.22 Billion, A 93.6% Decline From Idr 285.13 Billion A Year Earlier, And Earnings Per Share Fell To Idr 1.76 From Idr 27.51. Revenue Contracted 14.64% To Idr 1.69 Trillion, While Total Equity Rose Modestly To Idr 6.77 Trillion And Total Assets Slipped To Idr 13.51 Trillion.

title: Leyand International Posts Narrowed Q3 2025 Loss And Jsi Sinergi Mas Raises Its Shareholding To 39.2%
body: For The Quarter Ended 30 September 2025, Leyand International Recorded A Net Loss Of Rp1.22 Billion (Down 61.87% Yoy) With Earnings Per Share Of Rp0.41, While Sales Rose 26.33% To Rp211.38 Billion And Gross Profit More Than Doubled To Rp11.11 Billion. On 30 October 2025, Jsi Sinergi Mas Acquired An Additional 1.04 Billion Shares (26.28% Of The Company), Increasing Its Total Stake To 39.23% And Moving Toward A Targeted 51% Ownership Alongside Other Major Shareholders.

title: Global Digital Niaga Posts Q3 2025 Loss Of Rp1.84 Trillion Despite 25% Revenue Growth
body: For The Quarter Ended 30 September 2025, Global Digital Niaga Reported A Net Loss Of Rp1.84 Trillion (Loss Per Share Rp14) While Net Revenue Rose 25.55% Year‑On‑Year To Rp15.23 Trillion And Gross Profit Increased 14.59% To Rp2.67 Trillion. Total Equity Fell To Rp9.12 Trillion, Liabilities Grew To Rp8.4 Trillion, And Total Assets Rose To Rp17.52 Trillion.

title: Citra Marga Nusaphala Persada Reports 19.8% Profit Decline In First Nine Months 2025 As Revenue Rises 25% And Founder Mohammad Jusuf Hamka Lifts Stake To 12.75%
body: Citra Marga Nusaphala Persada Posted A Net Profit Of Idr 722.92 Billion For The First Nine Months Of 2025, A 19.78% Decline Yoy, With Revenue Jumping 25% To Idr 3.4 Trillion And Earnings Per Share Falling To Idr 130. Meanwhile, Mohammad Jusuf Hamka Acquired An Additional 280 Million Shares, Bringing His Total Holding To 845.01 Million Shares (12.75% Of The Company) After The Transaction Completed On 30 Oct 2025, Assisted By Profindo Sekuritas Indonesia And Hsbc Indonesia.

title: PT Hartadinata Abadi Tbk Posts 90.7% Yoy Increase In Q3 2025 Net Profit To Rp575.75 Billion
body: Net Profit Attributable To The Parent Rose To Rp575.75 Billion, Up 90.7% Yoy, Driven By A Near‑Doubling Of Net Sales To Rp25.19 Trillion, While Total Assets Grew To Rp8.17 Trillion And Cash And Cash Equivalents Surged 458.2% To Rp1.14 Trillion. Following The Q3 2025 Results, Hrta Shares Gained 2.42% To Rp1,270 Per Share.

title: Ciputra Development Tbk (Ctra) Posts 27.5% Yoy Net Profit Increase In Q3 2025
body: The Property Developer Recorded A Net Profit Attributable To The Parent Of Rp1.62 Trillion (Up 27.55% Yoy) And Revenue Of Rp8.39 Trillion (Up 18% Yoy), Lifting Earnings Per Share To Rp88. Meanwhile, Total Assets Fell To Rp46.19 Trillion, Liabilities To Rp20.35 Trillion, Equity Rose To Rp25.83 Trillion, And The Share Price Slipped 2.75% To Rp885.

title: J Resources Asia Pacific Reports 479% Surge In Net Income For First Nine Months Of 2025
body: J Resources Asia Pacific Posted Net Income Of Usd 25.8 Million For The First Nine Months Of 2025, Up 479.77% From Usd 4.45 Million In The Same Period Last Year, While Sales Rose 27.45% To Usd 221.59 Million. The Company’s Earnings Per Share Climbed To Usd 0.0010, And Its Accumulated Equity Increased To Usd 441.59 Million, Though Total Assets Fell To Usd 854.17 Million.

title: Bapanas Sets 6-Month Rice Stock Rotation Period, Highlights 3.9 Million Tons In Bulog Warehouses
body: Bapanas Confirms A Maximum Six‑Month Rotation Period For Rice Stock To Preserve Quality, In Line With A Directive From House Representatives Commission Iv. The Agency Notes That Bulog’s Warehouses Now Hold 3.9 Million Tons Of Rice, Approaching The All‑Time High Of 4.2 Million Tons Reached In June 2025.

title: Indonesia And Timor-Leste Sign Mou To Develop Submarine Cable System
body: PT Telekomunikasi Indonesia International And Cabos De Timor-Leste Signed A Memorandum Of Understanding To Explore A Bilateral Submarine Cable System, With Telin Ceo Budi Satria Dharma Purba Emphasizing The Partnership’s Role In Regional Digital Transformation. Timor‑Leste’s Minister Of Transport And Communications Miguel Marques Gonçalves Manetelu Highlighted The Initiative As A Strategic Investment In Digital Infrastructure And Capacity Building For The Country’s Future.

title: PT Globe Kita Terang Tbk Pursues Green Economy Expansion While Facing Revenue Decline And Share Suspension
body: PT Globe Kita Terang Tbk, Formerly PT Trada Alam Minera Tbk, Is Shifting From Mobile Phone Trade To Green Economy Ventures Such As Circular Waste Management And Organic Fertilizer Through Its Subsidiary PT Herbal Globe Natural, While Its Mobile Phone Revenue Fell From Rp52 Billion To Rp7 Billion And Coffee Machine Sales Now Top Rp8.5 Billion. The Company’s Shares Have Been Suspended Since January 2020 Over A Corruption Case Involving PT Asuransi Jiwasraya And Its Chief Commissioner Heru Hidayat, And The Shares May Be Delisted If The Suspension Is Not Lifted.

title: Pan Brothers Reports Net Profit Of Usd 93.96 Million For Fy 2025, Reversing Prior Year Loss
body: Pan Brothers Posted A Net Profit Of Usd 93.96 Million For The Year Ended 30 September 2025, A 121% Increase From The Usd 443.67 Million Loss Reported In The Same Period Last Year. Despite A 17.95% Decline In Sales To Usd 202.3 Million, The Company’s Earnings Before Tax Rose To Usd 90.44 Million, And Total Equity Improved To Usd 123.95 Million From A Negative Balance The Previous Year.

title: Hillcon Reports First Nine Months 2025 Losses, Raises Capital Through Equity Sale
body: For The First Nine Months Of 2025, Hillcon Posted A Net Loss Of Rp134.19 Billion, A Sharp Reversal From A Rp9.35 Billion Profit In The Same Period Last Year, While Its Operating Loss Jumped To Rp195.7 Billion From A Profit Of Rp326.65 Billion. The Company Raised Rp127.35 Billion By Selling 578.9 Million Shares At Rp220 Each, Reducing The Controlling Shareholder’s Stake To 44.47 Percent.

title: Abm Investama Reports 9-Month Net Profit Of Usd 42.38M, Down 62% Yoy
body: Abm Investama’s First Nine Months Of 2025 Generated A Net Profit Of Usd 42.38 Million, A 62% Decline From Usd 111.94 Million In The Same Period Last Year, While Revenue From Customer Contracts Fell 12.52% To Usd 781.64 Million. Operating Profit Dropped To Usd 32.64 Million, Net Profit To Usd 42.2 Million, But Total Equity Rose To Usd 865.23 Million, With Liabilities Decreasing To Usd 1.21 Billion And Assets Slightly Down To Usd 2.07 Billion.

title: Sawit Sumbermas Posts Idr 1 Trillion Net Profit, Up 66.66% In Jan‑Sept 2025
body: Sawit Sumbermas Recorded A Net Profit Of Idr 1 Trillion For The January‑September 2025 Period, A 66.66% Increase Year‑On‑Year, With Earnings Per Share Rising To Idr 105.13 And Contract Revenue Climbing 49% To Idr 11.01 Trillion. The Company’s Gross Profit Grew 64.86% To Idr 3.66 Trillion, Equity Rose To Idr 3.5 Trillion, While Liabilities Fell To Idr 7.67 Trillion And Total Assets Slipped To Idr 11.17 Trillion.

title: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk. Atau Bbri Approves Up To Rp3 Trillion Share Buyback Amid Undervalued Stock And Strong Q3 2025 Results
body: The Company Received Shareholder Approval For A Share Repurchase Program Of Up To Rp3 Trillion, To Be Carried Out On And Off The Exchange Within 12 Months, Citing The Stock’s Undervaluation (Pbv 1.80X, 81% Of Analysts Rating Buy With An Average Target Price Of Rp4,651). In Q3 2025 Bbri Reported Net Profit Of Rp41.2 Trillion, Assets Of Rp2,123.4 Trillion (+8.2% Yoy), Third‑Party Funds Of Rp1,474.8 Trillion (+8.2% Yoy), Loan Growth Of 6.3%, A Capital Adequacy Ratio Of 25.4% And A Loan‑Deposit Ratio Of 86.5%.

title: PT Cakra Buana Resources Energi Tbk. Potentially Operates The Hilong 106 Vessel To Support Activities In The Hidayah Block
body: PT Cakra Buana Resources Energi Tbk. Has Completed The Acquisition Of The Hilong 106 Vessel, Which May Be Used To Support Oil And Gas Exploration And Production In The Hidayah Block, And This Move Could Be A Step Towards Expanding The Company's Energy And Offshore Business Lines. The Potential Utilization Of The Hilong 106 Vessel In The Project Could Have A Positive Impact On The Company's Valuation, Attracting Investor Attention Due To The Increase In Asset Value And Potential Revenue Growth.

title: Lonsum Reports A 55% Increase In Net Profit To Rp1.25 Trillion As Of September 30, 2025
body: Lonsum's Net Profit Has Increased By 55% To Rp1.25 Trillion, Driven By A 35% Rise In Sales To Rp3.96 Trillion, Primarily Due To Higher Average Selling Prices And Increased Sales Volume Of Palm Oil Products. The Company's Ebitda Has Also Surged By 58% To Rp1.61 Trillion, With Total Assets Reaching Rp14.78 Trillion, Up From Rp13.84 Trillion At The End Of The Previous Year.

title: Wismilak Reports A 37% Increase In Net Profit To Rp284.96 Billion For The First Nine Months Of 2025.
body: Wismilak's Net Profit Surged 37% To Rp284.96 Billion, Driven By A 34% Increase In Net Sales To Rp4.6 Trillion, With Laba Per Saham Dasar Rising To Rp137.28 From Rp99.97. The Company's Laba Kotor Also Jumped 29.87% To Rp1 Trillion, While Total Aset Grew To Rp3.35 Trillion, Up From Rp3.02 Trillion At The End Of The Previous Year.

title: Financial Performance Of A Company For 30 September 2025 Shows A Significant Decline In Surplus And Net Profit Per Share.
body: The Company's Surplus Declined By 192% To Usd 5.01 Million, Resulting In A Net Loss Per Share Of Usd 0.0021, Compared To A Profit Of Usd 0.0023 In The Same Period Last Year. The Company's Net Sales Also Decreased By 13.67% To Usd 121.58 Million, With A Gross Profit Of Usd 34.57 Million, Down From Usd 43.44 Million In The Same Period Last Year.

title: PT Satria Andalan Prima Tbk Reports Lower Revenue For The Period Ending September 30, 2025
body: PT Satria Andalan Prima Tbk's Revenue Decreased To Idr 402.30 Billion As Of September 30, 2025, Compared To Idr 523.35 Billion In The Same Period Last Year. The Company's Net Profit Also Declined To Idr 911.34 Million From Idr 967.33 Million.

title: PT Avia Avian Tbk. Announces An Interim Dividend Of Rp11 Per Share For The 2025 Fiscal Year, Totaling Approximately Rp600 Billion In Dividend Value.
body: PT Avia Avian Tbk., The Company Behind The Avian Brands, Has Declared An Interim Dividend Of Rp11 Per Share, Reflecting Its Commitment To Providing Sustainable Value To Shareholders Amidst Challenging Market Conditions. The Company's Financial Performance Includes A 9.7% Increase In Sales To Rp5.9 Trillion And A Net Profit Of Rp1.18 Trillion With A 20% Net Profit Margin For The First Nine Months Of 2025.

title: PT Atlas Resources Tbk. Appoints New Board Members And Approves Private Placement
body: PT Atlas Resources Tbk. Has Appointed A New Board Member And Approved A Private Placement Of Up To 319 Million Shares, Representing 8.5% Of The Company's Issued Capital. The Private Placement Aims To Strengthen The Company's Capital Structure And Support Business Expansion.

title: PT Koka Indonesia Tbk. Clarifies Relationship With Nlem And Liqin Group Amid Acquisition Plans
body: PT Koka Indonesia Tbk. Has Clarified Its Relationship With Nlem And Liqin Group, Stating That Liqin Group Has Indirect Ownership Of Nlem's Shares. The Clarification Comes As PT Koka Indonesia Tbk. Is Set To Be Acquired By Ningbo Lixing Enterprise Management Co., Ltd. And Aims To Complete The Transaction Within Three Months.

title: PT Sinar Mas Multiartha Tbk. Provides Idr 433.95 Billion Loan To Subsidiary PT Ab Sinar Mas Multifinance
body: PT Sinar Mas Multiartha Tbk. Has Extended A Idr 433.95 Billion Loan To Its Subsidiary PT Ab Sinar Mas Multifinance With A Five-Year Tenor And An Effective Interest Rate Of 10.5% Per Annum. The Loan Is Sourced From The Proceeds Of The Company's Sustainable Iii Tier I 2024 Bond Issuance.

title: PT Artalindo Semesta Nusantara Buy Shares of PT Yelooo Integra Datanet Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Artalindo Semesta Nusantara bought shares of PT Yelooo Integra Datanet Tbk (YELO.JK) at approximately IDR 66, increasing holdings from 709,349,998 to 715,299,998. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

body: According to the published announcement, Dr. Pulung Peranginangin buy shares of Gema Grahasarana Tbk (GEMA.JK) at approximately IDR 88, increasing holdings from 7,450,000 to 7,550,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Rondy Yunanda Buy Shares of PT Ecocare Indo Pasifik Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Rondy Yunanda bought shares of PT Ecocare Indo Pasifik Tbk (HYGN.JK) at approximately IDR 167, increasing holdings from 200,000,000 to 201,000,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia Pushes Modernization Of Village Co‑Ops To Attract Youth And Create Jobs
body: Cooperatives Minister Ferry Juliantono Announced A Nationwide Program To Modernize 82,320 Red‑White Village Co‑Ops, Deploying Digital Management Systems, Mentoring, And Infrastructure To Make All Co‑Ops Operational By March 2026 And Potentially Create Up To 1 Million Jobs By December 2025. The Initiative, Backed By State And Regional Budgets, Includes 1,104 Pmos, 8,000 Business Mentors, And Aims To Position Co‑Ops As Hubs For Savings, Loans, And Community Services.

title: Lps Urges Banks To Cut Deposit Rates Amid Rising Customer Rates Above Tbp
body: Lps Urged Banks To Lower Deposit Rates After A 25‑Basis‑Point Cut, Yet The Share Of Customers Receiving Rates Above The Tbp Rose To 32% In September 2025 From 13% In 2022. Lps Coverage Remains Above 90% Of Bank Accounts Nationwide, While 51 Million Indonesians Lack Savings Accounts, Prompting Lps, Kssk, Bank Indonesia, And The Financial Services Authority To Push For Financial Inclusion And New Regulations.

title: Antam Gold Bar Prices Rise On Tuesday, 1‑Gram Spot Up To Rp 2.286 Million
body: On Tuesday, The Price Of 1‑Gram Antam Gold Increased To Rp 2,286,000, Up Rp 8,000 From Monday's Rp 2,278,000. The Antam Buyback Price Also Rose By Rp 8,000 To Rp 2,151,000 Per Gram, With Other Bar Sizes Priced Accordingly And Excluding Tax.

title: Indonesia Launches Cesium‑137‑Free Certification Scheme To Boost Shrimp Exports To The United States
body: The Scheme, Developed In Partnership With The Nuclear Energy Regulatory Agency And The National Research Innovation Agency, Meets Us Fda Import Alert #99‑52 Requirements, Enabling Indonesian Shrimp To Be Verified As Cesium‑137‑Free. The First Certified Shipments Departed From Tanjung Priok On October 31 And November 1, Heading To New York, Los Angeles, Miami, And Jacksonville, With The Kkp Agency Acting As The Us‑Fda‑Appointed Certifying Body.

title: PT Pabrik Kertas Tjiwi Kimia Tbk Reports 108% Net Profit Increase Despite 2.7% Sales Decline
body: For The January‑September 2025 Period, PT Pabrik Kertas Tjiwi Kimia Tbk Posted A Net Profit Of Us$213.22 Million, Up 107.72% Yoy, While Sales Fell 2.68% To Us$738.93 Million, Driven By Higher Associate Earnings, A Us$16.21 Million Net Currency Gain And Other Income. Analyst Muhammad Wafi Expects Annual Net Profit Growth Of 60‑70% Yoy, Rates The Stock As Cheap (Pb ~0.7×, Pe <6×) And Recommends Buying With A Target Price Of Rp 8,300 Per Share.

title: Indonesian Retail Issuers Post Only Single‑Digit Sales Growth In Jan‑Sep 2025 As Consumer Purchasing Power Weakens
body: From January To September 2025, Most Retail Companies Such As PT Aspirasi Hidup Indonesia Tbk, PT Sumber Alfaria Trijaya Tbk, PT Midi Utama Indonesia Tbk, PT Map Aktif Adiperkasa Tbk, PT Dfi Retail Nusantara Tbk And PT Erajaya Swasembada Tbk Recorded Modest Single‑Digit Revenue Increases, With Only PT Map Aktif Adiperkasa Tbk Achieving Double‑Digit Growth Of 12.28%; PT Ramayana Lestari Sentosa Tbk And PT Matahari Department Store Tbk Posted Declines Of 11.15% And 9.98% Respectively. Analysts Attribute The Muted Performance To Reduced Middle‑To‑Lower‑Income Spending, High Food Inflation, Fuel Price Adjustments And A High‑Base Effect, But Note That Fiscal Stimulus, Rising M2 Liquidity And Seasonal Demand Could Boost Retail Sales In The Second Half Of 2025, Prompting Buy‑Weakness Recommendations For Amrt And Buy‑Breakout For Mapi.

title: PT Astra International Tbk Announces Up To Rp 2 Trillion Share Buyback Program
body: PT Astra International Tbk Will Repurchase Shares Worth Up To Rp 2 Trillion Between 3 November 2025 And 30 January 2026, Using Internal Cash And Limiting The Buyback To 20% Of Paid‑Up Capital While Keeping Free‑Float Above 7.5% Of Total Capital. Analysts View The Buyback As A Signal That The Stock Is Undervalued, Noting A Pe Ratio Of About 7.5× For 2026 And Maintaining A Target Price Of Rp 6,700 Per Share.

title: Indonesian Conglomerate-Listed Companies Post Mixed Q1‑Q3 2025 Performance, Highlighted By PT Barito Pacific Tbk'S 233% Revenue Surge
body: From January To September 2025, PT Barito Pacific Tbk (Brpt) Recorded A 232.93% Yoy Revenue Jump To Us$5.56 Billion And A 2,072% Rise In Net Profit To Us$26.8 Million, Driven By Downstream, Petrochemical And Green‑Energy Expansion, While Coal‑Exposed Groups Such As Adaro And Astra, As Well As Retail And Property Arms Of The Salim, Astra And Sinarmas Groups, Faced Earnings Pressure. Analysts Stress That Diversified Conglomerates—Especially Those With Strong Exposure To Energy, Infrastructure And Technology—Remain Resilient And Many Stocks Appear Undervalued, Recommending Gradual Accumulation.

title: Mohammad Jusuf Hamka Increases His Stake In Citra Marga Nusaphala Through Recent Stock Purchases
body: Mohammad Jusuf Hamka Has Acquired An Additional 46,464,896 Shares Of Citra Marga Nusaphala, Bringing His Total Holdings To 891.48 Million Shares, Equivalent To 13.36% Of The Company. This Purchase Follows A Previous Acquisition Of 280 Million Shares On October 30, 2025, Which Was Facilitated By Profindo Sekuritas Indonesia And Bank Hsbc Indonesia.

title: PT Leyand International Tbk Reports Strong Sales Growth And Improved Profitability In Q3 2025
body: For The Quarter Ended 30 September 2025, PT Leyand International Tbk Posted Sales Of Rp211.4 Billion, Up 26.35% Year‑On‑Year, And Gross Profit Of Rp11.1 Billion, A 113.5% Increase, While Its Loss Narrowed To Rp1.2 Billion From Rp3.2 Billion. The Company Also Reduced Liabilities To Rp152.7 Billion, Maintained Equity At Rp26.7 Billion, And Is Pursuing An Acquisition Of PT Jsi Sinergi Mas.

title: Intiputera Bumititra And Keraton Investments Ltd Sell All Leyand International Shares, Raising Rp10 Billion
body: On 30 October 2025, The Two Shareholders Divested A Combined 1,042,396,276 Shares Of Leyand International At Rp10 Per Share, Generating Rp10 Billion In Proceeds. Intiputera Bumititra Sold 534.13 Million Shares Worth Rp5.34 Billion And Exited Its Stake, While Keraton Investments Ltd Sold 508.26 Million Shares Worth Rp5.08 Billion, Also Ending Its Shareholding, Citing A Portfolio‑Investment Strategy.

title: Apexindo Reports 55.67% Increase In Nine‑Month 2025 Net Profit To Usd1.51 Million
body: Apexindo Posted A Net Profit Of Usd1.51 Million For The First Nine Months Of 2025, A 55.67% Increase From Usd971,980 A Year Earlier, With Basic Earnings Per Share Rising To Usd0.0004. Revenue Reached Usd63.72 Million, Gross Profit Usd16.71 Million, Total Equity Usd66.53 Million, Liabilities Usd176.33 Million And Assets Usd242.87 Million.

title: Chandra Daya Investasi Executes A Rp240 Billion Transaction Involving Chandra Warehouse Cilegon And Panca Puri Perkasa.
body: The Transaction, Which Was Finalized On 30 October 2025, Involves The Purchase Of A 30,091 Square Meter Warehouse And Supporting Facilities By Chandra Warehouse Cilegon From Panca Puri Perkasa. The Acquired Asset Will Be Used To Develop Warehousing Activities And Support The Operational Activities Of Chandra Warehouse Cilegon, With The Transaction Valued At Rp240 Billion.

title: Indo Tambangraya Megah Plans To Conduct A Maximum Buyback Of Idr 2.49 Trillion
body: Indo Tambangraya Megah Has Received Approval From Investors To Conduct A Buyback Of Up To Idr 2.49 Trillion, Which Will Be Funded From Internal Cash Reserves. The Company Aims To Support Long-Term Growth And Stability Of Its Stock Price Through This Buyback Plan.

title: PT Suparma Tbk., A National Paper Producer, Approves Share Dividend Distribution To Its Shareholders
body: PT Suparma Tbk. Has Approved The Distribution Of Share Dividends To Its Shareholders, With A Total Of Idr 378.49 Billion To Be Capitalized From Retained Earnings By The End Of 2024. The Company Also Increased Its Issued Capital From Idr 1.26 Trillion To A Maximum Of Idr 1.64 Trillion, With The Number Of Shares Increasing From 3.15 Billion To 4.10 Billion.

title: Bursa Efek Indonesia Temporarily Suspends Trading Of PT Wijaya Karya Tbk. Due To Non-Payment Of Sukuk And Obligations
body: PT Wijaya Karya Tbk. Has Been Temporarily Suspended From Trading On The Bursa Efek Indonesia Due To Its Inability To Pay The Principal Of Sukuk Mudharabah Berkelanjutan Iii Wijaya Karya Tahap I 2022, Which Is Worth Idr 281.82 Billion. The Company Is Facing Liquidity Constraints Due To A Decline In Construction Industry Performance.

title: PT Jasuindo Tiga Perkasa Tbk Increases Capital For Its Subsidiary PT Jasuindo Informatika Pratama
body: PT Jasuindo Tiga Perkasa Tbk Has Increased The Capital Of Its Subsidiary PT Jasuindo Informatika Pratama, Resulting In A Significant Increase In Shares From 5 Million To 25 Million, With The Parent Company's Ownership Rising To 99.99%. The Capital Injection Of Rp2.5 Billion Is Intended To Strengthen The Subsidiary's Financial Position Without Significantly Impacting The Parent Company's Operations Or Financial Condition.

title: Indonesia’S Palm Oil Association Says Companies Ready For Eu Deforestation‑Free Regulation, But Smallholders Pose Biggest Challenge
body: Gapki, Indonesia’s Palm Oil Association, Reports That Companies Are Prepared For The Eu Deforestation‑Free Regulation (Eudr) Effective December 2025, But Smallholders Remain The Main Hurdle, As They Must Meet Traceability And Due‑Diligence Requirements. Despite A Decline In European Palm‑Oil Exports From 4.1 Million Tonnes In 2023 To 3.3 Million Tonnes In 2024, The Government And Eu Are Negotiating A One‑Year Transition For Smallholders To Avoid Significant Industry Impact By 2026, While The Indonesia‑Eu Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement Is Expected To Ease Trade Barriers.

title: Indonesia’S Finance Minister Reassures Nusantara Project Won’T Become Ghost City Amid Ongoing Construction And Planned Funding
body: Indonesia’s Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa Reassured That The New Capital Nusantara Will Not Become A Ghost City, Citing Ongoing Construction And Planned Government Funding, While The Nusantara Capital Authority Projects That Up To 20,000 Workers Will Be Employed And That Legislative And Judicial Complexes Covering 42 And 15 Hectares Will Cost Rp8.5 Trillion And Rp3.1 Trillion Respectively, With Construction Slated To Begin In November And Last 25 Months.

title: Indonesia To End Diesel Imports By Early 2026, Minister Confirms
body: Indonesia’s Minister Of Energy And Mineral Resources Bahlil Lahadalia Announced That The Country Will Stop Importing Diesel Fuel Starting Early 2026, Following The Inauguration Of The Balikpapan Refinery And The Rollout Of The B50 Biodiesel Program, Which Is Expected To Create A Surplus Of Diesel. The Government Currently Imports About 4.9–5 Million Tons Of Diesel Annually, A Figure That The New Domestic Production And Biofuel Initiatives Aim To Eliminate.

title: PT Indo Tambangraya Megah Tbk Approves Up To Idr 2.49 Trillion Share Buyback At Extraordinary Shareholders Meeting
body: PT Indo Tambangraya Megah Tbk Held An Extraordinary Shareholders Meeting And Approved A Share Buyback Of Up To Idr 2.49 Trillion Using Internal Cash, To Be Executed On The Indonesia Stock Exchange Within 12 Months With Full Discretion Granted To The Directors. Management Said The Buyback Reflects The Stock’s Undervaluation, Aims To Enhance Shareholder Returns And Price Stability, And Noted A Q1‑2025 Net Profit Of Us$482.52 Million.

title: PT Sentra Investa Maksima Sell Shares of PT Lavender Bina Cendikia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Sentra Investa Maksima sold shares of PT Lavender Bina Cendikia Tbk (BMBL.JK) at approximately IDR 45, decreasing holdings from 111,195,100 to 108,335,100. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Ali Gunawan Buy Shares of PT Allo Bank Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Ali Gunawan bought shares of PT Allo Bank Indonesia Tbk (BBHI.JK) at approximately IDR 29, increasing holdings from 10,167,300 to 10,238,900. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Wir Asia Reports 26.83% Increase In Net Profit To Idr 74.82 Billion For January-September 2025
body: Wir Asia's Net Profit Surged 26.83% To Idr 74.82 Billion In The January-September 2025 Period, Driven By A 3.72% Increase In Revenue To Idr 1.95 Trillion. The Company's Total Assets Also Rose To Idr 1.53 Trillion, While Total Liabilities Increased To Idr 642.11 Billion.

title: PT Harvest Capital International Buy Shares of PT Arkadia Digital Media Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Harvest Capital International bought shares of PT Arkadia Digital Media Tbk (DIGI.JK) at approximately IDR 34, increasing holdings from 527,364,800 to 578,947,200. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: President Prabowo Allocates Rp5 Trillion To PT Kai For 30 New Commuter Train Sets In Jakarta
body: President Prabowo Subianto Announced A Rp5 Trillion Allocation To State‑Owned Railway Operator PT Kai To Purchase Roughly 30 New Krl Commuter Train Sets For The Greater Jakarta Network. The Funding Exceeds PT Kai's Request Of Rp4.8 Trillion, Aims To Meet Rising Passenger Demand, And Each Train Set Costs About Us$9 Million With Delivery Targeted Within A Year.

title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Issues Uma Warning For PT Trimegah Karya Pratama Tbk After Sharp Uvcr Price Surge
body: Bei Announced A New Unusual Market Activity (Uma) Caution For PT Trimegah Karya Pratama Tbk, Noting That Uvcr Shares Jumped 80.60% To Rp121 And Continued To Rise Sharply, Though The Warning Does Not Imply A Regulatory Breach And The Exchange Will Monitor The Trading Pattern. Investors Are Urged To Review The Company's Response, Performance, Disclosures And Pending Corporate Actions, As The Stock Surged Another 25.62% To Rp152 The Next Day, Indicating High Volatility.

title: PT Sinergi Inti Andalan Prima Tbk Strengthens Position In Indonesia'S Digital Infrastructure Industry Through Strategic Acquisition
body: PT Sinergi Inti Andalan Prima Tbk Has Signed A Termsheet With PT Trans Hybrid Communication's Shareholders For A 60% Stake Acquisition, Marking The Beginning Of A Collaboration Between Two National Technology Entities To Enhance Digital Infrastructure And Connectivity In Indonesia. The Acquisition Aims To Expand The Company's Business Network In The Fiber Optic, Ip Transit, Cloud, And Data Center Segments.

title: President Prabowo Announces Plan To Extend Whoosh High‑Speed Rail To Banyuwangi Despite Debt Concerns
body: At The Inauguration Of The Renovated Tanah Abang Station, President Prabowo Subianto Said The Whoosh Jakarta‑Bandung High‑Speed Rail Will Be Extended Past Surabaya To Banyuwangi, The Easternmost District Of Java. He Asserted That The Projected Rp1.2 Trillion Annual Debt Is Manageable And Pledged Personal Responsibility For The Project's Financial Sustainability.

title: President Prabowo Inaugurates Upgraded Tanah Abang Baru Station In Jakarta
body: President Prabowo Subianto Opened The Renovated Tanah Abang Baru Commuter‑Rail Station, Completing A Two‑Year Revitalisation Project That Began In April 2023 And Addressing Severe Overcrowding With A Modernised Façade, Expanded Entrance And Dedicated Pedestrian Lanes. He Praised PT Kai And The Ministry Of Transportation, Noting That Indonesia's Railway Network Now Serves Around 486 Million Passengers Annually.

title: Humpuss Maritim Internasional Expands Its Logistics Capacity With The Addition Of A New Lpg Vessel, Hummingbird, Through Its Subsidiary, Pcs Internasional.
body: The New Vessel, Hummingbird, Is A 3,815 Dwt Lpg Tanker Designed To Transport Various Types Of Lpg Mix, And Its Addition Is Part Of Humpuss Maritim Internasional's Strategy To Expand Its Operational Capacity And Support The Growth Of The National Energy Sector. Humpuss Maritim Internasional Targets Adding 10 New Vessels In 2025 With A Total Capital Expenditure Of Usd39.57 Million, And Has Already Acquired 8 Vessels, Including Hummingbird, With A Total Capital Expenditure Of Usd53.14 Million.

title: Cbdk Reports A 74% Increase In Net Profit To Rp1.4 Trillion In Q3 2025, Driven By The Realization Of Strategic Projects In The Pik2 Area.
body: Cbdk's Net Profit Surged 74% To Rp1.4 Trillion In Q3 2025, With Revenue Reaching A Record High Of Rp2.3 Trillion, Up 45% From The Previous Year, Driven By The Realization Of Strategic Projects In The Pik2 Area. The Company's Strong Performance Was Also Reflected In Its Gross Margin, Which Increased From 59% To 69%, And Its Operating Margin, Which Rose From 51% To 62%.

title: PT Multi Makmur Lemindo Tbk Reports Solid Financial Performance In Q3 2025, Driven By Transformation Into A Major Player In The National Energy Ecosystem
body: PT Multi Makmur Lemindo Tbk Achieved A Significant Increase In Revenue To Rp25.98 Billion In Q3 2025, With A Net Profit Of Rp2.66 Billion, Supported By Other Income Of Rp5.47 Billion, And Saw Its Earnings Per Share Rise To Rp0.91. The Company's Transformation, Led By PT Morris Capital Indonesia, Aims To Strengthen Its Position In The Energy Sector Through An Injection Of Assets Worth Approximately Rp3 Trillion.

title: Geoprima Solusi Tbk Shows Strong Optimism In Interim Financial Report
body: Geoprima Solusi Tbk Has Demonstrated A Significant Improvement In Its Financial Performance, With A Notable Increase In Operating Cash Flow And Efficiency In Expenses. The Company's Strong Cash Position And Prudent Management Of Liabilities Have Also Been Highlighted As Key Strengths.

title: Indonesia Pushes For Rare Earth Metal Governance And Downstream Development To Support Energy Transition
body: At A National Alumni Seminar In Bandung, Director Of Mineral And Coal Program Development Julian Ambassadur Outlined The Government's Plan To Classify Tailings As Strategic Minerals And To Revise Regulations, Emphasizing The Potential Of Tailings From Nickel, Tin, And Bauxite To Supply Rare Earth Elements Without New Exploration. The Strategy Includes Seven Steps Such As Multi‑Element Testing, By‑Product Utilization, Priority Industry Designation, Circular Economy Adoption, Fiscal Incentives, And Research Strengthening To Boost Indonesia’s Rare‑Earth Sector.

title: Hengtong Optic Electric International Co., Limited Sell Shares of Voksel Electric Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hengtong Optic Electric International Co., Limited sell shares of Voksel Electric Tbk (VOKS.JK), decreasing holdings from 2,275,612,820 to 2,275,612,720. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Martin Suharlie Buy Shares of PT M Cash Integrasi Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Martin Suharlie bought shares of PT M Cash Integrasi Tbk (MCAS.JK) at approximately IDR 876, increasing holdings from 70,132,000 to 70,311,200. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Skb Food Embarks On A Sustainable Business Journey
body: Under The Leadership Of Eko Pujianto, Skb Food Is Shifting Its Focus Towards A More Sustainable Business Model, Aligning Its Operations With The United Nations' Sustainable Development Goals. The Company Has Reported A 43.04% Revenue Growth In 2024, Reaching Idr 582.84 Billion, With A Significant Contribution From Its Food Supply Segment.

title: Sepu Bata Reports A Net Loss Of Idr 51.98 Billion For The Period Ending September 2025
body: Sepu Bata Reported A Net Loss Of Idr 51.98 Billion For The Period Ending September 2025, A 59.85% Decrease From The Same Period Last Year. The Company's Net Loss Per Share Decreased To Idr 39.99 From Idr 99.61.

title: Cakra Buana Resources Secures Usd 49 Million Loan Facility From Bank Rakyat Indonesia
body: Cakra Buana Resources Has Obtained A Usd 49 Million Loan Facility From Bank Rakyat Indonesia To Support The Acquisition Of A Pipe Laying & Lifting Vessel And Operational Expenses. The Loan Is Expected To Have A Positive Impact On The Company, Enabling It To Diversify Its Business Activities And Increase Revenue.
