body: According to the published announcement, Christopher Brandley Limbono sold shares of PT Agro Bahari Nusantara Tbk (UDNG.JK) at approximately IDR 15. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Christian Brandon Limbono Sell Shares of PT Agro Bahari Nusantara Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Christian Brandon Limbono sold shares of PT Agro Bahari Nusantara Tbk (UDNG.JK) at approximately IDR 15, decreasing holdings from 221,812,500 to 97,500,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Edwin Widjaja Buy Shares of PT Jaya Trishindo Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Edwin Widjaja bought shares of PT Jaya Trishindo Tbk (HELI.JK) at approximately IDR 258, increasing holdings from 81,400 to 189,300. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia Directs Msme Ministry To Promote Local Alternatives For Thrift Traders Amid Import Ban On Secondhand Clothing
body: President Prabowo Subianto Has Instructed The Ministry Of Micro, Small, And Medium Enterprises To Develop Locally Produced Alternatives For Thrift Businesses Following The Enforcement Of A Ban On Imported Secondhand Clothing. The Ministry Will Support Vendors In Transitioning To Domestic Products, Especially From Creative Hubs Like Bandung, While Maintaining The Ability Of Thrift Traders To Continue Operating.

title: President Prabowo Orders 30 New Commuter Trains For Greater Jakarta, Allocating Rp5 Trillion
body: President Prabowo Subianto Has Instructed PT Kereta Api Indonesia To Procure 30 New Commuter Train Sets For Greater Jakarta Within One Year, With Each Set Costing $9 Million And A Total Budget Of Rp4.8 Trillion, Of Which Rp5 Trillion Has Been Allocated. He Emphasized The Importance Of Rail‑Based Public Transport For Mobility And Announced Plans To Expand Railway Infrastructure Beyond Java To Reduce Logistics Costs.

title: Indonesia And The United States Near Finalization Of Zero‑Tariff Deal For Indonesian Commodities By November 4, 2025
body: Trade Minister Budi Santoso Confirmed That Negotiations To Eliminate U.S. Tariffs On Indonesian Products, Including Palm Oil And Cocoa Beans, Are Expected To Conclude This Month, With The Final Agreement Approved By Presidents Prabowo Subianto And Donald Trump. The Deal Would Reduce Import Tariffs From 32% To 19% And Potentially Bring Key Commodities To Zero Percent, Mirroring The U.S.–Malaysia Arrangement.

title: President Prabowo Launches Free‑Baggage Train Service For Farmers And Small Traders
body: President Prabowo Subianto Inaugurated A Special Commuter Train That Allows Farmers And Small Traders To Transport Goods Without Paying Baggage Fees, With Fares Subsidized At 60% By The Government. PT Kereta Api Indonesia Has Allocated Eight Trains For The Merak–Rangkasbitung Route, Operating 14 Daily Trips With Economy‑Class Carriages Featuring 73 Sideways Seats And Widened Doors To Facilitate Loading.

title: President Prabowo Orders Expansion Of Indonesia’S National Railway Network
body: President Prabowo Subianto Directed The Coordinating Minister For Infrastructure And Regional Development, Agus Harimurti Yudhoyono, To Draft Plans For Expanding Railway Networks Across Sumatra, Kalimantan, Java, And Sulawesi, Aiming To Reduce Logistics Costs And Support Natural Resource Distribution. He Also Tested A Commuter Line Service From Manggarai To New Tanah Abang Station During The Inauguration Of The New Station.

title: Indonesian Nickel Producers Record Strong Q3‑2025 Earnings Despite Global Price Weakness, Thanks To Higher Volumes And Downstream Integration
body: PT Pam Mineral Tbk Saw Sales Rise 64.82% Yoy To Rp 1.35 Trillion And Net Profit Jump 131.28% To Rp 401.66 Billion, While PT Central Omega Resources Tbk, PT Trimegah Bangun Persada Tbk (Harita Nickel), Nckl And Inco Also Posted Double‑Digit Sales And Profit Growth In Q3‑2025. Analysts Attribute The Outperformance To Sharp Increases In Ore Shipments (E.G., Nicl Up 88.76% Yoy), Operational Efficiencies And Expanding Smelter Capacity, But Warn That Sustained Gains Depend On Nickel Price Stability, Continued Efficiency Gains, And Progress On Downstream Projects.

title: Msci November 2025 Rebalancing May Add PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk And PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk To Index, Analysts Forecast Price Gains
body: Msci Announced Its November 2025 Index Review, With PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk (Bren) And PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (Brms) Expected To Be Added, Prompting Analysts To Note Rising Market Caps And Bullish Technical Outlooks, With Bren Targeted At Rp 9,975 And Short‑Term Buy Recommendations For Both Stocks. Inclusion Could Boost Demand As Fund Managers Tracking The Msci Index Purchase The Shares.

title: Two Investors, Christian Brandon Limbodo And Christopher Brandley Limbodo, Reduce Their Ownership Of Agro Bahari Nusantara Shares By Selling 283.56 Million Shares At A Price Of Rp15 Per Share.
body: The Duo Sold Their Shares On October 31, 2025, With Christian Brandon Limbodo Selling 124.31 Million Shares For Rp1.86 Billion And Christopher Brandley Limbodo Selling 159.25 Million Shares For Rp2.38 Billion, Resulting In A Total Of Rp4.25 Billion. The Sale Price Was Significantly Lower Than The Market Price Of Rp3,090 Per Share, Representing A Discount Of Approximately 99.51%.

title: President Prabowo Orders PT Kereta Api Indonesia To Add 30 Train Sets Worth Up To Rp5 Trillion To Boost Indonesia’S Rail Capacity
body: At The Inauguration Of The New Tanah Abang Baru Station, President Prabowo Subianto Directed PT Kereta Api Indonesia, Led By President Director Bobby Rasyidin, To Procure 30 Additional Train Sets Within A Year, Each Costing $9 Million, With A Budget Of Rp5 Trillion (Rp4.8 Trillion Proposed). He Also Emphasized Safety, Comfort And Readiness For The Upcoming Holiday Travel Season.

title: Indonesia Seeks Zero Tariffs On Palm Oil, Cocoa And Rubber In Upcoming Us Trade Agreement
body: Coordinating Minister Of Economic Affairs Airlangga Hartarto Said Indonesia Is Negotiating A Us‑Indonesia Trade Deal That Would Eliminate Import Tariffs On Key Commodities Such As Palm Oil, Cocoa And Rubber, And Request Special Treatment For Health‑Care Supply‑Chain Items. The Government Stresses That The Proposal Aims For Fair, Reciprocal Trade While Protecting National Interests, With Legal Drafting Underway And Further Talks Planned After The Apec Summit In Late November 2025.

title: Indonesian Nickel Producers Post Strong Q3 2025 Earnings And Stock Gains Despite Global Price Decline
body: PT Pam Mineral Tbk Reported A 64.82% Yoy Sales Increase To Rp1.35 Trillion And A 131.28% Rise In Net Profit To Rp401.66 Billion For Q3‑2025, While PT Central Omega Resources Tbk Saw Sales Rise 29.51% To Rp1.24 Trillion And Net Profit Climb 52.79% To Rp442.36 Billion, Driven By Higher Production Volumes (Nicl Up 88.76% To 2.4 Mt, Dkft Up 31% To 2.29 Mt) And Operational Efficiencies. Their Shares Surged This Year, With Dkft Up 250%, Nicl Up 278.85%, Nckl Up 52.98% And Inco Up 27.07%, Though Analysts Warn Future Performance Depends On Global Nickel Price Stability And Downstream Project Progress.

title: PT Cipta Sarana Medika Tbk Achieves Positive Financial Growth In Q3 2025
body: PT Cipta Sarana Medika Tbk, Also Known As Dkh Hospitals, Reported A 234% Year-Over-Year Increase In Net Income To Idr 6.82 Billion In The First Nine Months Of 2025, Driven By Efficient Strategies And Reduced Operational Costs. The Company Is Optimistic About Surpassing Its 2025 Targets, With A Projected Net Income Of Idr 8.2 Billion And Revenue Of Idr 165 Billion.

title: PT Waskita Karya Tbk Targets Divestment Of Several Toll Roads To Meet Financial Obligations
body: PT Waskita Karya Tbk Plans To Divest Several Toll Roads, Including Two Roads This Year, To Meet Its Financial Obligations And Strengthen Its Cash Flow. The Company Aims To Complete The Transactions By December 2025 And Is Also Exploring The Possibility Of Receiving Additional State Ownership Through Danareksa Asset Management.

title: Pemegang Saham Pengendali PT Arkadia Digital Media Tbk Increases Stake
body: PT Harvest Capital International Acquired 51.58 Million Shares Of PT Arkadia Digital Media Tbk In Late October 2025, Increasing Its Stake To 35.63% Of The Total Shares Outstanding. The Shares Were Purchased At A Price Range Of Idr 33.2 To Idr 35 Per Share, With An Estimated Total Value Of Idr 1.75 Billion.

title: Global Niaga Restructures Operations, Lays Off 270 Employees Amid Efforts To Enhance Efficiency And Growth
body: Global Niaga Has Completed Its Organizational Restructuring, Resulting In The Permanent Layoff Of 270 Employees, As Part Of Efforts To Optimize Operations And Improve Efficiency. The Company Aims To Achieve Sustainable Growth And Increase Shareholder Value Through Various Strategies, Including Cost Reduction And Digital Transformation.

title: PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk Reports Significant Financial Performance Growth In 2025
body: PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk Has Seen A 43.66% Increase In Net Income To Idr 6.35 Billion As Of September 30, 2025, Driven By A 44.43% Rise In Revenue To Idr 11.93 Billion. The Company's Total Assets Have Grown To Idr 187.57 Billion, With A Significant Increase In Cash And Cash Equivalents To Idr 105.32 Billion.

title: Indo Tambangraya Plans To Distribute Interim Dividend Of Usd 50.04 Million
body: Indo Tambangraya Will Distribute An Interim Dividend Of Usd 50.04 Million, Representing 55% Of Its Net Profit Of Usd 90.97 Million As Of June 30, 2025. The Dividend Payout Is Scheduled For November 26, 2025, With A Record Date Of November 14, 2025.

title: Pantai Indah Kapuk Achieves Record-High Revenue And Profit In First Nine Months Of 2025
body: Pantai Indah Kapuk Reported A 48% Increase In Revenue To Idr 3.1 Trillion And A 62% Surge In Net Profit To Idr 791 Billion In The First Nine Months Of 2025, Driven By Strong Sales Of Commercial Land And Residential Projects. The Company's Total Assets Reached Idr 49.5 Trillion, With A Significant Increase In Property Investments.

title: PP Presisi TBK Advances Mining Construction Strategy Amid Strong Indonesian Resource Base
body: Pp Presisi Tbk, Aiming To Become A Leading National Mining Construction Firm, Highlighted Its Capacity To Execute Large‑Scale Projects With Its Heavy‑Equipment Fleet And Noted Indonesia’s Dominance In Nickel, Bauxite, And Tin As A Strategic Advantage. In The First Nine Months Of 2025, The Company Reported Net Revenue Of Idr 2.77 Trillion, Up From Idr 2.72 Trillion A Year Earlier, With Gross Profit Rising To Idr 577.96 Billion And Total Assets Reaching Idr 7.94 Trillion.

title: Indonesia Commits To Support Whoosh High‑Speed Rail Amid Debt Concerns
body: Indonesia’s Government, Led By Minister Roesan Roeslani, Reaffirmed Its Commitment To The Whoosh High‑Speed Rail Project Despite Its Debt Burden, With President Prabowo Subianto Pledging Full Responsibility For The Rp1.2 Trillion Annual Debt Cost. The Project Remains 75% Financed By A 2% Interest Loan From China Development Bank.

title: President Prabowo Urges PT Kai To Enhance Safety And Expand Commuter Services
body: President Prabowo Subianto Visited Jakarta’s Manggarai Station And Urged PT Kai To Maintain Safety, Cleanliness, And Security On Its Tracks, While Adding 30 New Krl Commuter Carriages And Expanding Services Across Greater Jakarta. PT Kai, Which Served 486 Million Passengers Last Year, Will Also Coordinate With Ministries To Develop New Railway Networks In Kalimantan, Sumatra, Sulawesi, And Java, Aiming To Reduce Road Traffic And Fuel Costs.

title: Kundy Wijaya Sell Shares of PT Kusuma Kemindo Sentosa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Kundy Wijaya sold shares of PT Kusuma Kemindo Sentosa Tbk (KKES.JK) at approximately IDR 30, decreasing holdings from 82,355,200 to 82,272,300. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Buana Graha Utama Buy Shares of Multi Indocitra Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Buana Graha Utama bought shares of Multi Indocitra Tbk (MICE.JK) at approximately IDR 627, increasing holdings from 286,182,418 to 286,283,018. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Lindataty Buy Shares of PT Ateliers Mecaniques D Indonesie Tbk.
body: According to the published announcement, Lindataty bought shares of PT Ateliers Mecaniques D Indonesie Tbk. (AMIN.JK) at approximately IDR 266, increasing holdings from 5,000,000 to 5,002,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Bank Mandiri Q3 Net Profit Rebounds, Management Optimistic On 2026 Loan Growth
body: Bank Mandiri’s Net Profit For The First Nine Months Of 2025 Was Idr37.7 Trillion, 10.2 % Lower Yoy But Within 74 % Of Full‑Year Estimates, Driven By 3.7 % Qoq Loan Growth, 27 % Qoq Non‑Interest Income Growth And A 1.3 % Qoq Decline In Operating Expenses. Management Projects 8‑10 % Loan Growth For 2025, Stable Nim Of 4.8‑5 %, Opex Normalisation And Credit Cost Easing In 2026, With Reserve Buffers At 2.78 % And Provisioning Normalising Toward The Upper End Of The 80‑100 Bp Range.

title: Indonesia’S Deputy Minister Urges Msmes To Adopt Technology, Launches Rp20 Trillion Credit Scheme
body: Deputy Minister Helvi Moraza Highlighted That Over Half Of Indonesia’s 30 Million Msmes Still Use Manual Or Semi‑Manual Equipment And Announced A Rp20 Trillion Labor‑Intensive Investment Credit Scheme To Modernise Production And Create Jobs. The Government Also Rolled Out The Help Me Grow Program And Signed A Memorandum With Bank Indonesia (Brin) To Strengthen Financing, Research And Innovation For Msmes.

title: PT Pelayaran Jaya Hidup Baru Tbk Ipo Surges With 24.85% First‑Day Gain And 267‑Times Oversubscription
body: PT Pelayaran Jaya Hidup Baru Tbk (Pjhb) Debuted On The Indonesia Stock Exchange On Thursday, With Its Shares Rising 24.85% To Rp412 Before Auto‑Rejection And Its Series‑I Warrants Leaping 40,900% From Rp1 To Rp410 Within Minutes. The Ipo, Which Issued 480 Million New Shares At Rp330 Each And 240 Million Warrants At A 2:1 Ratio, Was Oversubscribed 267.04×, Raising About Rp6.3 Trillion, And The Proceeds Will Fund The Construction Of Three New Lct Vessels Costing Rp163 Billion, With Revenue Projected To Reach Rp144 Billion In Five Years.

title: Mitrabara Adiperdana Reports 96.9% Decline In Nine‑Month Net Profit To $503.47K
body: For The First Nine Months Of 2025, Mitrabara Adiperdana Posted A Net Profit Of Usd 503.47 K, A 96.9% Drop From Usd 16.18 M A Year Earlier, With Basic Diluted Eps Falling To Usd 0.0004 From Usd 0.0132. Revenue Fell 29% To Usd 117.22 M, While Operating Income Rose To Usd 6.4 M, Equity Slipped To Usd 183.1 M And Total Liabilities Increased To Usd 54.08 M.

title: Prima Global Logistik Announces Rp3.08 Bn Interim Dividend For Fy2025
body: The Company Will Pay An Interim Dividend Of Rp3.08 Billion, Representing 48.5% Of Its Net Profit Of Rp6.35 Billion For The Nine Months Ended 30 September 2025, At Rp4 Per Share. The Dividend Will Be Recorded On 17 November 2025 With Payment Scheduled For 28 November 2025, And The Firm Reports Retained Earnings Of Rp36.05 Billion And Total Equity Of Rp146.22 Billion.

title: Msci Revises Indonesian Constituents, Adding Barito Renewables Energy And Bumi Resources Minerals While Removing Kalbe Farma And Expanding The Small Cap Index
body: Msci Announced That Barito Renewables Energy And Bumi Resources Minerals Join Its Global Standard Indexes, Indofood Cbp Benefits, Kalbe Farma Is Removed, And Bren Together With Zijin Gold International And Gf Securities Co H Now Ranks Among The Three Largest Stocks In The Msci Emerging Markets Index, Indicating A Higher Indonesia Weighting. The Provider Also Adds Seven Stocks—Dharma Satya Nusantara, Energi Mega Persada, Kalbe Farma, Mnc Studios International, Rukun Raharja, Solusi Sinergi Digital, And Timah—To Its Small Cap Index And Eliminates Brms, Selamat Sempurna And Ultrajaya Milk Industry, Effective After The Close Of Trading On 24 November 2025.

title: Indonesia Cuts Subsidized Fertilizer Prices By 20% In East Java, Enforces Compliance
body: Coordinating Minister Food Zulkifli Hasan Inspected Bondowoso Kiosks And Confirmed That, Under President Prabowo Subianto’s Directive, Subsidized Fertilizer Prices Have Been Reduced By 20%, Setting The Retail Price Ceiling For 50‑Kg Urea At Rp90,000 And For Npk At Rp92,000. The State‑Run Company Pupuk Indonesia Has Begun Enforcing The New Pricing Mechanism, Revoking Permits For 190 Retailers And Distributors That Violated The Price Cut.

title: Visi Media Asia Reports Rp1.15 Trillion Net Profit For Q3 2025, Reversing Previous Year Loss
body: For The Quarter Ended 30 September 2025, Visi Media Asia Posted A Net Profit Of Rp1.15 Trillion, A 5,850% Increase From A Rp21.52 Billion Loss A Year Earlier, And Its Basic Eps Rose To Rp70.205 From A Loss Of Rp1.308. Revenue Fell 17% To Rp713.85 Billion While Operating Expenses, Liabilities And The Deficit Decreased, Resulting In Positive Equity Of Rp381.71 Billion.

title: PT Cakra Buana Resources Energi Tbk Signs 8‑Year Time‑Charter For Hai Long 106 With PT Guna Nusa Utama Fabricators, Valued At Rp4.3 Trillion
body: On 4 November 2025, PT Cakra Buana Resources Energi Tbk Entered An Eight‑Year Time‑Charter Agreement With PT Guna Nusa Utama Fabricators For The Offshore Support Vessel Hai Long 106, With An Estimated Contract Value Of Rp4.3 Trillion. The Company Expects The Deal To Increase Vessel Utilization, Generate Significant Additional Revenue, And Improve Cash Flow And Profitability In Upcoming Periods.

title: Asuransi Ramayana Approves A Rp20.93 Billion Stock Dividend For Fy2024 With A 20:1 Split Ratio
body: The Agm On 4 Nov 2025 Authorized A Total Stock Dividend Of Rp20.93 Billion, Allocating Rp7.6 Billion To Paid‑Up Capital And Rp13.32 Billion To Share Premium, At A 20:1 Ratio (One New Share Per 20 Old Shares, Nominal Rp125). The Record Date Is 14 Nov 2025 (16:00 Wib) And The Distribution Will Occur On 5 Dec 2025, With Key Dividend Dates Spanning 12‑17 Nov 2025.

title: Multi Garam Utama Plans Private Placement Of 394.81 Million Shares To Raise Capital
body: The Company Intends To Issue 394,814,146 New Shares At A Nominal Value Of Rp20, Representing Up To 10% Of Its Paid‑In Capital, With Proceeds Earmarked For Business Expansion, Working Capital And General Corporate Purposes. Upon Completion, Assets Are Projected To Rise 8.86% And Equity 10.04%, While Non‑Participating Shareholders Could See Dilution Of Up To 9.09%; The Proposal Will Be Voted On At An Extraordinary Shareholders Meeting On 12 December 2025.

title: Keraton Investments Ltd Divests Its 12.81% Stake In PT Leyand International Tbk, Selling 508.26 Million Shares For Rp5.08 Billion
body: On 30 October 2025, Keraton Investments Ltd Sold Its Entire Holding Of 508,260,231 PT Leyand International Tbk Shares At Rp10 Each, Totaling Rp5.08 Billion And Reducing Its Ownership From 12.81% To Zero. Following The Sale, Lapd Shares Fell 9.8% And Triggered An Auto‑Rejection Floor At Rp184 Per Share.

title: PT Medela Potentia Tbk Posts 2.8% Revenue Rise And 16% Profit Jump In First Nine Months Of 2025
body: For The First Nine Months Of 2025, PT Medela Potentia Tbk Reported Revenue Of Rp11.1 Trillion (Up 2.8%) And Net Profit Of Rp294 Billion (Up 16.3%), Driven By Diversification, New Export Shipments From Its Subsidiary PT Deca Metric Medica To Timor Leste And Cambodia, And Strong Digital Platform Growth (Gpos B2B Sales +10%, Goapotik Revenue +31%). The Company Also Expanded Its Distribution Network Through PT Anugrah Argon Medica With Four New Principals And Highlighted Ongoing Esg Commitments.

title: PT Trisula International Tbk Reports 10% Sales Growth And 29% Profit Increase In Q3 2025
body: In Q3 2025, PT Trisula International Tbk Posted Sales Of Idr 1.18 Trillion, Up 10% Yoy, And Net Profit Of Idr 80.23 Billion, Up 29% Yoy, Driven By Strong Performance Across Manufacturing, Distribution And Uniform Segments. Export Sales Accounted For 61% Of Revenue, Reaching Idr 720.10 Billion (13% Yoy Growth), While Domestic Sales Rose 5% To Idr 468.77 Billion.

title: PT Emdeki Utama Tbk. Establishes New Subsidiary PT Calcindo Agro Utama With Rp5 Billion Capital
body: On 3 November 2025, PT Emdeki Utama Tbk. Announced The Creation Of PT Calcindo Agro Utama Together With Its Controlled Unit PT Emde Plast Utama, Issuing Rp5 Billion In Paid‑In Capital. The Ownership Structure Gives Mdki 99.98% (49,999 Shares) And PT Emde Plast Utama 0.02% (1 Share), Aiming To Expand The Use Of Limestone As Agricultural Lime And Strengthen The Agro‑Industry Supply Chain.

title: PT Chitose Internasional Tbk Posts 53% Profit Rise In Q3 2025 Driven By Education And Office-Equipment Sales
body: The Furniture Maker Reported Net Sales Of Rp324.29 Billion And A Net Profit Of Rp10.23 Billion For The Quarter Ended 30 September 2025, A 53.06% Increase Year‑On‑Year. Growth Was Led By Its Education Segment (Rp156.67 Billion, Up 13.31%) And Office‑Equipment Segment, Which Together Accounted For Most Of Sales, While The Company Highlighted Ongoing Product Innovation, Operational Efficiency And Factory Modernization.

title: Pefindo Downgrades PT Wijaya Karya'S Sustainable Sukuk Rating After Missed Rp109.3 Bn Principal Payment
body: Indonesia's Official Securities Rating Agency Pefindo Lowered The Rating Of PT Wijaya Karya's Sustainable Sukuk Mudharabah Iii Stage I/2022 Series From Idccc To Idd After The Company Failed To Meet The Rp109.3 Billion Principal Due On 3 Nov 2025. The Agency Kept Wika's Corporate Rating At Idsd And Other Instrument Ratings Unchanged, Citing Weak Liquidity And Financial Profile.

title: Bank Rakyat Indonesia Reports Q3 Profit Decline, Q2 Gains, And 2026 Credit Cost Improvement Outlook
body: Bank Rakyat Indonesia’s Q3 Net Profit Fell 5.6% Yoy To Inr15.4 Trillion, But Rose 15.1% From Q2, And First‑Nine‑Month Earnings Were Down 9.5% To Inr40.8 Trillion, Still Meeting Full‑Year Expectations Of Uob Kay Hian. Loans Grew 6.3% In Q3 And Are Expected To Accelerate In Q4, Helping The Bank Hit Its 7‑9% Annual Growth Target, While Management Forecasts A Slight Improvement In Credit Costs For 2026 With A 2026 Coc Guidance Of 2.9‑3.2% And Notes That Asset‑Quality Deterioration Has Peaked, As Stated By Pakpahan And Reported By Uobkh Analyst Posmarito.

title: Indonesia Targets Downstreaming Of Nickel‑Based Ev Batteries Within Two Years, Highlights Gaikindo Makassar Show
body: Setia Diarta, Director General Of The Ministry Of Industry, Announced That Indonesia Plans To Begin Downstreaming Nickel‑Based Electric‑Vehicle Batteries Within The Next Two Years, With Construction Underway In Karawang And Other Consortiums Involving State‑Owned Enterprises. At The Gaikindo Indonesia International Auto Show In Makassar, The Exhibition Showcased Low‑Emission Vehicles And Highlighted A 4.1% National Sales Target, While Chairman Putu Juli Ardika Noted The Region’s Surplus Electricity Could Support Home Charging And Boost Sales In Eastern Indonesia.

title: Indonesia Seeks To Restore 0% Us Palm Oil Tariff Through Trade‑Negotiation Leverage
body: Indonesia’s Palm Oil Industry, Led By Chairman Eddy Martono, Aims To Regain The Historic 0% Tariff On U.S. Imports By Offering Trade‑Counterparts Such As Soybean Purchases Or Boeing Aircraft, While Economists Caution That Achieving This Requires Careful Trade‑Off Calculations. Negotiations Are Slated To Begin In November 2025, With Indonesia Targeting A Tariff Structure Similar To Malaysia’s For Palm Oil, Cocoa, And Rubber.

title: Dragonmine Mining Limited Plans To Acquire 80% Of Berkah Prima Perkasa, Pending Due Diligence And Tender Offer
body: Dragonmine Mining Limited Is Negotiating To Purchase 334.4 Million Shares, Representing 80% Of Berkah Prima Perkasa, From Shareholders Including Herman Tansri, Siek Agung Guntoro, Fajar Tasrif, Rudy Tasrif And PT Cetak Biru Kapital. The Transaction Will Be Subject To Due‑Diligence, A Conditional Sale Agreement And Compliance With Pojk No. 9/2018, After Which A Mandatory Tender Offer Is Required; Berkah Prima Perkasa’s Share Price Has Risen About 162% To Rp2,160 In The Past Month.

title: Lso Becomes Controlling Shareholder Of Smkm After Acquiring 25% Stake From PT Vina Nauli Jordania
body: Lso, Via Lim Shrimp Org Pte. Ltd., Purchased 313,250,000 Smkm Shares (25% Of The Paid‑Up Capital) From PT Vina Nauli Jordania, Completing The First Tranche Of A Conditional Share Purchase Agreement That Allows Lso To Acquire Up To 450 Million Shares (35.91% Of Capital). The Pending Second Tranche Of 136,750,000 Shares (10.91%) Must Be Completed By 30 June 2026, After Which Lso Will Conduct A Mandatory Tender Offer, Restructure Smkm’s Management And Launch A Rights Issue To Transform The Firm Into A Regional Aquaculture Group With Expanded Shrimp And Sea‑Cucumber Production.

title: Indian Commissioner Raises Stake In PT Ancara Logistics Indonesia Tbk To 2.21%
body: The Commissioner Bought 500,000 Shares At Rp540 On 7 Oct 2025 And 9,448,800 Shares At Rp555 On 4 Nov 2025, Increasing His Holding To 350,521,500 Shares (2.21%). After The Purchases, PT Ancara Logistics Indonesia Tbk’s Stock Rose 0.90% To Rp560, Up 3.70% Over The Month, 18.1% Over Six Months, And 46.6% Year‑To‑Date.

title: Indonesia Boosts Rice Production, Driving Global Prices Down To $371/Ton
body: Indonesia’s Increased Domestic Rice Output Has Helped Lower Global Rice Prices From About $650/Ton To $371/Ton, According To Head National Food Agency Andi Amran Sulaiman. The Government’s Policies, Including Halting Rice Imports In 2025, Have Built A 3.8‑Million‑Ton Reserve And Are Expected To Support A 34.77‑Million‑Ton Production Target For 2025, Creating A Surplus Of 3.8 Million Tons.

title: Dr. Pulung Peranginangin Buy Shares of Gema Grahasarana Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Dr. Pulung Peranginangin buy shares of Gema Grahasarana Tbk (GEMA.JK) at approximately IDR 87, increasing holdings from 7,550,000 to 7,660,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Handi Sutanto Sell Shares of Aneka Tambang Tbk.
body: According to the published announcement, Handi Sutanto sold shares of Aneka Tambang Tbk. (ANTM.JK) at approximately IDR 16. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Yulisar Khiat Buy Shares of PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Yulisar Khiat bought shares of PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk (HEAL.JK), increasing holdings from 952,724,966 to 954,969,966. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Sanjeva Advani Sell Shares of PT MD Entertainment Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Sanjeva Advani sold shares of PT MD Entertainment Tbk (FILM.JK) at approximately IDR 5,300, decreasing holdings from 4,750,079 to 3,100,079. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Rondy Yunanda Buy Shares of PT Ecocare Indo Pasifik Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Rondy Yunanda bought shares of PT Ecocare Indo Pasifik Tbk (HYGN.JK) at approximately IDR 169, increasing holdings from 201,000,000 to 201,800,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Bank Of Singapore LIMITED Buy Shares of Pioneerindo Gourmet International Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Bank Of Singapore LIMITED bought shares of Pioneerindo Gourmet International Tbk (PTSP.JK), increasing holdings from 17,634,220 to 17,637,920. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Harvest Capital International Buy Shares of PT Arkadia Digital Media Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Harvest Capital International bought shares of PT Arkadia Digital Media Tbk (DIGI.JK) at approximately IDR 35, increasing holdings from 500,084,000 to 527,364,800. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Harvest Capital International Buy Shares of PT Arkadia Digital Media Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Harvest Capital International bought shares of PT Arkadia Digital Media Tbk (DIGI.JK) at approximately IDR 35, increasing holdings from 500,084,000 to 527,364,800. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Febyan Buy Shares of PT Indonesia Pondasi Raya Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Febyan bought shares of PT Indonesia Pondasi Raya Tbk (IDPR.JK) at approximately IDR 302, increasing holdings from 54,110,800 to 55,199,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Irsyad Sahroni Buy Shares of PT Indosat Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Irsyad Sahroni bought shares of PT Indosat Tbk (ISAT.JK) at approximately IDR 1,911. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Diah Asriningpuri Sugianto Buy Shares of PT Prima Andalan Mandiri Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Diah Asriningpuri Sugianto bought shares of PT Prima Andalan Mandiri Tbk (MCOL.JK) at approximately IDR 5, increasing holdings from 2,894,800 to 3,983,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara Sell Shares of PT Dharma Polimetal Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara sold shares of PT Dharma Polimetal Tbk (DRMA.JK), decreasing holdings from 72,350,000 to 71,397,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk. To Secure Usd 600 Million Syndicated Loan To Fund Palu Underground Gold Mine And Expansion Projects
body: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk. Plans To Raise A Usd 600 Million Syndicated Loan, With About Half Allocated To Its Underground Gold Mining Project In Palu, Central Sulawesi, And The Rest To Exploration And Plant Construction, While The Company Reported A Third‑Quarter 2025 Operating Profit Of Usd 69.71 Million And Net Profit Up 129% Yoy, Driven By A 25% Increase In Gold Production And A 34% Rise In Gold Prices.

title: PT Pp Presisi Tbk Reports Strong Q3 2025 Net Profit Of Idr 194.69 Billion
body: During The First Nine Months Of 2025, PT Pp Presisi Tbk Posted A Net Income Of Idr 2.77 Trillion, Up From Idr 2.72 Trillion A Year Earlier, While Gross Profit Rose To Idr 577.96 Billion From Idr 507.11 Billion. Consolidated Assets Reached Idr 7.94 Trillion And Equity Increased To Idr 3.67 Trillion, Reflecting The Company's Ability To Maintain Liquidity And Solvency Amid Industry Challenges.

title: Indonesia Opens Letung And Sedanau Ferry Ports To Boost Riau Islands Connectivity
body: Minister Of Transportation Dudy Purwagandhi Inaugurated The Letung And Sedanau Ferry Ports In Riau Islands, Aiming To Improve Inter-Island Mobility And Reduce Logistics Costs, With The New 1,500 Gt Vessel Kmp Bahtera Nusantara 01 Expected To Serve Routes Such As Tanjung Uban–Letung–Matak–Midai–Sedanau–Penagi–Subi–Serasan–Sintete. The Launch Also Includes A New Class Ii Riau Islands Land Transportation Management Office To Enhance Public Service Capacity And Support Equitable Development In Frontier Areas.

title: Prabowo Orders Accelerated Downstreaming Projects Across 18 Sectors
body: President Prabowo Subianto Instructed Ministers To Fast‑Track 18 Downstreaming Projects That Have Completed Pre‑Feasibility, Aiming To Start On‑Site Work In 2026. The Projects Span Minerals, Energy, Agriculture, And Fisheries, While The Government Also Pushes The Red‑White Fishermen Village Program To Develop 65 Villages By End‑2025, And The Meeting Involved Danantara Ceo Rosan Roeslani.

title: Adhi Properti Reports Nine‑Month 2025 Loss Of Rp4.85 Bn As Revenue Drops 41%
body: For The First Nine Months Of 2025, Adhi Properti Posted A Net Loss Of Rp4.85 Billion, With Loss Per Share Narrowing To Rp0.22 From Rp1.36 A Year Earlier. Revenue Fell 40.92% To Rp165.54 Billion, Gross Profit Declined To Rp41.03 Billion, And Operating Profit Dropped To Rp7.6 Billion While Total Assets Rose To Rp6.96 Trillion.

title: Indonesia Accelerates 18 Downstream Projects Worth Rp600 Trillion Under President Prabowo'S Directive
body: President Prabowo Subianto Has Ordered The Rapid Implementation Of 18 Downstream Projects, Totaling Nearly Rp600 Trillion, With The Aim To Complete Preliminary Processes This Year And Begin On‑Field Work In 2026. Energy Minister Bahlil Lahadalia Highlighted That The Projects, Supported By Indonesia’s Investment Agency Danantara, Will Boost Economic Growth, Create Jobs, And Reduce Dependence On Imported Goods, Including A New Lpg‑To‑Dme Facility In East Kalimantan And A Diesel Refinery In Balikpapan.

title: Indonesia And Russia Explore Partnership To Develop Modern Maritime Technologies
body: During A Bilateral Consultation, Coordinating Minister Infrastructure Regional Development Agus Harimurti Yudhoyono Announced That Indonesia Will Work With Russia To Develop Hovercraft, Hydrofoil Vessels, Energy‑Efficient Fishing Boats And Other High‑Speed, Environmentally Friendly Ships Under A New Memorandum Of Understanding Covering Six Key Maritime Areas. Chairman Maritime Council Russian Federation Nikolai Patrushev Said Russia Will Share Its Shipbuilding Expertise To Help Indonesia Strengthen Its Maritime Industry And Become A Global Hub.

title: Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto Inaugurates Southeast Asia’S Largest Petrochemical Plant In Cilegon
body: President Prabowo Subianto Pressed The Siren Button To Launch The $4 Billion Lotte Chemical Indonesia (Lci) Petrochemical Complex In Cilegon, Banten, Which Will Produce Up To 1,000 Kt Of Ethylene And Other Products Annually. Investment Agency Danantara Has Expressed Interest In Acquiring A 35 % Stake In Lci, Preferring A Direct Investment Approach Rather Than Through A State‑Owned Enterprise.

title: Commissioner Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara Reduces Stake In PT Dharma Polimetal Tbk. After Multiple Share Sales
body: Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara Sold 952,400 PT Dharma Polimetal Tbk. Shares At Rp1,050 Each, Bringing His Holding To 71.3 Million Shares (1.517%). This Follows Earlier Sales Of 100,000 Shares At Rp1,115 On 27 Oct 2025 And 50,000 Shares At Rp995 On 13 Oct 2025.

title: PT Atlantis Subsea Indonesia Tbk. Commissioner Rudi Reksa Sutantra Sells And Buys Shares, Lowering His Stake To 49.19%
body: Rudi Reksa Sutantra Sold 30.5 Million Shares Of PT Atlantis Subsea Indonesia Tbk. At Rp67‑67.5 Per Share On Oct 30‑31 And Repurchased 5.49 Million Shares At Rp67 On Nov 3, Reducing His Holding To 3.04 Billion Shares (49.19%) From 3.05 Billion (49.23%). Earlier In October He Sold 20 Million Shares At Rp77‑80, And Atla Closed Up 3.17% At Rp65, About Rp2 Below The Recent Transaction Price.

title: Jakarta Provincial Government Boosts Creative Economy With Coffee Initiatives At Jicc 2025
body: Deputy Governor Rano Karno Announced Plans To Strengthen The Coffee Sector—Supporting Young Talent, Expanding Sme Market Access, And Developing Coffee Tourism Corridors—During The Opening Of The Jakarta International Coffee Conference 2025, Which Runs From November 6 To 8 And Includes Speakers, Capacity‑Building Sessions, Competitions And Networking. The Announcement Follows Indonesia’s Export Of 206,700 Tons Of Coffee In The First Half Of 2025.

title: Intermedia Capital Posts A Rp10.81 Billion Loss For The Quarter Ended 30 September 2025
body: The Company Recorded A Net Loss Of Rp10.94 Billion, Translating To A Loss Per Share Of Rp0.28, After A Profit Of Rp100.44 Billion In The Comparable Period Last Year, While Net Revenue Fell 3.75% To Rp465.3 Billion. Operating Expenses Declined Across The Board, Resulting In A Lower Equity Of Rp980.02 Billion And Total Assets Of Rp4.33 Trillion.

title: Persada To Receive New Controlling Shareholder After Inet Acquires 53.57% Of Koperasi Pegawai Indonesia
body: Inet Has Taken A 53.57% Stake In Koperasi Pegawai Indonesia, Acquiring 1.68 Billion Shares Of Kopindosat, And Will Become The Controlling Shareholder Of Persada. The Transaction Is Currently At The Indicative Termsheet Stage With Due Diligence Underway, And The New Owner Plans To Pursue Synergies In Telecom And Digital Outsourcing While Keeping Persada Listed On The Indonesian Stock Exchange.

title: PT Gunung Raja Paksi Tbk. Reports Q3 2025 Net Loss Of $34.87 Million As Sales Halve
body: The Company Posted A Net Loss Of Usd34.87 Million For The Quarter Ended 30 September 2025, Reversing A Usd131.12 Million Profit A Year Earlier, As Net Sales Fell 50.6% To Usd132.44 Million And It Recorded A Gross Loss Of Usd14.32 Million. Total Assets Declined 8.8% To Usd722.63 Million And Liabilities Fell 34.5% To Usd66.98 Million, While The Share Price Slipped About 1% To Rp202.

title: PT Merdeka Gold Resources Tbk Announces Resignation Of President Commissioner Hardi Wijaya Liong
body: The Company Received Hardi Wijaya Liong’s Resignation Letter On 3 November 2025, Which Will Take Effect After Shareholder Approval, And Said The Change Will Not Affect Its Operations, Legal Matters, Or Financial Condition. Hardi Had Been Appointed President Commissioner In June 2025 After A Career That Included A Managerial Role At Prasetio Utomo & Rekan Arthur Andersen Indonesia.

title: Dragonmine Mining Limited Seeks To Acquire 80% Of PT Berkah Prima Perkasa Tbk (Blue) As Shares Rally Sharply
body: Dragonmine Mining Limited Is Negotiating To Purchase 344.4 Million Shares, Equal To 80 % Of The Issued And Paid‑Up Capital Of PT Berkah Prima Perkasa Tbk (Blue) From Controlling Shareholders Including Herman Tansri, Siek Agung Guntoro, Fajar Tasrif, Rudi Tasrif And PT Cetak Biru Kapital. The News Drove Blue’s Stock To An Auto‑Rejection Level Of Rp2,370 Per Share, Up About 187 % In The Past Month And More Than 600 % Over The Last Six Months.

title: PT Pelayaran Jaya Hidup Baru Tbk Lists On Indonesia Stock Exchange With Ambitious Growth Plans
body: PT Pelayaran Jaya Hidup Baru Tbk (Pjhb) Has Successfully Listed On The Indonesia Stock Exchange With A Target To Increase Revenue By Nearly Three Times To Idr 144 Billion In The Next Five Years, While Also Planning To Expand Its Fleet With Three New Lct Vessels.

title: Indonesia Launches Nationwide Red White Village Cooperative Program With Rp2.5 Billion Construction Budget Per Site
body: Indonesia’s Ministry Of Cooperatives Announced That Each Red White Village Cooperative Will Cost Approximately Rp2.5 Billion, Covering Construction, Equipment, And Operational Vehicles, With State‑Owned Banks Providing Loans Up To Rp3 Billion. The Program Targets 80,000 Cooperatives Across 7,923 Sites, Aims To Complete Physical Infrastructure By March 2026, And Is Being Executed By PT Agrinas Pangan Nusantara, Which Has Already Received A First Disbursement Of Nearly Rp600 Billion.

title: Pertamina Geothermal Energy Expands Kamojang Operations To Boost Clean Power, Local Livelihoods And Carbon‑Reduction Goals
body: Pge’s Kamojang Geothermal Complex Now Operates Five Units Totaling 235 Mw, Generating 1.326 Gwh By September 2025 And Cutting Co₂ Emissions By 1.22 Million Tonnes Annually While Supplying Electricity To Over 260,000 Households. The Company Also Launched Community Initiatives—Including A Digital Rangers App, A Geothermal‑Dry Coffee House, Eagle Conservation And The Gemah Karsa Program—Supporting Income Growth And Sustainable Agriculture As It Targets 1 Gw Capacity Within 2–3 Years And 3 Gw Across Ten Sites By 2033.

title: Indonesia Accelerates Downstreaming Of Natural Resources With 18 Priority Projects And 618 Trillion Idr Investment
body: Indonesia’s Ministry Of Energy And Mineral Resources, Led By Bahlil Lahadalia, Has Launched A Task Force To Accelerate Downstreaming, Targeting 18 Priority Projects Worth More Than Idr 618 Trillion Managed By Bpi Danantara, With 67 % Of Sites Outside Java. The Program Aims To Create Over 276,000 New Jobs And Boost National Economic Sovereignty By Processing Domestic Resources Into High‑Value Products.

title: Komisaris PT Prima Andalan Mandiri Tbk, Diah Asriningpuri Sugianto, Increases Her Share Ownership In The Company.
body: Diah Asriningpuri Sugianto, A Commissioner Of PT Prima Andalan Mandiri Tbk, Has Purchased 1,088,200 Shares Of The Company At A Price Of Rp3,800 Per Share, Increasing Her Total Share Ownership From 2,894,800 To 3,983,000 Shares. The Company's Stock Price Has Experienced A 0.52% Decrease To Rp3,820 Per Share On The Day Of The Transaction, With A 3.52% Increase Over The Past Month And A 24.3% Decrease Over The Past Year.

title: Sanjeva Reduces Its Stake In Md Entertainment By Selling 1.65 Million Shares
body: On October 30, 2025, Sanjeva Sold 1.65 Million Md Entertainment Shares At An Execution Price Of Rp5,300 Per Share, Generating Rp8.74 Billion, Which Is About 2.3% Below The Closing Price Of Rp5,425. The Transaction Lowered Sanjeva’s Holding To 3.1 Million Shares (0.029% Of Md Entertainment) From 4.75 Million Shares (0.047%).

title: PT Rmk Energy Tbk Signs Memorandum To Build Hauling Road In South Sumatra To Boost Coal Logistics
body: PT Rmk Energy Tbk, Through Its Subsidiary PT Royaltama Mulia Kencana, Signed A Memorandum Of Understanding With PT Medco E & P To Utilize 500 M² Of Land In Bangun Sari Village For A Hauling Road That Will Connect The Company’s Gunung Megang Loading Station To Prospective Mines In Muara Enim–Tanjung Enim. The Infrastructure, Led By President Director Vincent Saputra, Is Expected To Accelerate Coal Haulage Toward The Company’s Long‑Term Target Of 20 Million Tons And Support Indonesia’s National Energy Security.

title: Smga Partners With PT Hengsheng New Energy Material Indonesia To Build Oxygen‑Enriched Nickel Smelter
body: Smga, A Subsidiary Of PT Sumber Global Energy Tbk. And PT Sumber Mineral Global Abadi Tbk., Has Entered The Nickel Smelting Business And Signed A Strategic Partnership With PT Hengsheng New Energy Material Indonesia To Acquire Shares And Jointly Develop A Converter Smelter Using Oxygen‑Enriched Side‑Blowing Furnace Technology. The Collaboration Aims To Upgrade Low‑Grade Nickel Matte To High‑Grade, Strengthen Indonesia’s Downstream Nickel Value Chain, And Meet Rising Global Demand For Nickel In Electric‑Vehicle Batteries And Energy Storage, Projected To Grow From 545,000 T In 2024 To Over 1.5 Million T By 2030.

title: Indonesia Orders E‑Commerce Platforms To Shut Down Online Stores Selling Imported Secondhand Clothes
body: Indonesia’s Micro, Small And Medium Enterprises Minister Maman Abdurrahman Has Ordered E‑Commerce Operators To Stop Selling Imported Secondhand Clothing, Citing A 17.33% Rise In Imports Of Used Textiles Worth $78.19 Million From January To July 2025, And Will Meet Operators To Ensure Compliance While Encouraging Promotion Of Locally Made Products. The Government Also Plans To Help Thrift Sellers Transition To Domestic Apparel To Protect Jobs.

title: President Prabowo Pledges Secure, Business-Friendly Environment As Lotte Chemical Indonesia Opens $4 Billion Petrochemical Plant In Cilegon
body: At The Inauguration Of Lotte Chemical Indonesia’s New Ethylene And Petrochemical Complex In Cilegon, Banten, President Prabowo Subianto Reiterated Indonesia’s Commitment To A Safe, Open Climate For Both Domestic And Foreign Investors, Citing The $4 Billion Project As Evidence Of Global Confidence. The Plant, Spanning 107.8 Ha, Will Produce 1,000 Kt Of Ethylene Annually And Create Tens Of Thousands Of Jobs, With The President Emphasizing Legal Certainty And Strong Law Enforcement To Sustain Investor Trust.

title: Indoritel Secures Rp450 Bn Term Loan From Bank Mandiri While Indomaret Reports Higher Q3 Profit
body: Indoritel Obtained A Rp450 Billion Term Loan From Bank Mandiri, Split Into A Rp225 Billion Five‑Year Tranche And A Second Tranche With A Seven‑Year Term, Which It Says Will Support Operations Without Materially Affecting Its Financial Condition. For The Quarter Ended 30 September 2025, Indomaret’s Parent Reported Net Profit Of Rp838.66 Billion (Up 12.56% Yoy), Revenue Of Rp1.22 Trillion, Operating Profit Of Rp990.65 Billion, Total Equity Of Rp15.02 Trillion, Liabilities Of Rp7.36 Trillion, And Assets Of Rp22.38 Trillion.

title: Rukun Raharja Added To Msci International Small‑Cap Indexes, Effective 25 Nov 2025
body: Rukun Raharja (Raja) Was Announced By Msci On 6 Nov 2025 To Become A Constituent Of The Msci Capital International Small‑Cap Indexes Starting 25 Nov 2025, Following Its Subsidiary Raharja Energi Cepu’s Earlier Inclusion In August 2025 And Its August Inclusion In Indonesia’s Idx80. The Company Said The Dual Index Memberships Will Broaden Its Exposure To Global Institutional Investors As It Pursues Multiple Growth Projects, Including A Gas‑Compressor Plant In South Sulawesi, A Fuel‑Pipeline In East Kalimantan, And Strategic Acquisitions In Lng Shipping And Clean‑Energy Assets.

title: PT Bumi Citra Investindo Reduces Its Stake In PT Bumi Citra Permai Tbk Through Recent Share Sales
body: PT Bumi Citra Investindo Sold 12.255 Million Shares Of PT Bumi Citra Permai Tbk At Rp103‑Rp109 Per Share Between 22‑30 Oct 2025, Following A Prior Sale Of 12.7 Million Shares At Rp114‑Rp123 Per Share In Late September 2025, Lowering Its Holding To 532.1 Million Shares (37.22%). After The Transactions, Bcip’s Share Price Fell 1.77% To Rp111, Down 7.5% For The Month But Up 122% Year‑To‑Date.

title: PT Solusi Sinergi Digital Tbk. (Surge) And Its Subsidiary PT Telemedia Komunikasi Pratama Secure A 1.4 Ghz Band As Wifi Joins The Msci Indonesia Small Cap Index
body: PT Telemedia Komunikasi Pratama Was Awarded An 80 Mhz Block Of The 1.4 Ghz Frequency For Java, Papua And Maluku For Ten Years, Supporting Surge’s Plan To Reach 5 Million Homepass By 2026, While Wifi Met The Msci Indonesia Small Cap Index Eligibility Thresholds Of A Usd 375 Million Free‑Float Market Cap, Usd 1.5 Million Average Daily Traded Value And A 15 % Annualized Traded‑Value Ratio. Analysts Expect Foreign Fund Inflows Of Roughly Rp100‑500 Billion And Note That Surge Will Leverage Its 6,927 Km Fiber Backbone, Partnerships With Ntt E-Asia Pte. Ltd., Nokia, Qualcomm, Orex Sai, Huawei, Tower Bersama Group And Centrutama, And The Launch Of Indonesia’s First Starlite Wi‑Fi 7 Network To Expand Broadband Coverage.

title: Jodi Andrea Suryokusumo Buy Shares of Mustika Ratu Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Jodi Andrea Suryokusumo bought shares of Mustika Ratu Tbk (MRAT.JK) at approximately IDR 392, increasing holdings from 73,100 to 74,700. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Trukindo Persadasejahtera Sell Transaction of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Trukindo Persadasejahtera sell shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK), decreasing holdings from 58.108.200 to 56.308.200. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Fersindo Nusa Jaya Buy Transaction of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Fersindo Nusa Jaya buy shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK), increasing holdings from 99.953.700 to 103.009.600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Bakrie Metalindustries, PT Buy Transaction of PT VKTR Teknologi Mobilitas Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Bakrie Metalindustries, PT buy shares of PT VKTR Teknologi Mobilitas Tbk (VKTR.JK), increasing holdings from 6.814.828.900 to 6.820.777.900. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Hengtong Optic Electricinternational Co., LIMITED Sell Transaction of Voksel Electric Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hengtong Optic Electricinternational Co., LIMITED sell shares of Voksel Electric Tbk (VOKS.JK), decreasing holdings from 2.275.161.620 to 2.275.072.020. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Budiarto Halim Buy Shares of Erajaya Swasembada Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Budiarto Halim bought shares of Erajaya Swasembada Tbk (ERAA.JK), increasing holdings from 6,250,000 to 8,493,306. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Jong Woon Kim Buy Shares of Erajaya Swasembada Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Jong Woon Kim bought shares of Erajaya Swasembada Tbk (ERAA.JK). This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Danis Subyantoro Buy Shares of PT Bank Mandiri (Persero) Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Danis Subyantoro bought shares of PT Bank Mandiri (Persero) Tbk (BMRI.JK) at approximately IDR 4,260, increasing holdings from 344,800 to 348,584. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia Targets Aquaculture Expansion to Strengthen Global Food Security
body: Marine Affairs and Fisheries Minister Sakti Wahyu Trenggono highlighted that Indonesia’s blue food production reaches at least 24 million tonnes annually, with aquaculture output around 5.6 million tonnes and only 1.2 million hectares of the 18 million hectares suitable for farming currently utilized. The ministry is launching a 20,000‑hectare saline tilapia program in West Java, projected to yield 1.56 million tonnes per year, as part of efforts to meet rising global protein demand.

title: Indonesia and US to Resume Reciprocal Tariff Negotiations Next Week
body: Indonesia’s trade minister confirmed that reciprocal tariff talks with the United States will resume next week, though the exact date has not yet been set. The U.S. has lowered its reciprocal tariff on Indonesian goods to 19% from an initial 32%, and both sides are drafting legal agreements while exempting commodities such as palm, cocoa, and chocolate from tariffs.

title: BP Akr Places Second 100,000‑Barrel Order from Pertamina Patra Niaga Amid Ongoing Fuel Shortages
body: BP Akr has ordered an additional 100,000 barrels of base fuel from Pertamina Patra Niaga, following a first order of the same size at the end of October, as the ministry of energy seeks to restore stocks at private gas stations that have faced shortages since mid‑August. The government may reinstate a 10 % additional import quota scheme, while other operators such as PT Vivo Energy Indonesia and Shell are also negotiating supply agreements; Vivo’s earlier 40,000‑barrel deal was cancelled after a 3.5 % ethanol content was detected.

title: Indonesia's Housing Finance Programs KPP and FLPP Show Strong Commitment Under President Prabowo
body: Minister of Housing Settlements Maruarar Sirait highlighted that the KPP working‑capital program and the FLPP subsidized mortgage scheme are concrete examples of President Prabowo’s commitment to the housing sector, with FLPP distribution reaching 213,630 units worth Rp26.51 trillion (US$1.5 billion) and KPP loans totaling Rp267.1 billion (US$16 million) as of early November 2025.

title: Indonesia to invest Rp371 trillion in agricultural downstreaming to boost food self‑reliance
body: Indonesia’s agriculture ministry and sovereign wealth fund Danantara have pledged about Rp371 trillion (≈US$22 billion) to accelerate downstream processing of key commodities such as coconut, cocoa, cashew and palm oil, aiming to triple export value and create three million jobs over the next four years.

title: Indonesia’s Ministry Plans Rebranding of Thrift Markets to Promote Local Apparel
body: Indonesia’s Ministry of Micro, Small and Medium Enterprises announced a rebranding of major thrift markets such as Senen and Gedebage, partnering with over 150 local brands and offering business credit support to help traders shift from imported used clothing to locally produced apparel, in line with President Prabowo’s directive to curb illegal imports. The ministry also noted that used clothing prices are not always lower than new local products, emphasizing the need for regulated pricing.

title: Adaro Andalan Indonesia to Pay Interim Dividend of USD 250 Million
body: Adaro Andalan Indonesia will distribute an interim dividend of USD 250 million, representing about 42.5% of its net profit of USD 587.32 million as of September 30, 2025. The dividend will be paid on November 27, 2025, with key dates including ex‑dividend on November 18 and recording date on November 19.

title: PT Ashmore Asset Management Indonesia Tbk Announces Final Cash Dividend of Rp18.5 per Share for FY 2025, to be Paid on 24 November 2025
body: PT Ashmore Asset Management Indonesia Tbk will distribute a final cash dividend of Rp18.5 per share (total 32.5 per share including the Rp14 interim dividend already paid on 24 February 2025) for the year ending 30 June 2025, with payment scheduled for 24 November 2025. The dividend decision was approved at the virtual annual general meeting on 5 November 2025, and the company also reaffirmed its auditor and other strategic agenda items.

title: PT J Resources Asia Pasifik Tbk Secures Shareholder Approval to Sell All Shares of Subsidiary PT Arafura Surya Alam to PT Andusa Tambang Nusantara
body: PT J Resources Asia Pasifik Tbk (PSAB) obtained shareholder approval to sell its 2,331,139 shares of PT J Resources Nusantara to PT Andusa Tambang Nusantara, a unit of PT United Tractors Tbk, pending regulatory approval from the Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources. The transaction, part of a portfolio restructuring, is expected to be completed by December 23, 2025, with an enterprise value of US$540 million for the related ASA assets.

title: PT Jaya Sukses Makmur Sentosa Tbk CEO Resigns, Shares Slip 0.31%
body: Belinda Natalia has resigned as CEO of PT Jaya Sukses Makmur Sentosa Tbk, with the resignation accepted on November 5, 2025, and the company will convene an AGM within 90 days while operations remain unaffected. The shares of Rise fell 0.31% to Rp8,025 on Friday trading.

title: PT cita mineral investindo Tbk. secures USD 100 million 12‑month loan facility for short‑term working capital
body: On 5 November 2025, PT cita mineral investindo Tbk. signed a USD 100 million (approximately IDR 1.55 trillion) syndicated loan with a 12‑month tenor, interest rate SOFR + 0.5%, and lenders including dbs bank ltd., oversea-chinese banking corporation limited, PT bank ocbc nisp Tbk, and PT bank dbs indonesia. The funds will support short‑term working capital and operations, secured by fiduciary certificates on receivables, insurance claims, movable assets, inventory, and a pledge of PT cita shares held by PT harita jayaraya valued at no less than USD 200 million.

title: PT medikaloka hermina Tbk. CEO Yulisar Khiat increases heal ownership to 6.21% after recent purchases
body: Yulisar Khiat, director of PT medikaloka hermina Tbk., bought an additional 2,245 million heal shares at Rp1,442 each on 6 November 2025, bringing his total holding to 954.9 million shares (6.21%). This follows an earlier purchase of about 40.9 million shares at an average price of Rp1,482, valued at roughly Rp60.6 billion, while heal’s share price fell 1.69% to Rp1,455.

title: PT Hafar Daya Konstruksi, a Petrosea subsidiary, wins a US$9.5 million EPC contract for the Hidayah field development with Petronas Carigali North Madura II Ltd.
body: The contract, valued at US$9.5 million (approximately Rp156 billion), covers engineering, procurement, construction, installation and commissioning of an integrated wellhead‑central processing platform, subsea pipeline and terminal for the Hidayah field in North Madura II, and will be signed on 9 November 2024. The deal supports Petrosea's non‑organic growth strategy and the company clarified it has no affiliation with PT Cakra Buana Resources Energi Tbk nor any vessel rental arrangement with CBRE.

title: Bank Tabungan Negara signs strategic partnership with Provinsi Kepulauan Riau to digitize regional finance and expand housing loans for civil servants
body: Bank Tabungan Negara (BTN) will provide fund management, digital payroll, revenue collection and competitive housing loan programs for ASN, health workers and BUMD employees in Provinsi Kepulauan Riau, aiming to create a transparent, inclusive and sustainable regional financial ecosystem. The governor highlighted that the province's economy grew 7.48% year‑on‑year through Q3‑2025, the highest in Sumatra and third nationally, underscoring the potential impact of the collaboration.

title: PT Bank Pembangunan Daerah Jawa Timur Tbk acquires 0.053% stake in PT Bank Pembangunan Daerah Banten Tbk for Rp742.8 million
body: The bank purchased 27.51 million shares of PT Bank Pembangunan Daerah Banten Tbk at Rp27 per share, amounting to a total investment of Rp742.82 million and representing about 0.053% of the target’s total share capital, following shareholder approval of a Rp10 billion capital injection. The transaction, executed on the regular market on 5 November 2025, coincided with a 7.41% rise in PT Bank Pembangunan Daerah Banten Tbk’s share price to Rp29.

title: PT Indosat Tbk Director Irsyad Sahroni Acquires Over One Million Shares Worth About Rp2 Billion
body: Irsyad Sahroni purchased 1,047,500 PT Indosat Tbk shares between 31 Oct and 3 Nov 2025 at prices ranging from Rp1,855 to Rp1,940 per share, totaling roughly Rp2 billion and giving him 0,0032% of the company's equity. Following the purchases, ISAT shares rose 6.66% to Rp2,100, up 21.3% over the past month.

title: PT Pikko Land Development Tbk Announces Resignation of President Director Eko Wiratmoko
body: PT Pikko Land Development Tbk confirmed that President Director Eko Wiratmoko submitted his resignation, which the company received on 4 November 2025, he had been reappointed in June 2024. The firm said daily operations will continue under the remaining two directors, while Eko remains liable for decisions made during his tenure until the resignation is approved by shareholders.

title: PT Buana Graha Utama Buy Shares of Multi Indocitra Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Buana Graha Utama bought shares of Multi Indocitra Tbk (MICE.JK) at approximately IDR 625, increasing holdings from 286,283,018 to 286,325,618. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Sintawati Halim Buy Shares of Erajaya Swasembada Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Sintawati Halim bought shares of Erajaya Swasembada Tbk (ERAA.JK), increasing holdings from 7,500,000 to 10,615,315. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia Targets White Sugar Self‑Sufficiency by 2026
body: Indonesia’s agriculture ministry announced a plan to achieve full self‑sufficiency of white sugar by 2026, backed by a Rp371 trillion downstream investment and the construction or reactivation of ten sugar factories, while also aiming for total self‑sufficiency including industrial bioethanol by 2030. The initiative, led by Minister Andi Amran Sulaiman and supported by CEO Danantara R. Perkasa Roeslani, seeks to revive the country’s once‑glorious sugar industry and boost food security.

title: Indonesia's Forestry Ministry Drafts New Regulations to Expand Voluntary Carbon Trading Ahead of COP30
body: During the 30th UN Climate Change Conference in Belém, Brazil, Indonesia’s forestry ministry delegation met with Verra to discuss opportunities in the voluntary carbon market, and the ministry announced it is drafting new ministerial regulations under Presidential Regulation No. 110/2025 to treat carbon as a primary commodity. The government aims to build a credible carbon trading framework and has set a target of Rp16 trillion in carbon trade at COP30.

title: Indonesia Targets Rp16 Trillion in Carbon Credit Sales at COP30
body: Indonesia aims to generate Rp16 trillion by selling 90 million tons of carbon credits from forest, marine ecosystem, and energy projects during the 30th UN Climate Change Conference in Belém, Brazil, and will host daily one‑hour “sellers‑meet‑buyers” sessions at its pavilion to facilitate trade.

title: Indonesia to Invest Rp20 Trillion in Integrated Poultry Farms to Boost Food Security
body: Indonesia announced a Rp20 trillion investment to build integrated broiler and layer farms across the country, with funding from the sovereign wealth fund Danantara, aiming to support the MBG nutrition program and achieve self‑sufficiency in chicken meat and eggs. The project, slated to begin in January 2026, will involve pre‑feasibility studies and target regions facing shortages to stabilize supply and prices.

title: Indonesia's Agriculture Downstreaming Program Aims to Create 8 Million Jobs with Rp371 Trillion Investment
body: Indonesia’s agriculture minister Andi Amran Sulaiman announced a downstreaming program that will invest Rp371 trillion in food, plantation, and livestock sectors, aiming to create up to 8 million jobs, with the first phase targeting 3 million workers over three years. The program will prioritize commodities such as sugarcane, cocoa, and cashews, and the minister cited the coconut industry as an example of how local processing can raise prices from Rp600 to Rp3,500 per coconut.

title: Indonesia Drafts Bill to Redenominate Rupiah by 2027
body: The Indonesian government has begun drafting a bill to remove three zeros from rupiah denominations, with the bill slated for completion in 2027, aiming to improve transaction efficiency, strengthen competitiveness, and stabilize the currency. Bank Indonesia’s governor has expressed readiness to support the policy, though execution will require careful management and public education.

title: PT Sunson Textile Manufacturer Tbk Announces Strategy to Strengthen Performance Amid SSTM Share Suspension
body: During a virtual public expose, Director and Corporate Secretary Mahayu Pudjiarto outlined PT Sunson Textile Manufacturer Tbk’s positive financial trend from January to September 2025, reporting net sales of Rp185.92 billion, operating profit of Rp4.53 billion, and net profit of Rp4.09 billion, while emphasizing operational efficiency, product quality, and the expansion of its yarn spinning division to boost capacity utilization and customer base. The company also noted that its shares (SSTM) remain suspended at Rp875 since October 30 2025, following a significant price surge of 176.90 % in the past month and a 351.63 % jump in the last quarter.

title: PT asuransi jiwa syariah jasa mitra abadi Tbk denies involvement in shareholder structure change amid BEI inquiry
body: PT asuransi jiwa syariah jasa mitra abadi Tbk (JMAS) said it has no specific information on the background, price, or parties involved in the recent change of its shareholder structure and that it was not directly involved in the transaction, noting no material events or strategic ties with PT pijar investasi. JMAS shares rose 0.6% to Rp151 on Friday, marking an 11.3% gain over six months from Rp136 on May 9, 2025.

title: djasa ubersakti plans Rp60 billion private placement to raise up to 3.75 billion shares
body: djasa ubersakti will conduct a private placement of Rp60 billion, offering up to 3.75 billion new shares at Rp16 each, aiming to strengthen its capital structure and increase the total shares to 5.25 billion. The company will seek investor approval at an extraordinary general meeting on 23 December 2025 at Shisha Star, Tebet Barat, Jakarta Selatan.

title: Avia Avian Receives Rp99.99 Billion Interim Dividend from Subsidiary, Reporting Strong Q3 2025 Results
body: Avia Avian received an interim dividend of Rp99.99 billion from its wholly‑owned subsidiary Tirtakencana Tatawarna, deposited on 7 November 2025, with no adverse impact on operations. For the quarter ended 30 September 2025, Avian Brands posted sales of Rp5.9 trillion (up 9.7% YoY) and a net profit of Rp1.18 trillion, yielding a 20% net margin.

title: PPRE Secures New Mining Contract with PT Sumberdaya Arindo in Halmahera Timur
body: PPRE has secured a new contract with PT Sumberdaya Arindo, a subsidiary of Aneka Tambang, to perform land clearing, cut‑fill, material hauling, pavement, and road maintenance work in Tanjung Buli, Halmahera Timur. The company says the project will strengthen its mining services, improve operational efficiency, and support Indonesia’s national mining industry while maintaining high safety and environmental standards.

title: Garuda Indonesia to raise Rp23.67 trillion via private placement of 315.61 billion new Series D shares at Rp75 each
body: Garuda Indonesia will raise Rp23.67 trillion by issuing 315.61 billion new Series D shares at Rp75 each, with 37% earmarked for working capital and aircraft maintenance and 63% for capitalizing Citilink through loan conversion and cash injection. The proceeds aim to strengthen Garuda’s balance sheet, support Citilink’s restructuring, and will dilute existing shares by 21.29% pending approval at an extraordinary AGM on November 12, 2025.

title: Pudjiadi Sons plans to raise IDR 152.4 billion by selling 40,640 sqm of land to Bekat BENOA Propertindo
body: The company will sell a non‑operational land parcel owned by Bali Realtindo BENOA to Bekat BENOA Propertindo for IDR 152.4 billion, representing about 78.12% of its equity as of 30 June 2025, to improve its financial structure. Shareholder approval will be sought at an extraordinary general meeting on 17 December 2025.

title: PT Infiniti Wahana Sell Shares of PT Steady Safe Tbk
body: PT Infiniti Wahana, an insider, sold 200,000 shares of PT Steady Safe Tbk at a price of 175. The transaction resulted in a decrease in PT Infiniti Wahana's holdings from 244,301,913 to 244,101,913. The purpose of the transaction was divestment, occurring on October 28, 2025, in the transportation-logistic sector.

title: PT Infiniti Wahana Sell Shares of PT Steady Safe Tbk
body: PT Infiniti Wahana, an insider, sold 1161200 shares of PT Steady Safe Tbk, a transportation-logistic company, on October 30, 2025, at various prices. The transaction reduced PT Infiniti Wahana's holdings from 244101913 to 242940713 shares. The purpose of the transaction was divestment.

title: PT Infiniti Wahana Buy Shares of PT Steady Safe Tbk
body: PT Infiniti Wahana, an insider, bought 4526000 shares of PT Steady Safe Tbk, increasing its holding from 242940713 to 247466713. The transaction occurred on November 5, 2025, for investment purposes, with prices ranging from 224 to 248.

title: KB Bank and MSIG Life launch Smart Wealth Assurance to offer flexible dual-use life insurance
body: PT Bank KB Indonesia Tbk and PT MSIG Life Insurance Indonesia Tbk have introduced Smart Wealth Assurance, a dual-purpose life insurance product that provides a guaranteed benefit of up to 175% of the guaranteed fund, a death benefit of 110% of the principal, and an additional 110% for accidental death. The policy also includes a premium waiver feature and flexible payment and coverage periods, aiming to support Indonesian families in planning a stable and structured financial future.

title: Salim Ivomas Reports 76% YoY Net Profit Growth to Rp1.41 Trillion for FY 2025
body: Salim Ivomas posted a net profit of Rp1.41 trillion for the year ended September 30 2025, up 76.25% from Rp806.19 billion in 2024, while revenue from customer contracts rose to Rp14.92 trillion and gross profit climbed to Rp3.63 trillion. Operating profit reached Rp2.74 trillion, and total equity increased to Rp25.72 trillion, although operating expenses and financial costs also rose.

title: Tower Bersama to Issue Rp2.2 Trillion in Bonds and Sukuk with Structured Series and Maturities
body: Tower Bersama will raise Rp2.2 trillion through Rp1.6 trillion of sustainable bonds (two series with 5.50% and 5.85% rates, maturing 2028 and 2030) and Rp600 billion of sukuk ijarah (two series with 11 and 23.40 billion annual instalments, maturing 2028 and 2030). Proceeds will fund existing obligations and provide financing for Tower Bersama’s loan repayments to Bank Negara Indonesia, with the offering scheduled for 17‑18 November 2025 and listing on 24 November 2025.

title: Nusantara Infrastructure to Pay Interim Dividend of Rp45.56 Billion Based on Net Profit of Rp76.39 Billion as of September 30, 2025
body: Nusantara Infrastructure will distribute an interim dividend of Rp45.56 billion, equivalent to Rp2.63 per share, funded by 60% of its net profit of Rp76.39 billion reported for the nine months ended September 30 2025. The dividend will be recorded on November 20, 2025, with payment scheduled for December 3, 2025, following the board’s approval on November 6, 2025.

title: Bumi Resources Completes 100% Acquisition of Wolfram Limited for Rp698.98 Billion
body: Bumi Resources has finalized the purchase of 100% of Wolfram Limited, paying Rp698.98 billion (AUD 63.5 million) and becoming the sole shareholder as of 8 November 2025. The deal, approved by Australia’s Foreign Investment Review Board, is part of Bumi Resources’ strategy to diversify beyond coal into strategic and critical minerals, with no material impact on its operations or financial condition.

title: Cakra Buana Secures Rp4.3 Trillion Ship Lease and Plans Right Issue to Strengthen Capital Structure
body: Cakra Buana Resources Energi has secured an eight‑year lease of the Hilong 106 offshore support vessel for Rp4.3 trillion, boosting its fleet utilisation and expanding its oil and gas service base. The company will hold an extraordinary general meeting on 18 December 2025 to approve a right issue that includes conversion of promissory notes from Hilong Shipping Holding Limited, Yafin Tandiono Tan, Saga Investama Sedaya and Superkrane Mitra Utama, positioning Cakra Buana as a global maritime player.

title: Multi Bintang and Delta Djakarta Report Slight Revenue Growth but Declining Net Profits in Q3 2025
body: In Q3 2025, Multi Bintang reported a net loss of Rp724.19 billion, down 5 % from Rp762.56 billion a year earlier, with sales up 1.72 % to Rp2.36 trillion. Delta Djakarta’s nine‑month net profit fell 0.52 % to Rp101.23 billion, while sales rose 0.3 % to Rp484.19 billion.

title: Kalon Holdings Extends Mandom Indonesia Tender Offer, Extending Closing Date to November 27, 2025
body: Kalon Holdings Co, Ltd has extended its tender offer for Mandom Corporation Japan shares from September 26 to November 19, 2025, adding seven business days and pushing the transaction completion to November 27, 2025. The company plans to acquire up to 40,467,222 shares at JPY 1,960 each, and after the deal Mandom Indonesia’s shares surged to Rp3,200, up 32.78% from Rp2,410.

title: Cakra Buana Resources Plans 48 Billion Rupiah Right Issue to Strengthen Capital Structure and Convert Debt to Equity
body: Cakra Buana Resources will offer up to 48 billion rupiah of new shares at a nominal value of Rp25, with an extraordinary general meeting scheduled for December 18 2025, after shareholders are registered by November 24 2025. The proceeds will be used to pay part of existing debt, provide working capital, and expand the fleet, while the company has already received conversion notices from Hilong Shipping Holding Limited, Yafin Tandiono Tan, Saga Investama Sedaya, and Superkrane Mitra Utama to convert their promissory notes into equity.

title: Mitrabahtera Reports 55.76% Increase in Net Profit to Rp307.19 Billion for Q3 2025
body: For the quarter ended September 30, 2025, Mitrabahtera posted net profit of Rp307.19 billion, up 55.76% from Rp197.21 billion a year earlier, while revenue rose to Rp663.19 billion. Earnings per share surged to Rp175.54 from Rp112.69, and total equity climbed to Rp3.85 trillion, reflecting stronger profitability and asset growth.

title: Garuda Indonesia’s public float falls below 7.5% after a 22.43% dilution from a Rp23.67 trillion private placement
body: Garuda Indonesia’s public float shrank to 5.03% after a 22.43% dilution from a Rp23.67 trillion private placement offering 315.61 billion new Series D shares at Rp75 each, with capital contributions from Andantara Asset Management (Rp17.02 trillion) and loan conversions (Rp6.65 trillion). The company pledges to meet the 7.5% free‑float requirement through future share sales, adjustments to public offerings, or other corporate actions, while Andantara will become the majority shareholder and Trans Airways, owned by Chairul Tanjung, will hold only 1.79%.

title: Sejahteraraya Reports Rp88.46 Billion Loss in First Nine Months of 2025
body: Sejahteraraya, the issuer Sri Tahir, reported a net loss of Rp88.46 billion for the first nine months of 2025, down 1.173% from a profit of Rp8.24 billion in the same period last year. Revenue rose 8.72% to Rp1.87 trillion, but operating profit fell to Rp84.71 million from Rp142.47 million, and the loss per basic share was Rp7.23 compared to a prior profit of Rp0.69.

title: Muhammad Quraish Shihab Resigns as Chairman of Sharia Supervisory Board at PT Bank Tabungan Negara Tbk, with Parallel Resignations at PT Bank CIMB Niaga Tbk
body: PT Bank Tabungan Negara Tbk announced that Muhammad Quraish Shihab submitted his resignation as chairman of its Sharia supervisory board, with the letter dated 3 November 2025, received on 4 November 2025, and effective upon the separation of the bank’s Sharia unit in accordance with OJK Regulation No. 2/2024. The bank said the departure will not affect its operations, legal standing, financial condition or business continuity, and noted that Quraish Shihab, appointed on 23 March 2018, also recently resigned from the Sharia board of PT Bank CIMB Niaga Tbk amid its sharia business spin‑off.

title: Garuda Indonesia Tbk appoints KJPP Areyanti & Rekan as independent valuator, reporting a market value of Rp6.88 trillion (≈Rp75 per share) and outlining a Rp23.67 trillion private placement
body: The valuation, based on discounted cash flow and market‑comparable methods, estimates the total market value of 100 % of Garuda Indonesia's shares at Rp6.88 trillion (US$423.9 million) or about Rp75 per share. The airline plans a private placement of 315.61 billion new D‑series shares at Rp75 each, raising Rp23.67 trillion to fund working‑capital, aircraft maintenance (37 %) and a capital boost for Citilink (63 %).

title: PT Batavia Prosperindo Trans Tbk appoints Williem Tanjung Oscar as new principal commissioner
body: At an extraordinary shareholders meeting on 6 November 2025, PT Batavia Prosperindo Trans Tbk appointed Williem Tanjung Oscar as principal commissioner, replacing Rudy Johansen who resigned. The board of directors remains unchanged, with Paulus Handigdo as President Director and Rima Rupita as Director, and the new board’s term runs until the 2028 annual meeting.

title: PT bank mandiri Tbk. board member Danis Subyantoro increases stake by 3,784 shares under management stock ownership program
body: Danis Subyantoro acquired 3,784 new PT bank mandiri Tbk. shares at an exercise price of Rp4,260 each, raising his holding to 348,584 shares (0,00037% of total) valued at roughly Rp16.11 million. The transaction does not affect control or operations, and the stock was trading at Rp4,750, up 0.85% weekly, 8.20% monthly, down 0.42% over six months and down 18.8% year‑to‑date.

title: PT erajaya swasembada Tbk. reports management stock option allocations to three directors
body: Three directors of PT erajaya swasembada Tbk. received shares under the management employee stock option plan at an exercise price of Rp437 per share, totaling about Rp2.85 billion; Budiarto Halim, Sintawati Halim and Jong Woon Kim now hold approximately 0.05%, 0.07% and 0.01% of the company's issued capital respectively. On Friday, ERAA shares edged up 0.94% to Rp428, a 4‑point rise from the prior close.

title: Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara Sell Shares of PT Dharma Polimetal Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara sold shares of PT Dharma Polimetal Tbk (DRMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,100, decreasing holdings from 71,397,600 to 68,897,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

body: PT Infiniti Wahana, an insider, bought 4,526,000 shares of PT Steady Safe Tbk, increasing its holding to 247,466,713 shares.
