title: Indonesia’s B50 Biodiesel Plan Sparks Global Palm Oil Concerns Amid EU Deforestation Rules
body: Indonesia’s proposal to mandate a B50 biodiesel blend—50 % palm‑oil‑derived FAME—has alarmed the palm‑oil sector, with industry leaders warning that the move, seen as a response to EU deforestation regulations, could accelerate a global phase‑out of palm oil. Meanwhile, Malaysia is also advancing a B30 mandate, and critics like Mohamad Helmy Othman Basha argue that EU risk classifications unfairly target developing producers while overlooking larger fossil‑fuel emissions, potentially reshaping edible‑oil and biofuel markets.

title: Indonesia’s palm oil sector plans to boost productivity and reach 92 million tonnes by 2045 through mandatory ISPO certification, replanting, and derivative development
body: The government announced that Indonesia’s 16.38 million‑hectare palm oil plantations—53 % private, 6 % state, 41 % smallholders—will undergo replanting to raise productivity from below 4 t/ha for smallholders to 10–12 t/ha for large firms, potentially doubling or tripling output in four years. The target is to increase national production to 92 million tonnes by 2045, up from 53 million tonnes today, supported by mandatory ISPO certification, a traceability system, and a biodiesel program that has stabilized fresh fruit bunch prices.

title: PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk Projects $1.5 Billion Investment for Seven Bukit Underground Mining Project
body: PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk estimates the total investment for its Seven Bukit underground mining project will reach about USD 1.5 billion, excluding smelter costs, with the project still in the pre‑production stage and drilling underway. The company targets 110,000 gold ounces and 11,300 tonnes of copper annually, while a feasibility study is expected to finish this year before construction and commercial production begin.

title: PT bumi resources Tbk expands overseas mining, acquires jubilee metals limited, and enters major Indonesian indices
body: PT bumi resources Tbk is broadening its portfolio by acquiring jubilee metals limited through a debt‑to‑equity conversion that will give it 64.98% control and cost about AUD 13.5 million (≈IDR 120 billion). In Q3 2025 the company posted a net profit of USD 29.4 million (down 76% YoY) on revenue of USD 1.03 billion (up 11.9% YoY), and its shares entered the LQ45, IDX80 and Bisnis‑27 indices, contributing 0.73% to LQ45 with a 29.19% free float and a price‑to‑book ratio of 1.17x.

title: Indonesia Positions Itself as Key Aluminum Supplier for Global EV Automotive Supply Chain
body: Indonesia’s PT Indonesia Asahan Aluminium, with a production capacity of over 300,000 t per year, is poised to become a primary raw‑material supplier for the global automotive industry, especially electric‑vehicle makers in Japan, South Korea and China, as the country shifts from exporting raw bauxite to producing higher‑value alumina and aluminum. A new 1 million‑ton‑per‑year alumina refinery by PT Aneka Tambang Tbk will feed the smelter, strengthening the upstream‑to‑downstream supply chain and supporting Indonesia’s strategy to secure a larger share of the EV market.

title: PT Trisula Insan Tiara Buy Shares of Trisula International Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Trisula Insan Tiara bought shares of Trisula International Tbk (TRIS.JK), increasing holdings from 411,666,800 to 711,666,800. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Inti Nusa Damai Sell Shares of Trisula International Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Inti Nusa Damai sold shares of Trisula International Tbk (TRIS.JK), decreasing holdings from 1,635,384,048 to 635,384,048. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Archi Indonesia Tbk Posts $71 Million Net Profit in Q3 2025, Driven by Higher Gold Production and Diversification into Geothermal Energy
body: PT Archi Indonesia Tbk reported a net profit of USD 71 million (IDR 1.18 trillion) for the first nine months of 2025, reversing a USD 4 million loss in the same period last year, thanks to a 23 % increase in gold production to 90 k ounces and rising global gold prices. The company also highlighted its geothermal development through PT Toka Tidung Geothermal, a joint venture with PT Ormat Geothermal Indonesia, targeting a 40 MW plant in North Sulawesi and supported by PT Sarana Multi Infrastruktur and World Bank risk‑mitigation financing.

title: PT PAM Mineral Tbk Reports 64.82% Sales Growth and 104.53% Gross Profit Increase in Q3 2025
body: PT PAM Mineral Tbk's Q3 2025 sales rose to IDR 1.35 trillion, up 64.82% YoY, driven by an 88.76% jump in nickel volume. Gross profit surged 104.53% to IDR 600.92 billion and net profit climbed 131.28% to IDR 401.66 billion, while total assets fell 7.45% to IDR 971.88 trillion.

title: SMGA Enters Nickel Smelting with Strategic Partnership to Build Converter Smelter Using Oxygen‑Enriched Side Blowing Furnace Technology
body: SMGA, a subsidiary of PT Sumber Global Energy Tbk and PT Sumber Mineral Global Abadi Tbk, has entered the nickel smelting business by signing a strategic partnership with PT Hengsheng New Energy Material Indonesia, acquiring a stake and collaborating to build a converter smelter that uses oxygen‑enriched side‑blowing furnace technology to upgrade low‑grade nickel matte to high‑grade. The partnership aims to strengthen SMGA’s position in Indonesia’s integrated nickel ecosystem, support the green economy, and capitalize on projected global nickel demand growth from 545,000 tons in 2024 to over 1.5 million tons by 2030, while improving refining efficiency and reducing emissions.

title: PT Karya Dwimanunggal Sejahtera Buy Shares of Trisula International Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Karya Dwimanunggal Sejahtera bought shares of Trisula International Tbk (TRIS.JK), increasing holdings from 280,120,000 to 980,120,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia Issues 3 Million New Business IDs in First Year of Prabowo Administration
body: Indonesia's Deputy Minister of Investment reported that about 3 million new business identification numbers were issued in the first year of President Prabowo’s administration, raising the total NIBs issued through the online single submission system from 10.6 million to 14 million. The ministry also highlighted Regulation No. 28/2025, which introduces a “fictive positive” service‑level agreement that automatically approves licenses after the deadline, covering 132 permit types such as hotel licenses that must be completed within 14 days.

title: Indonesia Weighs Restarting PT Freeport Indonesia Operations After September 2025 Landslide
body: Energy Mineral Resources Minister Bahlil Lahadalia inspected PT Freeport Indonesia’s unaffected mining areas following the September 2025 wet ore landslide at Grasberg Block Cave, and the company is seeking permission to resume production while evaluating mitigation measures. The Gresik smelter has remained idle since the incident, and the company is calculating the financial impact of the month‑long shutdown.

title: Toyota Tsusho Corporation to Invest $100 Million in Indonesia’s Tin and Copper Downstream Sectors
body: Toyota Tsusho Corporation announced a $100 million investment to build a solder paste production facility in partnership with PT Timah Tbk, aiming to support Indonesia’s 18 % share of global tin supply and meet rising demand for solder paste projected to grow from 5,170 tons in 2024 to 6,300 tons in 2029. The move comes amid Japan’s status as Indonesia’s fourth‑largest foreign investor, with $18.89 billion invested over five years and an average annual growth of 12.4 %.

title: Toyota Motor Corporation plans bioethanol investment to support Indonesia's E10 fuel blend policy
body: Deputy Minister of Investment and Downstreaming Todotua Pasaribu said Indonesia will need about 4 million kiloliters of bioethanol by 2027 for the E10 policy and Toyota motor corporation aims to establish a joint venture with Pertamina in 2026 to build a 60,000‑kiloliter annual facility valued at Rp2.5 trillion. The move aligns with President Prabowo Subianto's energy self‑sufficiency goals and Toyota's development of second‑generation bioethanol using non‑food biomass.

title: John Veter Firdaus Buy Shares of PT Pelayaran Jaya Hidup Baru Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, John Veter Firdaus bought shares of PT Pelayaran Jaya Hidup Baru Tbk (PJHB.JK) at approximately IDR 1,000, increasing holdings from 86,515,200 to 96,015,200. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia opens retail subscription to Shariah sukuk ST015 with 5.20% and 5.45% floating yields for 2‑ and 4‑year tenors
body: The government of Indonesia, via SBSN Indonesia, opened subscription for retail investors to purchase the ST015 sukuk series from 10 November to 3 December 2025, offering a 2‑year ST015T2 at a 5.20% floating coupon (minimum Rp1 million, up to Rp5 billion) and a 4‑year green ST015T4 at a 5.45% floating coupon (up to Rp10 billion). As of 19:55 WIB, sales reached 11.08% of the Rp5 trillion quota for ST015T2 and 5.28% for ST015T4, with PT bank central asia Tbk (BCA) and Bibit noting the competitive yields and early‑redemption feature.

title: CP Prima reports a 24.6% increase in nine‑month 2025 net profit to Rp426.73 billion.
body: For the first nine months of 2025, CP Prima posted net profit of Rp426.73 billion, up 24.59% year‑on‑year, with earnings per share rising to Rp7.2 and revenue reaching Rp7.33 trillion. The company’s total assets grew to Rp7.09 trillion, equity to Rp3.99 trillion, while liabilities fell to Rp3.10 trillion.

title: PT Merdeka Gold Resources Tbk reports 83% completion of the Pani gold mine, begins mining in October 2025 and aims for first gold production in Q1 2026
body: The company announced that the Pani gold mine in Gorontalo reached 83% construction completion, with mining commencing on 1 Oct 2025, first blasting on 15 Oct 2025, and a target to start ore stockpiling in Nov 2025 and produce gold in Q1 2026. It also disclosed a US$208.7 million investment in the project, reserves of 190.3 million tonnes of ore containing 4.8 million oz of gold and 4.9 million oz of silver, and a successful IPO that provides financial flexibility for further development.

title: Indonesia Stock Exchange ends FCA status for PT Pudjiadi Prestige Tbk and PT Mora Telematika Indonesia Tbk, shares surge
body: Effective 11 November 2025, the IDX removed the full‑call auction (FCA) status of PT Pudjiadi Prestige Tbk and PT Mora Telematika Indonesia Tbk, and both stocks jumped about 25% on the first regular‑session trade, reaching Rp925 and Rp3,250 respectively. In the preceding week, PUDP rose 30.94% and MOR A rose 81.56%, with quarterly gains of 335.71% and 759.79% and year‑to‑date increases of 285.59% and 591.49%.

title: Lippo Karawaci reports sharp decline in Q3 2025 net profit
body: Lippo Karawaci posted a net profit of Rp368.19 billion for Q3 2025, a 98.03% drop from Rp18.71 trillion a year earlier, and earnings per share fell to Rp5.19 from Rp264.10. Revenue fell 29.72% to Rp6.5 trillion, while total assets decreased to Rp49.35 trillion and liabilities to Rp18.34 trillion.

title: Handi Sutanto Rebuilds Small Stake in Aneka Tambang After Prior Sale
body: On November 7, 2025, Handi Sutanto purchased 1,700 shares of Aneka Tambang at Rp2,940 each, spending roughly Rp4.99 million and raising his ownership to 0,0000071 percent. Earlier, he had sold 300 shares on October 16, 2025 for Rp3,330 per share, and on November 10, 2025 the stock was trading at Rp2,910, up 0.34%.

title: Citra Putra Realty reports a Q3 2025 net loss of Rp9.72 billion as revenue drops 50% year‑on‑year
body: For the third quarter of 2025, Citra Putra Realty posted a net loss of Rp9.79 billion (loss per share Rp3.78) compared with a loss of Rp1.59 billion a year earlier, while revenue fell to Rp53.12 billion from Rp107.03 billion. The company's equity shrank to Rp3.33 billion, total assets to Rp532.35 billion, and total liabilities slightly increased to Rp529.14 billion.

title: Sinergi Inti Andalan Prima conducts due diligence to acquire 60% of Trans Hybrid Communication for West Kalimantan fiber expansion
body: Sinergi Inti Andalan Prima (Sinergy Networks) is evaluating a purchase of a 60% stake in Trans Hybrid Communication (THC) to leverage its fiber‑optic backbone in West Kalimantan, aiming to launch fiber‑home services and accelerate FTTH coverage. The move is part of a broader mid‑term expansion plan that also includes acquiring Persada and a right issue to fund larger ISP, IP‑transit and managed‑service operations.

title: John Veter Firdaus increases stake in Pelayaran Jaya Hidup Baru
body: On November 10, 2025, John Veter Firdaus purchased 9.5 million shares of Pelayaran Jaya Hidup Baru at a premium price of Rp1,000 per share—about 56.25% above the closing price of Rp640—costing roughly Rp9.5 billion and raising his holding to 96.01 million shares (5% of the company). The transaction, described by CEO Go Sioe Bie as a direct personal investment rather than a repurchase agreement, increased his stake from 86.51 million shares (4.51%) to the current level.

title: PT Futura Energi Global Tbk appoints new board and outlines geothermal, floating solar and forest carbon projects
body: The extraordinary shareholders meeting appointed General Police Sutanto as President Commissioner, Harry Maryanto Supoyo as Commissioner and Anggara Suryawan as President Director of PT Futura Energi Global Tbk. The company plans to reactivate a 30 MW geothermal exploration in Purwokerto with an estimated USD 120 million investment, launch a floating solar PV project in Bali in Q2 2026, and develop a 70,000‑hectare forest carbon project in Sulawesi targeting carbon market entry in 2027, all of which are still in development and will not impact next‑year revenue.

title: Surya Citra Media Announces Rp571.2 Billion Interim Dividend for FY 2025
body: Surya Citra Media will pay an interim dividend of Rp571.2 billion, representing 96.65% of its net profit of Rp591.57 billion for the nine months ended September 30 2025, resulting in a dividend of Rp9 per share. The dividend will be recorded on November 20 2025, with payment scheduled for December 9 2025, and the relevant cum‑ and ex‑dividend dates are outlined in the announcement.

title: PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk denies any agreement with Grab and announces an extraordinary shareholders meeting on 25 November 2025
body: Legal and corporate secretary R. A. Koesoemohadiani confirmed that no decision or agreement has been reached regarding a potential transaction with Grab, emphasizing the company's compliance with regulations and its strong strategic position reflected in recent quarterly results. He also stated that the upcoming extraordinary general shareholders meeting is part of good corporate governance and is unrelated to any corporate action.

title: PT mitra investindo Tbk Announces Private Placement of 209.79 Million B Shares to Raise Up to Rp60 Billion
body: The company will issue up to 209.79 million new B shares at a price of Rp286 per share, representing 5.59% of its total shares and potentially raising Rp60 billion. Proceeds will fund business expansion, silica mineral projects, capital expenditures, and internal financing, increasing paid‑up capital from Rp231.04 billion to Rp241.52 billion.

title: Rudi Reksa Sutantra Increases Stake in PT Atlantis Subsea Indonesia Tbk.
body: Rudi Reksa Sutantra, the controlling shareholder of PT Atlantis Subsea Indonesia Tbk., has increased his stake in the company by purchasing 2.3 million shares on November 5, 2025. The purchase valued at IDR 151.8 million, increasing his stake to 49.23% from 49.19%.

title: PT Telefast Indonesia Tbk reports selling 203,100 shares from its buyback program, generating Rp49.23 million in funds.
body: PT Telefast Indonesia Tbk has sold 203,100 shares acquired through its buyback program at an average price of Rp245 per share, resulting in total proceeds of Rp49.23 million. The company's stock price has experienced significant fluctuations, dropping 40% in the last month but rising 74.2% over the past six months and 121% year-to-date.

title: PT Dharma Polimetal Tbk's Commissioner Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara Sells Shares in Two Transactions
body: Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara, Commissioner of PT Dharma Polimetal Tbk, sold 3,452,400 shares in two transactions on November 4, 2025, with a total value of Rp2,750,000,000, reducing his ownership to 68,897,600 shares or 1.464%. The transactions consisted of a repo transaction of 952,400 shares at Rp1,050 per share and a divestment transaction of 2,500,000 shares at Rp1,100 per share.

title: Bursa Efek Indonesia lifts trading suspension on PT Djasula Ubersakti Tbk after the company fulfills its financial reporting obligations.
body: The trading suspension on PT Djasula Ubersakti Tbk has been lifted, allowing its shares to be traded again in the regular and cash markets, after the company completed its outstanding financial reporting requirements. PT Djasula Ubersakti Tbk is also planning a private placement of up to 3.75 billion new shares at Rp16 per share to improve its capital structure and financial position.

title: Robin Sunyoto Buy Shares of PT Hero Global Investment Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Robin Sunyoto bought shares of PT Hero Global Investment Tbk (HGII.JK) at approximately IDR 7,800,000, increasing holdings from 13,225,000 to 13,275,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk shares jump 9.8% on merger rumors with Grab
body: PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk closed at Rp 67 per share, up 9.84% and 13.56% over the past week, amid intensified merger speculation with Grab and confirmed government investment involvement. Mirae Asset analyst Nafan Aji Gusta recommends accumulating the stock with a short‑term target price of Rp 74 per share.

title: PT Aneka Tambang Tbk reports Q3 2025 net profit of Rp1.3 trillion amid gold sales slump and strong nickel performance
body: The company posted a net profit of Rp1.3 trillion in Q3 2025, a 50% quarterly decline but a 96% year‑on‑year increase, driven by a sharp drop in gold sales volume to 4,900 tons after the Grasberg block‑cave mudslide, while nickel ore prices rose to $56.7 per wmt supporting the segment. Over the first nine months, net profit rose 171% YoY to Rp5.9 trillion, and analysts issued mixed recommendations ranging from hold (target Rp3,200) to buy (target up to Rp4,100).

title: PT Infiniti Wahana Sell Shares of Steady Safe Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Infiniti Wahana sold shares of Steady Safe Tbk (SAFE.JK) at approximately IDR 177, decreasing holdings from 246,556,613 to 244,301,813. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Infiniti Wahana Sell Shares of Steady Safe Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Infiniti Wahana sold shares of Steady Safe Tbk (SAFE.JK) at approximately IDR 177, decreasing holdings from 246,556,613 to 244,301,813. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Lotte Chemical Titan Tbk. clarifies no material information is available to influence stock price volatility
body: PT Lotte Chemical Titan Tbk stated that it is unaware of any material information that could affect the company's stock price, in response to a clarification request from the Indonesia Stock Exchange regarding the significant surge in stock transactions. The company's stock, FPNI, had experienced a significant increase, rising 101.17% in a week and 175.40% in a month, before being suspended by the Indonesia Stock Exchange to allow investors to make informed decisions.

body: According to the published announcement, Handi Sutanto sold shares of Aneka Tambang Tbk. (ANTM.JK) at approximately IDR 3,330. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Ali Gunawan Buy Shares of PT Allo Bank Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Ali Gunawan bought shares of PT Allo Bank Indonesia Tbk (BBHI.JK) at approximately IDR 1,480, increasing holdings from 10,167,300 to 10,238,900. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Liryawati Sell Shares of PT MAP Boga Adiperkasa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Liryawati sold shares of PT MAP Boga Adiperkasa Tbk (MAPB.JK) at approximately IDR 1,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk Projects $1.5 Billion Investment for Tujuh Bukit Underground Mining, Targeting 110,000 Gold Ounces and 11,000 Tons Copper Production
body: PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk, through its subsidiary PT Bumi Suksisiindo, estimates that the Tujuh Bukit underground mining project will require about USD 1.5 billion, excluding smelter costs, and aims to produce roughly 110,000 gold ounces and 11,000 tons of copper annually once commercial production begins. The company is still in pre‑production, conducting drilling and feasibility studies, with a planned transition from open‑pit to underground operations and a projected 30‑plus year life at peak capacity of 24 million tons of ore per year.

title: Indonesia Identifies 920,000 Hectares for Ethanol Production to Meet 1 Million Hectare Goal
body: The Indonesian government has earmarked about 920,000 hectares across 18 provinces for ethanol feedstock, bringing it close to the 1 million‑hectare target needed for producing E10 fuel. The plan includes tax incentives and a 2027 mandate, with an annual ethanol requirement of 1.4 million kiloliters and support from a Brazil cooperation agreement.

title: Indonesia Projected to Enter Global Top Four Ceramic Producers Amid Strong Investment Growth
body: Indonesia’s Ministry of Industry, via Director General Taufiek Bawazier, announced that the country is on track to become the world’s fourth‑largest ceramic producer, citing a 10.07% YoY growth in the ceramics sub‑sector and Rp20.3 trillion in investment realization between 2020‑2024. The government is developing strategic industrial zones and offering incentives to support this expansion, with production utilization rising to 72.5% in the first ten months of 2025.

title: Indonesia Positions Itself as Global Hub for High-Integrity Carbon Credits Ahead of COP30
body: At the 2025 UN Climate Change Conference in Belém, Deputy Minister Rohmat Marzuki highlighted Indonesia’s strategy to offer high‑integrity carbon credits, backed by new regulations and a social forestry program that has allocated 8.4 million hectares to 1.4 million households and created 5.6 million green jobs. The country also aims to reach Rp16 trillion in carbon trade by COP30, positioning itself as a key player in the global carbon market.

title: Indonesia's Industry Ministry Pushes Local MSMEs to Cut Import Dependence, Highlights Steel Sector Growth
body: The Ministry of Industry announced a partnership scheme to strengthen collaboration between secondhand clothing traders and local MSMEs, aiming to reduce reliance on imported finished goods across sectors such as textiles, steel, electronics, cosmetics, ceramics, and footwear. In the steel sector, Indonesia still depends on 55% imports, but domestic production rose to 18 million tonnes in 2024—a 110% increase from 2019—making it the 14th largest producer worldwide.

title: Indonesia Accelerates Construction of 100 BULOG Warehouses to Strengthen Rice and Corn Absorption
body: The Indonesian government, through a joint ministerial decree signed by key ministers, will build 100 BULOG warehouses across the country at a cost of Rp5 trillion to improve storage and absorption of harvested rice and corn. The move aims to support farmers, streamline the supply chain, and boost national rice production to 34.77 million tons in 2025.

title: Indonesia to Build 100 New Warehouses to Strengthen Rice and Corn Absorption
body: President Prabowo Subianto has ordered the construction of 100 new warehouses by state‑run logistics company Bulog to improve storage and absorption of rice and corn yields, following a joint decree signed by key ministers and an earmarked Rp5 trillion finance initiative. The move comes as rice production is projected to rise 13 % from 30 million to 34.77 million tons this year.

title: Siantar Top reports Q3 2025 earnings with net profit down 15.84% to Rp885.18 billion
body: The company posted a net profit of Rp885.18 billion, down 15.84% from Rp1.01 trillion, while earnings per share fell to Rp675.71 from Rp774.74. Net sales rose to Rp3.86 trillion, but cost of goods and operating expenses increased, leaving total assets at Rp7.68 trillion.

title: cakra buana secures usd 45m loan and issues promissory notes to finance 106-hulled ship acquisition
body: cakra buana has purchased a 106-hulled ship from hilong shipping holding limited for usd 100 million, with 45% (usd 45 million) payable by 31 december 2025, and has obtained a term loan of usd 45 million from bank rakyat indonesia to meet that obligation. to expedite funding, the company also issued promissory notes to yafin tandiono, superkrane, and saga investama sedaya, and to skrn as the designated payment recipient per the seller’s instruction.

title: Widodo Makmur Perkasa posts reduced nine‑month 2025 losses amid higher net sales and lower bond issuance
body: For the first nine months of 2025, Widodo Makmur Perkasa recorded a net loss of IDR 173.59 billion, down from IDR 310.7 billion a year earlier, with loss per share falling to IDR 5.27 from IDR 9.70. Net sales rose 87.33% to IDR 697.62 billion, while bond issuance was cut by 45.64% to IDR 155.04 billion, and equity fell to IDR 519.72 billion as total assets decreased to IDR 4.58 trillion.

title: mitra pack signs conditional share purchase agreement with deep source pte ltd to acquire controlling stake in master print
body: On 11 November 2025, mitra pack entered into a Conditional Share Purchase Agreement with deep source pte ltd for the acquisition of 1.47 billion shares, representing 77.19% of master print, pending fulfillment of pre‑closing conditions and an independent valuation. The parties consider the transaction material and will comply with applicable regulations, with further updates to follow.

title: PT PP to receive IDR 1.41 trillion from divesting 81% of PP Infrastruktur to Varsha Zamindo Laksana
body: PT PP will raise IDR 1.41 trillion by selling 81% (621,161 shares) of its subsidiary PP Infrastruktur to Varsha Zamindo Laksana, aiming to improve operational efficiency, focus on core business and strengthen liquidity. After the transaction, Varsha Zamindo Laksana holds 81% of PP Infrastruktur while PT PP’s stake is reduced to 18.15%.

title: PT Harta Djaya Karya Tbk outlines two‑month timeline for Triple B takeover
body: The management of PT Harta Djaya Karya Tbk said the acquisition by Triple B, announced on 18 September 2025, will take about two months, with due‑diligence expected to finish by the end of November 2025. Subsequent negotiations are slated for December 2025 and the share‑payment process is targeted for completion by the end of January 2026.

title: PT Multi Makmur Lemindo Tbk posts 30% revenue increase and plans diversification as PT Morris Capital Indonesia launches mandatory tender offer for pipa shares
body: PT Multi Makmur Lemindo Tbk's revenue rose 30.49% to Rp25.89 billion for the nine months ended 30 Sept 2025, with pipe segment revenue reaching Rp22.69 billion (87.6% of total). CEO Imanuel Kevin Mayola said the company will diversify into oil‑&‑gas and HDPE pipe manufacturing, targets Rp38 billion revenue, and noted that PT Morris Capital Indonesia’s mandatory tender offer to acquire about 43.8% of pipa shares at Rp21 each could raise its ownership to up to 96.35% without disrupting operations.

title: Happy Hapsoro acquires 19.35% of Pakuan shares at a 78.6% discount
body: On 7 November 2025, Happy Hapsoro purchased 234,178 million shares of Pakuan (19.35% of the company) from controlling shareholder Sirius Surya Sentosa at Rp467 per share, costing about Rp109.36 billion, which is 78.57% below the closing price of Rp2,180. The transaction reduced Sirius Surya Sentosa’s stake to 41% (496.04 million shares) from 60.35% (730.22 million shares).

title: PT sarana mitra luas Tbk targets IDR 600 billion revenue by 2026 through coal‑mining equipment leasing expansion
body: PT sarana mitra luas Tbk aims for IDR 600 billion revenue in 2026, with total assets rising to IDR 1.15 trillion and short‑term liabilities at IDR 105.31 billion as of Q3 2025. The firm is expanding into coal‑mining equipment leasing via a 40% joint operation with partners under sarana cipta minergi, PT barakara and PT atoz at a new mine in Painan, West Sumatra, targeting an initial output of 200,000 tonnes per month and generating significant recurring income.

title: Link Net reports Q3 2025 net loss of Rp1.03 trillion despite 45.7% revenue growth
body: For the quarter ended 30 September 2025, Link Net posted a net loss of Rp1.03 trillion, a 28.75% increase from the same period last year, with loss per share rising to Rp374. Revenue rose 45.73% to Rp2.39 trillion, while total assets grew to Rp15.08 trillion and equity fell to Rp3.97 trillion.

title: Kasikornbank raises its stake in PT Bank Maspion Indonesia Tbk to 89.48% to deepen regional collaboration
body: Kasikornbank increased its ownership of PT Bank Maspion Indonesia Tbk to 89.48%, citing a strategic push into Indonesia’s high‑potential market and the rollout of integrated cross‑border financial solutions for corporate clients across the AEC+3 region. The move aligns with KBank’s regional “bank choice” strategy and leverages Indonesia’s $1.4 trillion GDP, 285 million population and recent Rp208 trillion foreign‑direct investment inflows.

title: PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk reports stable Q3 2025 gold and copper output while expanding nickel operations and advancing new projects
body: In Q3 2025 the company produced 25,338 ounces of gold at an average price of $3,275 per ounce, achieving a 24% higher cash margin and $104 million in revenue, and mined 3,228 tonnes of copper at a cash cost of $2.75 per pound. Nickel operations surged with an 89% YoY increase in saprolite and a 51% rise in limonite, boosting nickel pig iron margin to $2,215 per tonne and lowering cash cost 16%, while the HPAL plant reached 54% completion and other new facilities are slated for commissioning in late 2025.

title: PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi Tbk announces interim cash dividend of IDR 5 per share for FY2025
body: The board approved a total interim cash dividend of IDR 305.74 billion (IDR 5 per share) payable to shareholders on record as of 21 Nov 2025, with ex‑dividend dates on 20 Nov (regular market) and 24 Nov (cash market). The payout follows FY2025 results showing net profit of IDR 6.43 trillion, retained earnings of IDR 15.04 trillion, total equity of IDR 54.50 trillion, and a dividend yield of about 0.39% at a closing price of IDR 1,270.

title: PT Perintis Triniti Properti Tbk posts Q3 2025 net profit of Rp31 bn, a 150% YoY increase from a loss
body: The company recorded a net profit of Rp31 bn in Q3 2025, up 150% year‑on‑year from a Rp62 bn loss and 484% quarter‑on‑quarter from Rp5 bn, driven by cost efficiency, accelerated sales and portfolio optimization. Marketing revenue reached Rp942.7 bn in October, an 18% month‑on‑month rise, supported mainly by the Sequoia Hills and Collins Boulevard projects.

title: PT JSI Sinergi Mas raises its stake in PT Leyand International Tbk to 51% after buying 466.7 million shares
body: PT JSI Sinergi Mas acquired 466,691,324 shares of PT Leyand International Tbk at Rp10 per share, totaling Rp4.67 billion, increasing its ownership from 39.23% to 51%. Following the transaction, PT Leyand International Tbk's stock rose 2.22% to Rp186.

title: Indonesia's October 2025 car sales climb 19% to 74,020 units, with Astra posting a 4% increase to 34,888 units
body: National wholesale car sales rose to 74,020 units in October 2025, a 19% month‑on‑month gain, while Astra's wholesale sales grew 4% to 34,888 units. Astra's corporate communications head attributed the rise to stable economic conditions, recovering market activity, and the company's focus on relevant technology and strong after‑sales service.

title: Indonesian government reviews merger of Grab Holdings Ltd. and PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk., while Danantara considers a minority stake
body: Minister of State Secretariat Prasetyo Hadi said the government is evaluating a merger of Grab Holdings Ltd. and PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk. under a presidential regulation covering driver status, protection and tariffs, with Danantara potentially participating in the deal. If completed, the combined entity could command over 91% of Indonesia's ride‑hailing market; PT Goto's shares rose 2.99% to Rp69, up 25% over the past month.

title: Bakrie Metalindustries, Pt Buy Shares of PT VKTR Teknologi Mobilitas Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Bakrie Metalindustries, Pt bought shares of PT VKTR Teknologi Mobilitas Tbk (VKTR.JK) at approximately IDR 362, increasing holdings from 6,837,196,600 to 6,839,996,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Bakrie Metalindustries, Pt Buy Shares of PT VKTR Teknologi Mobilitas Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Bakrie Metalindustries, Pt bought shares of PT VKTR Teknologi Mobilitas Tbk (VKTR.JK) at approximately IDR 362, increasing holdings from 6,820,777,900 to 6,837,196,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Nalinkant Amratlal Rathod Buy Shares of PT Ancara Logistics Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Nalinkant Amratlal Rathod bought shares of PT Ancara Logistics Indonesia Tbk (ALII.JK) at approximately IDR 555, increasing holdings from 350,521,500 to 352,621,500. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Jsi Sinergi Mas Buy Shares of Leyand International Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Jsi Sinergi Mas bought shares of Leyand International Tbk (LAPD.JK) at approximately IDR 10, increasing holdings from 1,556,147,176 to 2,022,838,500. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Jap Janti Kusuma Jaya Sell Shares of PT MAP Boga Adiperkasa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Jap Janti Kusuma Jaya sold shares of PT MAP Boga Adiperkasa Tbk (MAPB.JK) at approximately IDR 1,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

body: According to the published announcement, Anthony Valentine Mcevoy sold shares of PT MAP Boga Adiperkasa Tbk (MAPB.JK) at approximately IDR 1,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Sirius Surya Sentosa Sell Shares of PT Pakuan Tbk

title: Michael Buy Shares of PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Michael bought shares of PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi Tbk (CUAN.JK) at approximately IDR 2,180, increasing holdings from 3,402,000 to 3,542,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Handi Sutanto Buy Shares of Aneka Tambang Tbk.
body: According to the published announcement, Handi Sutanto bought shares of Aneka Tambang Tbk. (ANTM.JK) at approximately IDR 2,890, increasing holdings from 1,700 to 3,500. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Asmin Bara Bronang and Turangga Resources inaugurate a new coal processing and washing plant to advance their 15 million‑ton annual sales target
body: During the opening ceremony, PT Asmin Bara Bronang, a subsidiary of PT Tuah Turangga Agung and part of the PT United Tractors Tbk group, launched a coal processing plant and Washing Plant 2, with a first hauling operation led by Turangga Resources’ president director Hendra Hutahean. The new facilities, featuring a 425‑ton‑per‑hour washing plant with dense‑medium separator technology and a new hauling road, are expected to boost production capacity and help Turangga Resources reach its 15 million‑ton coal sales target by 2026.

title: PT Energia Prima Nusantara Completes 734.66 kWp Rooftop Solar Project at Sri Facility, Reducing CO2 Emissions
body: PT Energia Prima Nusantara (EPN), a subsidiary of PT United Tractors Tbk, partnered with PT Suryaraya Rubberindo Industries to install a 734.66 kWp rooftop solar plant at Sri’s Cileungsi production facility, projected to generate 799,130 kWh annually and cut CO₂ emissions by 689.35 tons—equivalent to planting over 31,000 trees. The project signals EPN’s commitment to ESG goals and sets the stage for future solar PV initiatives at the company’s plants.

title: IHSG Gains 0.26% on Wednesday, Driven by Foreign Buying of Rp 337bn
body: IHSG strengthened 0.26% to 8,388.56 on Wednesday, driven by net foreign buying of Rp 337bn, with foreign investors heavily buying bmri, bbca, bren, bbni, and untr, and BNI Sekuritas predicts further gains tied to US market moves. The research also lists speculative buy targets for several stocks, including pgas, brpt, dssa, and others, with defined entry and exit levels.

title: PEfindo assigns IDA‑ rating with stable outlook to PT Industri Kereta Api
body: PEfindo (PT pemeringkat efek indonesia) gave PT Industri Kereta Api an IDA‑ rating, citing its strategic role for the Indonesian government, market-leading position in domestic rail equipment manufacturing, and strong financial flexibility. The rating may be upgraded if INKA expands its contract backlog and customer base, but could be downgraded if shareholder support weakens, project deliveries are delayed, or leverage rises significantly.

title: PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi Tbk’s 630 MW Power Plant in Halmahera Set to Generate $328 M Revenue from 2028
body: The company, through its subsidiary Volta Daya Energi Indonesia after acquiring 90 % of PT Guna Darma Integra, expects the 630 MW plant to start contributing in 2028 with projected revenue of $328 million and EBITDA of $133 million, enhancing long‑term cash flow stability. Analyst Nafan Aji Gusta of Mirae Asset Securities views the diversification as a positive catalyst, noting CUAN’s 96.85 % YTD gain and recommending a sell‑strength position.

title: PT Cahayasakti Investindo Sukses Tbk to invest IDR195.83 billion in PT Bogorindo Cemerlang through a rights issue representing 55.31% of its equity
body: The company will raise IDR195.83 billion by issuing 522.8 million new shares at IDR100 each, with funds earmarked for infrastructure projects in Cikembang, Bukit Panenjoan and a Sentul warehouse, and for acquiring land around Cikembar. The material transaction, deemed fair by an independent valuation, coincides with a 24.53% share price jump to IDR264 and an extraordinary shareholders meeting scheduled for 19 December 2025.

title: PT Midi Utama Indonesia Tbk cuts 2025 store expansion target to 150 but maintains bullish outlook and price targets
body: The retailer reduced its planned new Alfamidi openings from 200 to 150 for 2025, with 58 stores slated for Q4, while still targeting 200 openings in 2026; analysts from CGS Sekuritas keep an ADD recommendation with a Rp 430 target price (DCF) and note EPS growth forecasts of 33% in 2025 and 14% in 2026 despite modest gross‑margin pressure from promotions.

title: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk posts 75% QoQ net profit rise in Q3 2025 despite margin pressure and outlines expansion plans
body: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk reported Q3 2025 net profit of $14.9 million, up 75.5% quarter‑on‑quarter, with revenue of $63 million (9% QoQ) driven by gold sales of $61 million, while operating profit and EBITDA fell 13.5% and 10.5% due to higher costs and a royalty increase to 15.7%; nine‑month net profit reached $37.9 million, up 142.2% YoY. The firm plans to expand its carbon‑in‑leach plant capacity from 500 to 2,000 t/day by early 2027, and analysts from Bri Danareksa, Samuel and Maybank issued buy or hold recommendations with target prices of Rp1,080, Rp1,300 and Rp950 respectively, citing strong gold and silver sales but warning of margin pressure and gold‑price volatility.

title: PT Petrindo Jaya Kreasi Tbk secretary Robertus Maylando Siahaya increases stake in Cuan to 480,200 shares
body: On 12 November 2025, Robertus Maylando Siahaya purchased 140,000 Cuan shares at Rp2,180 each, spending Rp305.2 million and raising his holding to 480,200 shares (0,0043%). The transaction was disclosed as a direct investment, while Cuan's share price closed at Rp2,190, down 0.45% on the day after an 85.59% year‑to‑date gain.

title: Indonesia Stock Exchange suspends trading of PT Puri Sentul Permai Tbk after sharp price surge
body: BEI halted PT Puri Sentul Permai Tbk’s shares on Thursday for an estimated one‑day, first‑stage suspension due to abnormal price movement. Prior to the halt, the stock had jumped 24.35% to Rp286, reaching the upper auto‑rejection limit, and had risen 81.01% over the week, 148.70% in a month, 136.36% quarter‑on‑quarter, and 138.33% year‑to‑date.

title: Jung Ho Han acquires 11.78 million shares of bank kb indonesia, raising his ownership to 0.0000627%
body: On 10 November 2025, Jung Ho Han purchased 11,776,400 shares of bank kb indonesia at Rp68 per share, costing approximately Rp800.79 million. The transaction brings his holding to 11.77 million shares, representing 0,0000627% of the company, and was described by company secretary Ariz Dian Perkasa as an internal investment purpose.

title: Sreeya Sewu posts a net profit of IDR 5.08 billion for the quarter ended 30 September 2025, a 137% year‑on‑year swing from a loss.
body: The company recorded net profit of IDR 5.08 billion and basic EPS of IDR 2.76, reversing a loss of IDR 13.6 billion and negative EPS of IDR 7.40 in the same quarter last year. Net sales fell 1.75% to IDR 3.93 trillion, while gross profit rose to IDR 355.02 billion and total assets slightly decreased to IDR 3.02 trillion.

title: Starlight Ltd acquires 10% stake in MNC Energy Investments through 3.13 billion-share purchase
body: On 11 November 2025, Starlight Ltd, assisted by mnc sekuritas, bought 3,136,444,591 shares of mnc energy investments, representing a 10.03% ownership and an estimated transaction value of about Rp222.68 billion based on the share price of Rp71. Other shareholders holding more than 5% are mnc asia holding (24.21%), karya pacific (39.39%) and the public (36.39%).

title: Elcorps to Secure IDR 75 billion from Asset Sale to Karya Utama Putra Mandiri
body: Elcorps (zatta jaya alias elcorps) will receive IDR 75 billion by selling a 14,579 m² land parcel and a 14,862 m² building to Karya Utama Putra Mandiri, a non‑affiliated jewelry production company. The proceeds are intended to strengthen cash, support operations and business development, and reduce debt, pending shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting on 19 December 2025.

title: Investor Lo Kheng Hong expands stake in intiland to 6.66% after November 2025 share purchase
body: Lo Kheng Hong increased his holding in intiland by buying an additional 3,659,800 shares on 11 November 2025, bringing his total to 690.07 million shares (6.66% of the company) valued at approximately Rp479.43 million. The transaction was executed through several securities firms and followed a recent 8.39% price correction that lowered intiland's share price to Rp131.

title: PT PIMSf Pulogadung expands control of PT GeoPrima Solusi Tbk with 45.45% stake and launches mandatory tender offer for the remaining shares
body: PT PIMSf Pulogadung now holds 45.45% of PT GeoPrima Solusi Tbk and has committed to a mandatory tender offer for the remaining 54.55% (363,707,303 shares) at Rp436 per share, up to Rp158.58 billion, with the MTO period running from 11 Nov to 11 Dec 2025. The company also highlighted plans to create synergies, citing asset valuation of Rp200 billion and average annual revenue of Rp150 billion.

title: PT Bukit Asam secures Rp3.55 trillion senior term facility from state-owned banks to fund rail‑linked coal handling projects
body: On 12 November 2025, PT Bukit Asam signed a five‑year Rp3.55 trillion senior term facility with Bank Mandiri, Bank Rakyat Indonesia and Bank Negara Indonesia to finance a coal handling facility and train loading stations 6 & 7, aiming to boost rail transport capacity by 20 million tonnes per year with commercial operations expected in Q2 2026. Under OJK regulations the transaction is classified as an affiliate but not material, as the amount represents less than 20 % of the company’s equity (Rp22.64 trillion as of 31 Dec 2024 and Rp20.77 trillion as of 30 Sep 2025).

title: JSI Sinergi Mas Completes Acquisition of 51% Stake in Leyand Internasional Tbk
body: JSI Sinergi Mas has acquired a 51% stake in Leyand Internasional Tbk, equivalent to 2,022,838,500 shares, at a price of Rp10 per share, totaling Rp20.23 billion. The acquisition is part of JSI's business expansion plan, and the company is preparing for further processes, including a mandatory tender offer, to comply with regulatory requirements.

title: Michael Increases Stake in Petrindo Jaya Kreasi with Purchase of 140,000 Shares
body: Michael, the CEO of Petrindo Jaya Kreasi, has acquired 140,000 shares of the company, increasing his total holdings to 480,200 shares, or 0,00427% of the company, with a total investment of Rp305.2 million. The purchase was made at a price of Rp2,180 per share, with the goal of investing and directly owning the shares.

title: Happy Hapsoro Reduces Stake in Rukun Raharja by Selling 25.51 Million Shares
body: Happy Hapsoro has sold 25.51 million shares of Rukun Raharja, with the transaction valued at approximately Rp127.55 billion based on the closing price of Rp5,000 per share on November 11, 2025. The sale, conducted through both direct and indirect schemes, reduces Happy Hapsoro's direct stake to 27.93% and indirect stake to 35.77%.

title: PT TBS Energi Utama Tbk is undergoing a major transformation to become a green energy company, allocating $600 million for clean energy projects over the next five years.
body: PT TBS Energi Utama Tbk is shifting its business focus from coal to green energy, with plans to allocate $600 million for clean energy projects, including energy management, new and renewable energy, and electric vehicles. The company aims to position itself as a leading clean energy company in Indonesia, supporting the government's net zero emission target by 2060.

title: Barito Pacific Denies Plans to List Griya Idola in IPO and Dismisses Rumors of Acquiring Nusa Raya Cipta
body: Barito Pacific, a company owned by Prajogo Pangestu, has denied plans to list its property unit, Griya Idola, in an initial public offering (IPO). The company also dismissed rumors of acquiring Nusa Raya Cipta, a construction company, and stated that its focus will remain on organic and inorganic expansion in the energy, petrochemical, and infrastructure sectors.

title: PT Atlantis Subsea Indonesia Tbk's Director of Main Commissioner Rudi Reksa Sutantra Conducts Buy and Sell Transactions
body: Rudi Reksa Sutantra, the main commissioner and controller of PT Atlantis Subsea Indonesia Tbk, conducted two transactions on November 7 and 11, selling 8.9 million shares and buying 4.7 million shares, respectively. The total value of the transactions amounts to approximately IDR 862 million.

title: PT Shield Service Tbk. Announces Merger Plans with PT Alsok Bass Indonesia Security Services
body: PT Shield Service Tbk plans to merge with PT Alsok Bass Indonesia Security Services, aiming to achieve operational efficiency, resource optimization, and increased productivity. The merger is expected to be completed by April 1, 2026, with a target free float of 7.5% to be achieved in Q1 2026.

title: Antam gold bar prices rise by IDR 29,000 per gram on Thursday
body: On Thursday, the price of 1‑gram gold from PT Aneka Tambang Tbk increased to IDR 2,396,000, up IDR 29,000 from Wednesday, and the buyback price rose to IDR 2,261,000, also up IDR 29,000. The article notes that per‑gram prices differ by bar size due to printing costs, with the 1‑kilogram bar used as the market benchmark.

title: PT XLSMART Telecom Sejahtera Tbk posts strong Q3 2025 results post‑merger, with revenue up 38% and net profit soaring 288%
body: PT XLSMART Telecom Sejahtera Tbk reported Q3 2025 revenue of IDR 11.47 trillion (+38% YoY), normalized net profit of IDR 1.15 trillion (+288% YoY) and EBITDA of IDR 5.40 trillion (+24% YoY) at a 47% margin, with data and digital services contributing 89.7% of revenue and cumulative nine‑month revenue reaching IDR 30.57 trillion. The company said its subscriber base hit 79.6 million (ARPU IDR 39 k), net debt stood at IDR 22.5 trillion (net‑debt/EBITDA 3.27x), free cash flow rose 23% to IDR 9.41 trillion, it targets $150‑200 million of post‑integration synergies in 2025, and its shares slipped 1.45% to IDR 2,710.

title: PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama Buy Shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama bought shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSS.JK) at approximately IDR 375, increasing holdings from 7,330,108,800 to 7,572,291,100. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

body: According to the published announcement, Vikram Sinha bought shares of PT Indosat Tbk (ISAT.JK) at approximately IDR 2,106, increasing holdings from 8,800,500 to 11,170,700. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

body: According to the published announcement, Astra International Tbk sold shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSS.JK), decreasing holdings from 3,263,822,361 to 2,681,027,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

body: According to the published announcement, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama bought shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSS.JK) at approximately IDR 375, increasing holdings from 7,572,291,100 to 8,233,839,800. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

body: According to the published announcement, Patrick Rudolf Dannacher bought shares of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk. (CYBR.JK) at approximately IDR 1,342, increasing holdings from 188,196,822 to 189,143,622. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Astra International Tbk Sell Shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Astra International Tbk sold shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSS.JK), decreasing holdings from 3,263,822,361 to 2,681,027,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Astra International Tbk Buy Shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk

title: Indonesia to Cut Coal Production in 2026 to Stabilize Global Prices After Oversupply
body: Indonesia will reduce coal production volume in 2026 to stabilize international coal prices, following an oversupply in 2024 where production reached 836 million tons, exceeding the 710 million tons target. The benchmark coal price dropped to US $103.75 per ton in early November 2025 from US $114.43 in November 2024, prompting the government to consider reducing export quotas and potentially increasing the domestic market obligation above 25 percent.

body: According to the published announcement, Budi Gunawan bought shares of PT Guna Timur Raya Tbk (TRUK.JK) at approximately IDR 100, increasing holdings from 14,990,000 to 21,750,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Jordan's King Offers danantara Three Major Investment Projects
body: Jordan's King Abdullah II offered danantara, Indonesia's sovereign wealth fund, three major investment project opportunities covering gas pipeline development, toll road construction, and the logistics sector. danantara CEO Rosan Roeslani confirmed that these proposals will be followed up with an in-depth evaluation by a technical team under President Prabowo's direction.

title: PT Guna Timur Raya Tbk Sell Shares of PT Guna Timur Raya Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Guna Timur Raya Tbk sold shares of PT Guna Timur Raya Tbk (TRUK.JK) at approximately IDR 100, decreasing holdings from 279,060,000 to 174,734,700. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia's transport ministry readies special operations and incentives for year‑end travel surge
body: The ministry has activated a dedicated air‑transport observation centre and a special operation plan to manage an expected surge of 286 jet and 40 propeller flights between Dec 18, 2025 and Jan 4, 2026, with 560 aircraft (366 ready, 194 under maintenance) from carriers including lion air, garuda indonesia and its subsidiary wings air. It also announced fare‑discount measures—VAT, airport‑service and fuel subsidies—for tickets bought Oct 22, 2025 to Jan 10, 2026, covering flights from Dec 22, 2025 to Jan 10, 2026.

title: Indonesia transport minister says ALFI's support is crucial for achieving zero ODOL policy by 2027
body: At the Indonesia Logistic Outlook 2026 conference in Tangerang, Transport Minister Dudy Purwagandhi emphasized that the success of the zero over‑dimension over‑loading (ODOL) policy slated for 1 January 2027 will depend on the Indonesian Logistics Forwarders Association (ALFI), which represents over 4,000 logistics companies. The government has formed a special task team to revise Road Traffic Law No. 22/2009 and accelerate implementation to ensure a safer, more efficient logistics ecosystem.

title: Indonesia cuts subsidized fertilizer prices by 20% to boost farmer productivity
body: Agriculture Minister Andi Amran Sulaiman announced a 20% reduction in subsidized urea and NPK fertilizer prices, which has already lifted fertilizer purchases by 20% this month. The government also simplified the redemption process, limited subsidies to smallholders with up to two hectares, and revoked licenses of distributors that violated regulations.

title: Bali Tightens Foreign Investment Rules to Protect Local Businesses and Land
body: Governor Wayan Koster announced new regulations requiring foreign investments to exceed Rp10 billion, avoid MSME sectors and agricultural land, and enforce stricter tax compliance after noting foreigners taking over rentals and other local enterprises. Deputy Minister Todotua Pasaribu said the central government will help revoke non‑compliant permits and establish a dedicated service desk for investors.

title: Bank Indonesia promotes QRIS adoption on Belakang Padang Island, highlighting rapid growth in Riau Islands digital payments
body: Bank Indonesia held an event on Belakang Padang Island, Batam, to promote the QRIS digital payment method, emphasizing the island’s role as a cross‑border digital‑economy hub with Singapore. QRIS transactions in the Riau Islands rose to 64.9 million by September 2025, a 117.3 % increase from 2024, with a transaction value of Rp7.7 trillion.

title: PT Bisnis Bersama Berkah shares jump 10.29% on market speculation over planned corporate action with Triple B
body: The stock of PT Bisnis Bersama Berkah closed up 10.29% at Rp150 per share, marking a 118.46% gain over the past six months, after the company announced a collaboration with Triple B and appointed it as lead advisor for a corporate action slated for 2026. Analysts warned the rally is sentiment‑driven and urged investors to await official details on the action’s structure before making further investment decisions.

title: Indonesia's technology sector index surges 158% YTD, boosted by PT DCI Indonesia, PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi and merger/IPO news
body: The IDX Technology sector index has risen 157.96% year‑to‑date, led by PT DCI Indonesia Tbk’s 521.85% gain and PT Elang Mahkota Teknologi Tbk’s 150% rise to Rp1,230, giving EMTK a market cap of Rp75.51 trillion. Analysts cite the pending Superbank IPO and the proposed PT Goto Gojek Tokopedia Tbk‑Grab merger as additional catalysts, while noting the supportive environment from Bank Indonesia’s recent interest‑rate cuts.

title: Indonesian market eyes BI rate decision as analysts split on potential cut and its impact on IHSG, banking and property stocks
body: Analysts from BRI Danareksa expect Bank Indonesia to cut the policy rate to 4.50% on Nov 19, which could boost foreign inflows and support the IHSG, while Mirae Asset forecasts the rate will be held steady due to rupiah pressure and a delayed Fed easing. Both see the index trading near the 8,315‑8,355 support zone with resistance around 8,440‑8,480, and highlight banking (BBCA, BBRI, BMRI, BTPS) and property (CTRA, BSDE, PWON) stocks as the most sensitive to any monetary move.

title: Indonesia's IHSG projected to weaken amid expectations of a BI rate cut and US policy cues, with analysts noting technical downside and stock-specific targets
body: Analysts from Kiwoom Sekuritas Indonesia and MNC Sekuritas expect the Jakarta Composite Index (IHSG) to trade lower, citing a weakening MACD, potential death cross, and declining RSI, while market sentiment hinges on a likely 25‑basis‑point BI rate cut to 4.5% and upcoming FOMC minutes after the US government shutdown. They identify support around 8,305‑8,338 and resistance near 8,442‑8,450, and highlight price targets for PT Essa Industries Indonesia Tbk (Rp710‑760), PT Sawit Sumbermas Sarana Tbk (Rp1,570‑1,690), PT Wintermar Offshore Marine Tbk (Rp454‑478) and speculative‑buy ranges for PT Aneka Tambang Tbk (Rp2,870‑3,280) and PT Barito Pacific Tbk (Rp3,620‑4,120).

title: Indonesia’s ST015 Sukuk Tabungan sells over IDR 1 trillion, with the 2‑year tenor capturing more than 70% of orders
body: The ST015 sukuk offering, launched on Monday and running until 3 December 2025, provides a floating‑floor yield of 5.20% for the 2‑year ST015T2 and 5.45% for the 4‑year ST015T4, with a minimum investment of IDR 1 million. Strong demand from retail and syariah investors—driven by a premium over benchmark yields, low tax and flexible reinvestment—has led Bibit and BCA to report sales exceeding IDR 1 trillion, most of which are for the 2‑year tenor.

title: Sillo Maritime Reports 17.4% Decline in Net Profit to USD13.12 Million for FY 2025
body: For the year ended September 30, 2025, Sillo Maritime posted a net profit of USD13.12 million, down 17.43% from USD15.89 million in the same period last year, while revenue fell 4.6% to USD127.91 million and operating profit dropped to USD29.36 million amid higher SG&A and interest expenses.

title: Itama Ranoraya Reports 122% YoY Profit Surge in First Nine Months of 2025
body: Itama Ranoraya posted net profit of Rp44.6 billion for the first nine months of 2025, up 122.22% from Rp20.07 billion in the same period last year, while net sales rose 74.13% to Rp1.01 trillion. Gross profit increased to Rp185.51 billion, and earnings per share rose to Rp29.58 from Rp13.32, amid higher cost of sales and interest expenses.

title: Dos Ni Roha Secures 30‑Day Extension of PKPU Status Until December 13, 2025
body: The court granted Dos Ni Roha’s subsidiary, Dosni Roha Indonesia, a 30‑day extension of its permanent PKPU status, effective from November 13 to December 13, 2025, following a lawsuit filed by QNB Indonesia. The company will continue restructuring its obligations and communicating with creditors during this period.

title: Archi Indonesia Expands into Geothermal Power via Toka Tidning Geothermal Joint Venture with Ormat Geothermal Indonesia
body: Archi Indonesia, through its joint venture PT TTG with Ormat Geothermal Indonesia, has secured a geothermal permit for a 40 MW plant in Pinasungkulan, Bitung, and plans further environmental approvals and exploration to confirm resource size. Meanwhile, the company aims to hit a 25% gold production target by end‑2025 while expanding mining operations and improving gold recovery.

title: PT Multi Sarana Nasional Increases Stake in PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk Through Two Large Purchases
body: On November 12, 2025, PT Multi Sarana Nasional bought 38,788,300 shares of PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk in two transactions, raising its ownership from 19.26% to 19.71% and spending roughly Rp12.43 billion. The shares closed up 5.66% at Rp336, above the purchase price, according to corporate secretary Antoninus Limbong.

title: PT Kedoya Adyaraya Tbk Secures Rp200 Billion Credit Facility from PT Bank CIMB Niaga Tbk, Extending to Related Entities
body: PT Kedoya Adyaraya Tbk has increased its credit facility with PT Bank CIMB Niaga Tbk to Rp200 billion, covering direct uncommitted and extra revolving lines, and the same facility is extended to PT Sarana Meditama Metropolitan Tbk and its affiliated entities. The transaction, backed by Rp150 billion in building‑use‑rights collateral, is classified as an affiliate transaction but does not constitute a conflict of interest under regulation.

title: PT Guna Timur Raya Tbk experiences significant internal stock transactions on Friday, November 14, 2025, involving its controlling shareholder, director, and commissioner.
body: PT Guna Makmur Raya, the controlling shareholder of PT Guna Timur Raya Tbk, sold 104,325,300 shares at Rp100 per share, while Director Budi Gunawan and Commissioner Carolina Kusuma purchased 6,760,000 and 10,565,300 shares, respectively, at the same price, resulting in a total transaction value of Rp12.16 billion. The transactions led to a significant increase in the company's stock price, which rose by 24.75% to Rp494 per share.

title: Directors of PT Champ Resto Indonesia Tbk Purchase Company Shares, Citing Optimism in the Restaurant Industry
body: The directors of PT Champ Resto Indonesia Tbk, Christopher Supit and Hendrik Alexander W. Mboi, have purchased company shares, with Christopher Supit now owning 421,000 shares and Hendrik Alexander W. Mboi owning 354,600 shares, in a transaction valued at approximately Rp440.51 million. The purchase reflects their optimism in the restaurant industry, which they believe has potential for growth and will yield positive results for investors in the future.

title: Elang Mahkota Teknologi Tbk Buy Shares of Surya Citra Media Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Elang Mahkota Teknologi Tbk bought shares of Surya Citra Media Tbk (SCMA.JK) at approximately IDR 360, increasing holdings from 49,968,319,840 to 52,068,319,840. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

body: According to the published announcement, Phillip Tjipto bought shares of PT Impack Pratama Industri Tbk (IMPC.JK) at approximately IDR 2,600, increasing holdings from 158,750,000 to 159,500,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

body: According to the published announcement, Patrick Rudolf Dannacher bought shares of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk. (CYBR.JK) at approximately IDR 1,343, increasing holdings from 189,143,622 to 191,129,122. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.
