body: According to the published announcement, PT Rajati Alia Kapital sold shares of PT Era Digital Media Tbk (AWAN.JK) at approximately IDR 250, decreasing holdings from 2,684,750,000 to 2,656,136,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT MNC Asia Holding Tbk Sell Shares of PT MNC Energy Investments Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT MNC Asia Holding Tbk sold shares of PT MNC Energy Investments Tbk (IATA.JK) at approximately IDR 88, decreasing holdings from 7,571,473,566 to 5,891,473,566. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Junita Ciputra Buy Shares of Metropolitan Kentjana Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Junita Ciputra bought shares of Metropolitan Kentjana Tbk (MKPI.JK) at approximately IDR 24,000, increasing holdings from 74,856,027 to 74,857,527. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT perusahaan gas negara Tbk advances biomethane injection project in South Sumatra
body: PT perusahaan gas negara Tbk, a natural gas sub‑holding of the state‑owned oil‑gas group pertamina, is developing a biomethane injection point at Pagardewa to feed renewable natural gas from palm‑oil‑mill waste into the PT pgn network, targeting about 1.2 bbtud of supply. The project is expected to cut greenhouse‑gas emissions by up to 204,867 tons CO₂e per year and generate new revenue for the company.

title: PT Archi Indonesia Tbk forms 95% Ormat-owned JV PT Toka Tindung Geothermal to develop a 40 MW geothermal project in Bitung
body: Archi Indonesia Tbk announced a joint venture with Ormat Geothermal Indonesia, creating PT Toka Tindung Geothermal in which Archi holds 5% and Ormat 95%, to develop a geothermal power project within Archi’s mining concessions (PT Meares Soputan Mining and PT Tambang Tondano Nusajaya). The project, targeting 40 MW capacity in Bitung city, has secured a geothermal permit in June 2025 and is expected to boost the sustainability of Archi’s subsidiaries and generate geothermal electricity sales.

title: Indonesia's Energy Minister urges PT Freeport Indonesia to buy copper concentrate from PT Amman Mineral Nusa Tenggara amid smelter disruptions
body: Minister Bahlil Lahadalia asked PT Freeport Indonesia to engage in B2B talks to purchase PT Amman Mineral Nusa Tenggara's copper concentrate for processing at Freeport's smelter at an economical price, but said the ministry cannot intervene further as it is a business matter. PT Amman Mineral has received an export recommendation for up to 480,000 dry metric tons of concentrate valid until April 2026, while both companies face smelter shutdowns due to a force majeure at Amman's plant and a mudslide at Freeport's Grasberg block cave mine.

title: Sun Energy expands green‑mining solutions for Indonesia’s mining sector
body: Sun Energy, as a strategic partner, has deployed solar power plants and containerized battery energy storage systems at more than 15 Indonesian mining sites, including the first integrated solar‑BESS project with PT Cipta Kridatama and a solar PV roll‑up at PT Berau Coal. The company says the shift from diesel generators—currently accounting for up to 50% of operating costs in off‑grid mines—to solar‑plus‑storage will cut fuel consumption and lower energy logistics expenses.

title: PT Bukit Asam Tbk Secures IDR 3.56 trillion Loans to Expand Coal Transport Capacity by 20 Million Tons per Year
body: PT Bukit Asam Tbk obtained loan facilities totaling IDR 3.56 trillion from PT Bank Mandiri Tbk, PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk, and PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk to fund the Tanjung Enim–Kramasan coal handling and train loading project. The development is expected to raise the company's coal transport capacity by 20 million tons annually, strengthening its role in national energy security.

title: PT Bumi Resources Tbk Pays Down Payment for 45% Stake in PT Laman Mining and Outlines Alumina Expansion Plans
body: PT Bumi Resources Tbk has paid a $20 million down‑payment toward a $59.1 million acquisition of a 45 % stake in PT Laman Mining, with the remaining $39.1 million to be paid after conditions precedent are satisfied and the transaction slated to close by 30 October 2026. The deal, part of Bumi’s diversification into bauxite and alumina, includes a planned $1.5 billion alumina plant and has been welcomed by analysts who expect it to boost non‑coal EBITDA and reduce reliance on thermal coal.

title: PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk expands production with the Tujuh Bukit underground mine and Pani Gold project amid rising gold price expectations
body: PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk. is developing the Tujuh Bukit underground mine, targeting 110,000 tons of copper and 350,000 ounces of gold annually, while its subsidiary PT Merdeka Gold Resources Tbk. advances the Pani Gold project, which is 83% complete and slated to start operations in Q1 2026; PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk. is arranging a $600 million syndicated loan for its own underground mining initiatives. Market analysts note that these expansions could boost production capacity, asset portfolios and average selling prices, especially as Goldman Sachs projects gold prices could reach $5,000 per ounce by 2026.

title: BPJPH pledges to boost Indonesia’s halal ecosystem with free certifications to support President Prabowo’s development agenda
body: BPJPH head Ahmad Haikal Hasan said the agency will allocate one million free halal certifications this year to help MSMEs access global markets, improve product quality, and drive economic growth in line with President Prabowo Subianto’s agenda. He emphasized that expanding halal certification will strengthen the value chain, increase industrial competitiveness, and enhance community welfare.

title: Indonesia and the United States Near Finalizing Zero‑Tariff Agreement for Key Indonesian Commodities
body: Coordinating Minister Airlangga Hartarto said the Indonesia‑US tariff negotiations are in the final legal‑drafting stage, with zero‑percent tariffs to be applied to Indonesian crude palm oil, rubber, tea, coffee and related products, while textiles and footwear remain under discussion. The deal also includes Indonesia’s commitment to import $15 billion of US energy products and $4.5 billion of US agricultural goods, and a $10 billion US‑backed blue‑ammonia investment, aiming to restore the bilateral trade balance.

title: Indonesia drafts rule to let PT Pertamina buy U.S. energy without bidding as part of reciprocal tariff deal
body: The government is preparing a presidential regulation that permits state oil firm PT Pertamina to purchase energy from U.S. companies without a bidding process, linked to a broader U.S.–Indonesia tariff‑exemption negotiation covering commodities such as palm oil, rubber, tea and coffee. The agreement also commits Pertamina to increase U.S. energy imports to about US$15 billion and includes a US$10 billion investment in a blue‑ammonia facility, aiming to improve the trade balance.

title: Bumi Resources Minerals Assures No Impact from Gold Export Tax Due to Domestic Sales
body: bumi resources minerals confirmed that the government's planned 15% tax on gold exports will not affect its revenue, as 100 percent of PT citra palu minerals' gold and silver products are sold to the domestic market. PT citra palu minerals, a subsidiary operating mines in Sulawesi Tengah and Selatan, sells to domestic buyers including hartadinata abadi, PT simba jaya utama, and PT pegadaian galeri dua empat.

title: Indosat Ooredoo Hutchison Deploys AI‑Driven Anti‑Spam System Protecting 11.5 Million Customers Monthly
body: The telecom’s AI‑powered feature, launched on 7 August, has flagged 290 million risky calls and 90 million suspicious messages in three months, shielding an average of 11.5 million customers per month from fraud. The system operates at the network level as part of Indosat’s AIvolusi5G strategy and is offered across its IM3 and Tri brands with zero‑trust verification and digital‑literacy initiatives.

title: PT Rukun Raharja Tbk establishes PT Banawa Rezeki Optima as new subsidiary for management consulting and maritime transport
body: PT Rukun Raharja Tbk created PT Banawa Rezeki Optima, holding 99.99% of its shares (Rp57,749 billion capital), to operate in management consulting, holding activities, and domestic and international sea transport. The company said the subsidiary will not materially affect current finances but is expected to boost future performance, while the stock slipped 0.43% to Rp4,670 after recent gains of 4% month‑on‑month, 116% over six months and 65% year‑to‑date.

title: Karya Pacific Investama expands stake in MNC Energy Investments to 44.76% after buying 1.68 billion shares
body: Karya Pacific Investama acquired 1.68 billion shares of MNC Energy Investments at Rp88 per share, costing Rp147.84 billion and raising its holding to 13.99 billion shares (44.76%). The transaction, executed on 13 November 2025, reduced MNC Asia Holding’s stake to 5.89 billion shares (18.84%), while earlier on 11 November 2025 Starlight Ltd purchased 3.13 billion shares (10.03%) with assistance from MNC Sekuritas.

title: Polychem Indonesia decides to permanently close its polyester segment
body: Following a board decision on 14 November 2025, Polychem Indonesia will permanently shut down its polyester division, which has incurred consecutive losses due to global oversupply, import competition, and high raw‑material costs. The company states the closure will not adversely affect its overall financial condition, as the segment has been inactive since 2022 and all employee and customer obligations have been fulfilled.

title: Mitra Investindo raises IDR 60 billion through a private placement of 209,791,100 Series B shares
body: Mitra Investindo completed a private placement of 209,791,100 Series B shares at IDR 286 per share, attracting investors such as PT Samala Serasi Utama, PT Sumber Sari Rejeki, and PT Prime Asia Capital, and generating IDR 60 billion in tactical funds. The proceeds will fund capital expenditures, asset purchases, downstream silica mineral projects, and internal financing, increasing the company's paid‑up capital to IDR 241.52 billion and total shares to 3.75 billion, with the new shares listed on the IDX on 17 November 2025.

title: PT Carsurin Tbk. (CRSN) Restructures Leadership and Expands Business Scope Following RUPSLB.
body: PT Carsurin Tbk. (CRSN) held an Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders (RUPSLB) to approve changes in its Board of Commissioners and Directors and to add new business activities in oil and gas mining support. These strategic moves are part of a transformation and regeneration agenda aimed at strengthening business competitiveness and achieving sustainable growth, with CRSN shares rising 19.2% to Rp161 per share on Monday.

title: CEO Darmawan Suryadi SM Increases Shareholding in armada berjaya trans for Investment
body: Darmawan Suryadi SM, CEO of armada berjaya trans, acquired 9,397,100 shares of the company on November 10 and 14, 2025, for a total of Rp1.02 billion at prices ranging from Rp106-110 per share. This transaction increased his direct ownership in armada berjaya trans by 1.17% to 20.99% (167.65 million shares), with the stated purpose of investment.

title: panca global Plans Rights Issue and Warrant Offering to Fund Working Capital and Subsidiary Capital Injection
body: panca global intends to issue a maximum of 944.47 million shares through a rights issue and offer 944.47 million warrants, both with a nominal value of Rp100. The funds raised from both actions will be utilized for the company's working capital and to increase capital participation in its subsidiaries, pending investor approval at an Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders on December 24, 2025.

title: rajati alia kapital Divests 28.61 Million Shares of era digital media
body: rajati alia kapital divested 28.61 million shares of era digital media on November 12, 2025, at Rp250 per share, generating Rp7.15 billion. This transaction, priced 4.58% lower than the closing price, reduced rajati alia kapital's direct ownership in era digital media to 77.33% from 78.159%.

title: djasa ubersakti Plans Rp60 Billion Private Placement, Leading to New Controlling Shareholder
body: djasa ubersakti plans a Rp60 billion private placement by issuing 3.75 billion new shares at Rp16 each, with **penajam makmur jaya** acquiring all shares to become the new controlling shareholder with 71.43% ownership. The funds will be used for debt repayment, including Rp10 billion to **bpd kaltim kaltara**, and working capital, pending investor approval on December 24, 2025.

title: Elang Mahkota Increases Stake in Surya Citra Media to 70.39% After Rp755 Billion Share Accumulation
body: Elang Mahkota (Emtek) acquired an additional 2.1 billion shares of Surya Citra Media on November 11 and 14, 2025, for a total of Rp755 billion, raising its ownership to 70.39% from 67.55%. Concurrently, Surya Citra Media will distribute an interim dividend of Rp9 per share, totaling Rp571.2 billion, with payment scheduled for December 9, 2025.

title: PT Intan Baru Prana Tbk. Records Significant Revenue Growth and Expands Heavy Equipment Rental Partnerships
body: PT Intan Baru Prana Tbk. (IBFN) reported a significant revenue increase to Rp143.79 billion by September 2025, an 807% rise from Rp15.85 billion in the same period of 2024. The company is expanding its heavy equipment rental business, forging partnerships with companies like PT Darma Henwa Tbk, PT Mitra Stania Prima, and PT Petrosea Tbk, despite facing challenges from intense competition and operational costs.

title: PT dms propertindo Tbk's Subsidiary, PT accola hotel indonesia, Partners with pusat pengelolaan komplek kemayoran for 15-Year Kemayoran Golf Area Development
body: On November 14, 2025, PT accola hotel indonesia, a subsidiary of PT dms propertindo Tbk, signed a 15-year agreement with pusat pengelolaan komplek kemayoran to develop and manage the 466,600 m² Kemayoran Golf area. This collaboration involves a Rp218 billion investment for revitalization and facility development, including a new hotel, aiming to create an integrated leisure destination in Jakarta.

title: PT summarecon agung Tbk. (SMRA) Increases Capital in Two Subsidiaries Through Controlled Company
body: On November 13, 2025, PT summarecon agung Tbk. (SMRA) injected a total of Rp231.8 billion in capital into two subsidiaries, PT serpong cipta kreasi (Rp139.1 billion) and PT lestari kreasi (Rp92.7 billion), through its controlled company PT serpong cipta lestari. Corporate Secretary SMRA, Lydia Tjio, stated that this capital increase is part of strengthening the subsidiaries' capital structure and will not impact SMRA's operational activities, legal standing, financial condition, or business continuity.

title: PT Raharja Energi Cepu Tbk (RATU) Reports Increased Net Profit Despite Revenue Drop in Q3 2025
body: PT Raharja Energi Cepu Tbk (RATU) recorded a 28.1% increase in net profit to USD11.76 million by Q3 2025, despite a 12.97% decline in revenue to USD37.61 million, primarily due to a significant 31.64% reduction in cost of revenue. Concurrently, the company's total assets grew by 25.5% to USD66.31 million, while liabilities decreased by 19.7% to USD20.42 million by September 2025.

title: PT Central Omega Resources Tbk. Receives OJK Administrative Sanction, Assures No Direct Business Impact Amidst Stock Fluctuations
body: PT Central Omega Resources Tbk. received an administrative sanction from Otoritas Jasa Keuangan (OJK) for violating rules on the transfer of repurchased shares, though Director Feni Silviani Budiman stated it has no direct impact on the company's operations or financial condition. Despite a 5.81% drop on Monday, DKFT shares have seen significant gains of 7.2% in the last month, 112% in six months, and 282% year-to-date.

title: PT surya toto indonesia Tbk to Distribute Rp103.2 Billion Interim Cash Dividend for 2025
body: PT surya toto indonesia Tbk announced an interim cash dividend of Rp103.2 billion, equivalent to Rp10 per share, for the 2025 financial year, with the decision approved on November 13, 2025, and a cum dividend date set for November 27, 2025. This distribution is based on a net profit of Rp257.40 billion as of September 30, 2025, and shares of ZYRX saw a 1.31 percent increase to Rp155 per share on Monday.

title: PT elpi trans cargo, a Subsidiary of PT pelayaran nasional ekalya purnamasari Tbk., Secures Rp76.8 Billion Investment Credit from bank mandiri balikpapan
body: PT elpi trans cargo, a subsidiary of PT pelayaran nasional ekalya purnamasari Tbk., obtained an Rp76.8 billion investment credit facility from bank mandiri balikpapan on November 14, 2025, with a 96-month term. This funding is fully allocated for the procurement of two new tugboats and two new barges, a move expected to positively impact etc's operational and financial performance by strengthening its owned fleet.

title: PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama Buy Shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama bought shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSS.JK) at approximately IDR 375, increasing holdings from 8,233,839,800 to 8,372,229,800. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Buana Graha Utama Buy Shares of Multi Indocitra Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Buana Graha Utama bought shares of Multi Indocitra Tbk (MICE.JK) at approximately IDR 626, increasing holdings from 286,425,618 to 286,717,018. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Ubs Group Ag Buy Shares of Bumi Resources Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Ubs Group Ag bought shares of Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI.JK) at approximately IDR 182,199, increasing holdings from 25,976,928,863 to 28,265,080,463. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Ignatius Edy Suhardaya Buy Shares of Mitra Investindo Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Ignatius Edy Suhardaya bought shares of Mitra Investindo Tbk (MITI.JK) at approximately IDR 286, increasing holdings from 10,500,000 to 13,996,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Multi Sarana Nasional Buy Shares of PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Multi Sarana Nasional bought shares of PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk (HATM.JK) at approximately IDR 334, increasing holdings from 1,710,625,861 to 1,738,909,861. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Hexindo Adiperkasa Tbk launches new Hitachi excavators and an electric dump truck at IEE Series 2025 to strengthen its one‑stop solution in the competitive mining equipment market
body: At the two‑week IEE Series 2025 exhibition, Hexindo Adiperkasa Tbk unveiled the Hitachi ZX550LC‑PG, ZX350LC‑7G excavators and the Foton EV electric dump truck, highlighting features such as high‑capacity material handling, remote service technology and green‑energy capability. The company emphasized its strategy of offering competitive pricing, comprehensive after‑sales service, financing partnerships and product innovation to meet growing post‑pandemic mining demand in Indonesia.

title: Prof. Budi Mulyanto urges creation of a single national board to streamline Indonesia’s palm‑oil regulations
body: At the 2025 Indonesian Palm Oil Conference, Budi highlighted that Indonesia’s 16.8 million‑hectare plantations generate $30‑40 billion annually but are hampered by overlapping regulations from over 30 ministries, legal uncertainty for smallholders, and fragmented data. He proposed a national special board to provide one map, one data, and one authority, aiming for legal certainty, policy stability, sustainability, social justice and stronger downstream development.

title: Analysts warn Indonesia's forestry task force could curb palm oil output and raise global prices
body: Analysts Dorab Mistry (Godrej International Ltd) and Thomas Mielke (Oil World) said the recent forestry enforcement task force, which has seized millions of hectares of oil‑palm land, will likely keep Indonesia’s palm‑oil production stagnant this year and limit growth to 1‑1.5 million tons over the next two years without new plantation licenses. Mielke further projects a production decline in 2026‑2027 if more than 3 million hectares are taken, which could tighten global vegetable‑oil supplies and push export prices higher, especially for key buyer India.

title: Indonesia Announces 600,000‑ha Expansion of Palm Oil Plantations to Boost Output
body: The government plans to add 600,000 hectares of new palm oil land over four years—400,000 hectares for smallholder “plasma” farms and 200,000 hectares for the state firm Palmco—potentially raising production by about 2 million tonnes, while stating no forest clearing will occur. The move follows the lapse of a moratorium and coincides with a planned increase in the mandatory biodiesel blend to 50% next year.

title: Batam Secures $10.35 Billion in Investment Commitments at Singapore Forum
body: At the Second Edition Islands Growth forum in Singapore, Batam’s investment authority announced $10.35 billion in preliminary commitments from 20 multinational firms across energy, advanced manufacturing, maritime and logistics sectors. The authority also launched the "Batam – Your Best Friend Invest" initiative and introduced new regulations (GR 25/2025 and PP 47/2025) to streamline permits and expand the free‑trade zone, highlighting Batam’s strategic proximity to Singapore and its green industrial projects such as the Tembesi floating solar power plan.

title: PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk launches Rp 550 billion Sustainable Bond II Stage III offering
body: PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk announced a Sustainable Bond II Stage III issuance of Rp 550 billion, split into Series A (5‑year, 8% coupon) and Series B (7‑year, 8.5% coupon), with Rp 50 billion fully guaranteed and Rp 500 billion best‑effort guaranteed, rated IDA by pefindo and underwritten by PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk and PT Sinarmas Sekuritas. The bond sale, scheduled for 1‑2 December with allocation on 3 December, will be used to refinance existing Sustainable Bond I debt, bringing total Sustainable Bond II issuance to Rp 1.75 trillion if fully subscribed.

title: Jakarta Stock Index Falls 0.65% as Analysts Highlight Technical Setups for MEDC, BRMS and ULTJ
body: The IDX composite closed at 8,361.92, down 54.95 points, while MEDC slipped 2.33% to Rp1,260, BRMS held at Rp930 and may test its MA50 after breaking MA20, and ULTJ rose 2.1% to Rp1,460 with buying volume but constrained by MA20; analysts note a three‑black‑crows pattern with weakening RSI and suggest buying around Rp1,240‑1,245.

title: Indonesia's KUR program disburses Rp238.7 trillion, achieving 83% of 2025 target, says MSME Minister
body: MSME Minister Maman Abdurrahman told a House Commission VII meeting that as of 15 November 2025, KUR disbursement reached Rp238.7 trillion, or 83.2% of the Rp286.61 trillion annual target, with about 4 million borrowers. He added that 60.7% of the funds were allocated to the production sector and 1.32 million borrowers graduated to higher financing categories, surpassing the target, while warning that some banks still request collateral for loans under Rp100 million despite regulations prohibiting it.

title: PT ElNusa Tbk reports 9% revenue growth in Q3 2025 while net profit declines, with analysts maintaining buy recommendations
body: For the January‑September 2025 period, PT ElNusa Tbk posted revenue of IDR 10.4 trillion, up 9% YoY, but net profit fell 4.5% to IDR 526.6 billion; growth was driven by all business segments, especially energy distribution and logistics (58% of revenue) and integrated upstream services (31%). Analysts note margin pressure and oil‑price volatility but project 2025 revenue of IDR 13.9 trillion and net profit of IDR 763 billion, keeping a buy rating with target prices around IDR 575‑590 per share.

title: Indonesia's IHSG slides 0.65% amid global weakness and upcoming BI rate decision
body: The Jakarta Composite Index (IHSG) slipped 0.65% to 8,361.93 on Tuesday, pressured by global market weakness, a weakening rupiah and profit‑taking, while investors await the Bank Indonesia meeting where the policy rate is expected to stay at 4.75%. Analyst Alrich Paskalis Tambolang highlighted dominant sell pressure, possible MACD and Stochastic RSI death‑crosses and a support zone near 8,300‑8,325, noting that Darma Henwa plans a 1.66 trillion‑rupiah external buyback and recommending solid‑fundamental stocks such as tpia, cpin, isat, bbri and ultj.

title: Indonesia's IHSG Expected to Rebound After Recent Dip, Analyst Highlights Support Zone and Buy Recommendations
body: The Jakarta Composite Index fell 0.65% to 8,361.93, but analyst William Hartanto expects a rebound, noting the index remains within the 8,300‑8,400 support‑resistance range and that the correction is healthy. He recommends buying PT Indah Kiat Pulp & Paper Tbk, PT Elnusa Tbk, PT AKR Corporindo Tbk and PT Bank Mandiri Tbk based on constructive technical momentum.

title: PT Darma Henwa Tbk Announces Up to Rp 1.66 Trillion Share Buyback
body: PT Darma Henwa Tbk plans to repurchase shares worth up to Rp 1.66 trillion, representing roughly 10% of its paid‑up capital, between 19 November 2025 and 19 February 2026. The buyback is expected to raise earnings per share from Rp 4.13 to Rp 4.59 while reducing assets and equity by the buyback amount, supported by the company's strong liquidity.

title: Hospitality Sector Stocks Expected to Benefit from 2025/2026 Year-End Holidays
body: The hospitality sector is projected to benefit from the 2025/2026 year-end holidays, with analysts expecting high hotel occupancy driven by resilient spending from middle-to-upper class consumers despite overall weakening purchasing power. Key recommendations include PT Pakuwon Jati Tbk (PWON) as a defensive pick, PT Ciputra Development Tbk (CTRA) for growth with 17% projected net profit increase in 2025, and PT Summarecon Agung Tbk (SMRA) for safety, while PT Pembangunan Jaya Ancol Tbk (PJA) is a speculative option and PT MNC Tourism Indonesia Tbk saw a 99% net profit surge in 2024.

title: PT PP Tbk (PTPP) Divests Non-Core Assets Totaling Rp 1.69 Trillion to Strengthen Core Business and Financial Health
body: PT PP Tbk (PTPP) is divesting non-core assets worth Rp 1.69 trillion, including an 81% stake in PT PP Infrastruktur to PT Varsha Zamindo Laksana for Rp 1.41 trillion and a 47.81% stake in PT Celebes Railway Indonesia to PT Solra Energi Terbarukan for Rp 282.1 billion. This strategic move aims to strengthen its core business, improve operational efficiency, enhance cash flow, and support the company's financial health, with analysts viewing it positively for liquidity and focus.

title: Proposed Gold Export Duty Pressures Mining Stocks, Driving Focus Towards Domestic Market and Downstreaming
body: The Indonesian government's plan to implement a 7.5%-15% progressive export duty on gold products has immediately pressured shares of gold mining companies, including PT Merdeka Copper Gold Tbk (MDKA), PT Merdeka Gold Resources Tbk (EMAS), PT Archi Indonesia Tbk (ARCI), PT J Resources Asia Pasifik Tbk (PSAB), PT United Tractors Tbk, and PT Aneka Tambang Tbk (ANTM), due to concerns over reduced profit margins. However, analysts view this policy as a strategic catalyst for strengthening the domestic gold industry through downstreaming, with companies like PT Aneka Tambang Tbk (ANTM) and PT Hartadinata Abadi Tbk (HRTA) poised to benefit from increased local supply and market dominance, while PSAB is expected to face prolonged pressure.

title: PT Folago Global Nusantara Tbk Launches Folago Academy in Solo to Boost Digital Creator Economy
body: PT Folago Global Nusantara Tbk launched the Folago Academy program in Solo, a collaborative effort with TikTok and the Solo local government, to develop 480 creators, including those with disabilities, and foster the growth of Indonesia's digital and social commerce economy. This initiative aligns with IRSX's mission to build a national creator talent pipeline, aiming to strengthen Folago's position and tap into Indonesia's creator economy, which is projected to exceed US $376 billion by 2030.

title: waskita karya Secures IDR 1.23 Trillion People's School Project in South Sulawesi
body: Waskita Karya has been awarded a contract worth Rp1.23 triliun for the construction of the People's School Project Phase II in five regencies across South Sulawesi, with a target completion by mid-2026. This project aims to provide free education for underprivileged children and support human resource development, encompassing facilities from elementary to high school, dormitories, and other supporting infrastructure.

title: ubs group ag Boosts Holding in bumi resources with Rp416.89 Billion Share Purchase
body: UBS group ag acquired an additional 2.28 billion shares of bumi resources on November 11, 2025, for approximately Rp416.89 billion at a price of Rp182,199 per share. This transaction increased ubs group ag's total ownership in bumi resources to 28.26 billion shares, or 7.61%, for client derivative hedging activities.

title: darma henwa Allocates Rp1.66 Trillion for Share Buyback Program
body: Darma Henwa has allocated Rp1.66 trillion from internal cash for a share buyback program, aiming to repurchase approximately 10% of its issued and paid-up capital. This buyback, scheduled from November 19, 2025, to February 19, 2026, is expected to provide a good return for shareholders and increase investor confidence, without materially impacting the company's strong financial position.

title: capital strategic invesco Reduces Stake in capital financial indonesia Through Share Divestment
body: Capital Strategic Invesco divested 2.5 billion shares of capital financial indonesia on November 14, 2025, reducing its stake to 46.06% from 50.65% with an estimated transaction value of Rp2.71 trillion. This follows an earlier sale of 2.66 billion shares on October 15, 2025, valued at approximately Rp2.85 trillion, with both transactions facilitated by ocbc sekuritas indonesia, sinarmas sekuritas, and pacific sekuritas indonesia.

title: PT Sawit Sumbermas Sarana Tbk Secures Rp5.2 Trillion Syndicated Financing for Refinancing, Acquisition, and Working Capital
body: PT Sawit Sumbermas Sarana Tbk (SSMS) signed a syndicated financing agreement on November 18, 2025, with a consortium of national banks and financial institutions led by PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI), securing a maximum facility of Rp5.2 trillion. This financing aims to refinance previous loans, support the acquisition of PT Sawit Mandiri Lestari, and increase working capital to strengthen the company's financial structure, operational efficiency, and business expansion.

title: PT Janu Putra Sejahtera Tbk Stock Surges on Danantara Investment Rumors and Company Optimism for Poultry Sector.
body: Shares of PT Janu Putra Sejahtera Tbk surged 9.03% to Rp314, driven by market rumors of a Rp20 trillion investment by Danantara to finance national poultry farmers for the MBG program. The company's director expressed optimism for its role in the industry, targeting significant growth in 2025, supported by increasing retail investor interest.

title: Bank Mandiri Reinforces Treasury and Hedging Solutions Amidst Global Market Complexity, Achieving Significant Growth and Regional Recognition.
body: Bank Mandiri (BMRI) reaffirms its commitment to national businesses by providing adaptive treasury and hedging solutions, reporting a 5.67% YoY growth in foreign exchange treasury transactions and a 47.09% YoY increase in FX volume via Kopra Mandiri by Q3 2025. The bank also received the 'Best Hedging Foreign Exchange Solutions 2025' award for Southeast Asia, solidifying its position as a competitive regional provider.

title: Green City SG Pte. Ltd. Becomes New Controlling Shareholder of PT Bangun Karya Perkasa Jaya Tbk (KRYA) to Drive EV Ecosystem Expansion
body: Green City SG Pte. Ltd., along with PT Green Power Group Tbk. and Yang Jie, has become the new controlling shareholder of PT Bangun Karya Perkasa Jaya Tbk (KRYA) by acquiring 1,031,645,000 shares, representing 62% of its paid-up capital, and is required to conduct a mandatory tender offer at Rp104 per share from November 18 to December 19, 2025. This strategic acquisition aims to accelerate lithium battery technology penetration and expand the electric vehicle ecosystem in Indonesia, positioning KRYA as a core entity in the green energy and sustainable mobility supply chain.

title: PT Bukit Makmur Mandiri Utama Completes Early Full Repayment of Senior Notes 2026
body: PT Bukit Makmur Mandiri Utama, a subsidiary of PT Buma Internasional Grup Tbk, has completed the early full repayment of its remaining Senior Notes 2026, valued at USD212.25 million with a 7.75% fixed coupon, ahead of its February 2026 maturity. This strategic move, largely funded through a syndicated facility involving PT Bank Negara Indonesia Tbk, PT Bank Mandiri Tbk, and PT Bank Central Asia Tbk, aims to reduce short-term payment risk, strengthen liquidity, and enhance the company's credit profile.

title: Director Phillip Tjipto Increases Stake in PT impack pratama industri Tbk. (IMPC) for Investment
body: Phillip Tjipto, a director of PT impack pratama industri Tbk., acquired 750,000 IMPC shares on November 14, 2025, at Rp2,600 per share, totaling an estimated Rp1.95 billion for investment purposes. This transaction increased his direct ownership to 159,500,000 shares (0.29%), while IMPC shares subsequently fell 7.22% to Rp2,440 per share on Tuesday.

title: PT tigaraksa satria Tbk. (TGKA) Sets Interim Dividend of Rp30 Per Share for Fiscal Year 2025
body: PT tigaraksa satria Tbk (TGKA) has officially approved an interim dividend of Rp30 per share, totaling Rp27,554,782,500, for the fiscal year 2025, based on its financial report as of September 30, 2025. The cum dividend cash market date is November 28, 2025, with the ex-date on December 1, 2025.

title: Rondy Yunanda Increases Stake in PT ecocare indo pasifik Tbk. (HYGN) Through Share Purchase
body: Rondy Yunanda, an individual shareholder of PT ecocare indo pasifik Tbk, purchased 1,000,000 shares of HYGN at Rp167 per share on November 12, 2025, increasing his ownership from 7.99% to 8.03% for investment purposes. While HYGN's stock recently declined 1.83% on Tuesday and 6.40% weekly, it has still gained 8.78% year-to-date from early January 2025.

title: PT Nusatama Berkah Tbk Secures Cooperation Agreement with Global Rail Manufacturer CRRC
body: PT nusatama berkah Tbk has received a cooperation agreement from crrc ced railway electric unit (crrc), the world's largest train manufacturer, signifying crrc's interest in ntbk's engineering capabilities and positioning ntbk as a potential strategic partner. A market source views ntbk's momentum positively, projecting a continued rally with a realistic target price of Rp500 per share, anticipating the partnership will elevate Indonesia's electric transportation industry through investment and technology transfer.

title: PT pulau subur Tbk. to Distribute Interim Cash Dividends for Fiscal Year 2025
body: PT pulau subur Tbk (PTPS) will distribute an interim cash dividend of Rp3.5 per share, totaling Rp7,586,301,996, for the 2025 fiscal year. Key dates include a cum dividend for regular and negotiation markets on November 27, 2025, and payment on December 15, 2025, backed by a net profit of Rp23,763,581,412 as of September 30, 2025.

title: trimegah sekuritas indonesia Plans Rp550 Billion Bond Issuance with Varied Terms
body: Trimegah sekuritas indonesia will issue Rp550 billion in bonds, split into Rp50 billion with full commitment and Rp500 billion with best effort, offering fixed interest rates of 8% and 8.50% for 5 and 7-year tenors. The funds will be primarily used to refinance existing bonds, with the public offering set for December 1-2, 2025, and listing on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on December 8, 2025.

title: GoTo Group Acknowledges Trademark Lawsuit, Awaiting Official Summons
body: GOTO group is facing a trademark lawsuit at the Central Jakarta District Court but claims it has not yet received an official summons or information regarding the case's details. The company previously won a similar trademark infringement case against PT Terbit Financial Technology in 2022, where the court ruled the lawsuit inadmissible, a decision that became final and binding.

title: multi sarana nasional Increases Stake in habco trans maritima by Acquiring 28.28 Million Shares
body: Multi Sarana Nasional acquired 28,284,000 shares of habco trans maritima on November 14, 2025, for a total of Rp9.5 billion, with prices ranging from Rp328-336 per share. This transaction increased multi sarana nasional's ownership in habco trans maritima by 0.33% to 20.03%, totaling 1.73 billion shares, for investment purposes.

title: Hengtong Optic Electricinternational Co., Limited Sell Shares of Voksel Electric Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hengtong Optic Electricinternational Co., Limited sell shares of Voksel Electric Tbk (VOKS.JK) at approximately IDR 268, decreasing holdings from 2,275,055,820 to 2,275,050,320. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Oki Widjaja Buy Shares of PT Galva Technologies Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Oki Widjaja bought shares of PT Galva Technologies Tbk (GLVA.JK) at approximately IDR 450, increasing holdings from 36,777,700 to 36,877,700. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,440, increasing holdings from 115,260,400 to 119,170,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,440, increasing holdings from 115,260,400 to 119,170,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Trukindo Persadasejahtera Sell Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Trukindo Persadasejahtera sold shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,440. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Majukarya Mandiriindonesia, Pt Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Majukarya Mandiriindonesia, Pt bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,440. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,440, increasing holdings from 109,334,700 to 115,260,400. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,440, increasing holdings from 119,170,000 to 120,517,800. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Kundy Wijaya Sell Shares of PT Kusuma Kemindo Sentosa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Kundy Wijaya sold shares of PT Kusuma Kemindo Sentosa Tbk (KKES.JK) at approximately IDR 29, decreasing holdings from 82,272,300 to 81,640,800. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Andreas Tjahjadi Buy Shares of Mitra Investindo Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Andreas Tjahjadi bought shares of Mitra Investindo Tbk (MITI.JK) at approximately IDR 286, increasing holdings from 195,036,416 to 205,526,016. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Harimas Tunggal Perkasa Sell Shares of PT Impack Pratama Industri Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Harimas Tunggal Perkasa sold shares of PT Impack Pratama Industri Tbk (IMPC.JK) at approximately IDR 995, decreasing holdings from 22,620,395,000 to 22,020,395,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Mohamad Indra Permana Buy Shares of Mitra Investindo Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Mohamad Indra Permana bought shares of Mitra Investindo Tbk (MITI.JK) at approximately IDR 286, increasing holdings from 15,000,000 to 18,496,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Mohammad Jusuf Hamka Buy Shares of Citra Marga Nusaphala Persada Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Mohammad Jusuf Hamka bought shares of Citra Marga Nusaphala Persada Tbk (CMNP.JK) at approximately IDR 980, increasing holdings from 257,467,100 to 891,481,496. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT. Vina Nauli Jordania Sell Shares of PT Sumber Mas Konstruksi Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT. Vina Nauli Jordania sell shares of PT Sumber Mas Konstruksi Tbk (SMKM.JK) at approximately IDR 37, decreasing holdings from 200,330,144 to 152,330,144. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Andre Khor Kah Hin Buy Shares of PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Andre Khor Kah Hin bought shares of PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (TPIA.JK) at approximately IDR 7,450, increasing holdings from 295,800 to 432,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Rajati Alia Kapital Sell Shares of PT Era Digital Media Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Rajati Alia Kapital sold shares of PT Era Digital Media Tbk (AWAN.JK) at approximately IDR 250, decreasing holdings from 2,656,136,000 to 2,632,039,615. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Catur Dharma Anugerah Buy Shares of PT Guna Timur Raya Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Catur Dharma Anugerah bought shares of PT Guna Timur Raya Tbk (TRUK.JK) at approximately IDR 100. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Dana Indonesia expands cross-border QR payment network and merchant acceptance across Southeast Asia
body: Dana, which serves over 74 million users, is broadening merchant acceptance online and offline and linking Indonesia’s QRIS system with Thailand’s PromptPay, Singapore’s NETS, Malaysia’s DuitNow and Japan’s JPQR Global to enable seamless scan‑and‑pay abroad. CEO Vince Iswara said the firm will continue to grow its user base, add financial services and deepen partnerships while keeping Indonesia as its primary market.

title: PT Pertamina announces a 724‑million‑barrel unconventional oil‑gas discovery in Rokan and outlines its expanding green‑energy initiatives
body: Deputy CEO Oki Muraza told the Indonesian House Commission VI that the Rokan working area in Riau holds an estimated 724 million barrels of oil‑equivalent unconventional reserves, the largest find for Pertamina in a decade, and that further reserves may exist. The company also highlighted its sustainable aviation fuel production of 9,000 bpd, a 100 kg/day hydrogen pilot plant, an 800 GWh geothermal plant, and the rollout of Pertamax 95 ethanol‑blended gasoline at 168 outlets.

title: Indonesia Inaugurates Aren‑Palm Bioethanol Pilot Plant in West Java, Targeting Mid‑2026 Commercial Production
body: The pilot plant in Kamojang, Bandung district, backed by the Ministry of Forestry, Pertamina, the Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources, the Investment Ministry and the National Research Innovation Agency, will start producing 300 L per day of aren‑based bioethanol in early 2026, scaling to 30,000 kiloliters annually for distribution through Pertamina Patra Niaga stations. The project supports President Prabowo Subianto’s goal of reducing fuel imports and achieving national energy self‑sufficiency.

title: Indonesia targets electrification of over 10,000 remote areas within five years, requiring Rp61 trillion investment
body: The Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources said PLN has reached a 98.54% electrification rate, leaving about 5,700 villages and 4,400 hamlets—mainly in Papua, Maluku and Nusa Tenggara—to be connected, with an estimated Rp61 trillion needed, including Rp2.5 trillion for a free‑connection program that has already reached 155,000 low‑income households. The plan also includes communal solar plants for 80,000 villages to support local economies.

title: Tower Bersama to issue Rp2.2 trillion of sustainable bonds and sukuk
body: Tower Bersama will launch a Rp2.2 trillion issuance comprising a Rp1.6 trillion sustainable bond (two series at 5.50% for 3 years and 5.85% for 5 years) and a Rp600 billion sustainable sukuk ijarah (two series with annual returns of Rp11 billion and Rp23.40 billion). Proceeds will mainly (78.1%) fund the repayment of existing sustainable bonds due December 2025, with the remainder used to partially repay loans to Bank Negara Indonesia and BNI, and the offering is scheduled for public subscription from 28 Nov to 1 Dec 2025 with listing on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on 5 Dec 2025.

title: Kundy Wijaya Reduces Stake in PT Kusuma Kemindo Sentosa Tbk (Kkes) Through Multiple Divestment Transactions
body: Kundy Wijaya, a shareholder of PT Kusuma Kemindo Sentosa Tbk, divested 631,500 shares of Kkes at Rp29 per share on November 13, 2025, following an earlier sale of 82,900 shares at Rp30 per share on October 30, 2025. These transactions reduced Kundy Wijaya's direct ownership in Kkes from 5.48% (82.27 million shares) to 5.44% (81.6 million shares).

title: Delta Giri announces IDR 50 billion interim dividend for FY 2025
body: The company will distribute an interim dividend of IDR 8.5 per share, representing 48.4% of its net profit of IDR 103.31 billion for the nine months ended 30 September 2025, with payment scheduled for 19 December 2025. Key dates include cum‑dividend on 27 Nov, ex‑dividend on 28 Nov, record date on 1 Dec, and cash payment on 19 Dec.

title: Sampoerna Group sells Sampoerna Agro to Posco International’s AGPA Pte. Ltd.
body: Twinwood Family Holdings Limited, on behalf of the Sampoerna Group, transferred its entire stake in Sampoerna Agro to AGPA Pte. Ltd., a subsidiary of Posco International Corporation, with Deutsche Bank and Baker McKenzie acting as financial and legal advisors respectively. In the first half of 2025, Sampoerna Agro reported a 236.06% increase in net profit and a 45.18% rise in sales year‑on‑year, highlighting strong performance before the ownership change.

title: Catur Dharma Anugerah Surya acquires 87 million shares of Guna Timur Raya, securing ~20% stake at heavily discounted price
body: On 14 November 2025, Catur Dharma Anugerah Surya purchased 87 million shares of Guna Timur Raya at an execution price of Rp100 per share, a 79.75% discount to the closing price of Rp494, costing roughly Rp8.7 billion. The transaction gives Catur Dharma Anugerah Surya about a 20% ownership and is intended as a direct investment, according to corporate secretary Budi Gunawan.

title: posco international corporation becomes new controlling shareholder of sampoerna agro after acquiring 65.721% of its shares
body: Through its subsidiary agpa pte ltd, posco international corporation acquired 1,195,217,500 shares (65.721%) of sampoerna agro from twinwood family holdings limited, making posco the new controlling shareholder. The transaction, notified on 19 November 2025, is not expected to materially affect sampoerna agro’s operations or financial condition, and posco will launch a mandatory tender offer per pojk no. 9/2018; the stock rose 1,200 points (19.92%) to IDR 7,225 per share.

title: bank btpn syariah announces Rp304.29 billion interim dividend
body: The bank will distribute an interim dividend of Rp304.29 billion, representing 32.2% of its net profit of Rp945 billion for the nine months ended September 30 2025, resulting in a dividend of Rp39.5 per share. The dividend will be paid on December 18 2025, with the record date on December 1 2025 and cum‑ex dates spanning November 27–28 and December 1–2.

title: Ashmore Announces Rp4.5 Billion Share Buyback Program Running Nov 2025–Feb 2026
body: Ashmore will allocate Rp4.5 billion for a share repurchase that may run up to three months from 21 November 2025 to 19 February 2026, with the option to finish earlier if all shares are bought. The buyback, triggered by a valuation below the historical average, is intended to optimise capital management and create shareholder value without materially affecting earnings or operating cash flow.

title: Mohammad Jusuf Hamka purchases 633.96 million shares of Citra Marga Nusantara Persada, raising his stake to 13.3%
body: Between October 22 and 31, 2025, Hamka bought a total of 633.96 million shares of Citra Marga Nusantara Persada at Rp980 per share, spending approximately Rp621.28 billion. The transactions increased his holding to 891.48 million shares, representing 13.3% of the company, up from 4.3% prior to the purchases.

title: PT Jakarta Setiabudi Internasional Tbk reports 4% revenue growth in Q3 2025 amid hotel segment gains and outlines multiple development projects
body: For the nine months ended September 2025, the company posted revenue of Rp1.79 trillion, up 4% YoY, while net profit slipped 2% to Rp231.2 billion, with hotel revenues rising 8% driven by a 12% increase in foreign visitors to Bali. JS​PT also highlighted ongoing renovations of Hyatt Regency Yogyakarta and several residential and hospitality projects, including Puri Botanical, Hyarta Ecovillage, Savanna Sumatera, a luxury villa‑condominium expansion near Andaz Bali, Bali Collection revitalisation, and a planned hotel‑residence development in Phuket, Thailand.

title: PT Puri Sentul Permai Tbk (KDTN) Reports Strong 2025 Performance Driven by Hotel Expansion and Projects Significant Growth for 2026.
body: PT Puri Sentul Permai Tbk (KDTN) recorded outstanding performance in 2025, with revenue reaching Rp23.8 billion and net profit Rp635.4 million as of September 30, 2025, fueled by strategic expansion of its rest area hotels, which led to a significant stock price surge and an increase in active investors. The company plans further diversification with a Rp10 billion investment in new facilities and targets a 30-40% increase in revenue and net profit for 2026, while committing to dividend distribution if financial conditions allow.

title: PT Waskita Karya Tbk. Wins Rp1.23 Trillion 'Sekolah Rakyat' Project and Boosts New Contract Value to Rp5.6 Trillion
body: PT Waskita Karya Tbk. secured a Rp1.23 trillion project to build 'sekolah rakyat' in five regencies across South Sulawesi, supporting the government's program for quality education and human resource development. The company also reported a significant increase in new contracts, reaching Rp5.6 trillion by October 2025 from Rp1.4 trillion in June 2025, primarily driven by water resource projects and other infrastructure developments.

title: PT harimas tunggal perkasa Reduces Stake in PT impack pratama industri Tbk. (IMPC) by Selling 600 Million Shares
body: On November 18, 2025, PT harimas tunggal perkasa sold 600 million shares of PT impack pratama industri Tbk. (IMPC) for Rp597 billion at an average price of Rp995 per share, aiming to realize investment and increase free float. This transaction, where the price was 61% lower than IMPC's closing price of Rp2,550, decreased PT harimas tunggal perkasa's direct ownership from 41.23% to 40.13%.

title: PT Astra International Tbk. (ASII) Announces Management Changes Following Extraordinary General Meeting
body: PT Astra International Tbk. (ASII) held an Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders on Wednesday, approving the resignations of John Raymond Witt and Hsu Hai Yeh from commissioner positions, and Chiew Sin Cheok as director. The company subsequently appointed Lincoln Lin Feng Pan and Lee Liang Whye as commissioners, and re-appointed Hsu Hai Yeh as director, with all changes effective immediately.

title: PT Bursa Efek Indonesia Suspends PT Primarindo Asia Infrastructure Tbk. (BIMA) Shares Due to Debt Default
body: PT Bursa Efek Indonesia has temporarily suspended trading of PT Primarindo Asia Infrastructure Tbk. (BIMA) shares across all markets starting Wednesday, following a default notice on the company's debt to PT Perusahaan Pengelola Aset. This suspension, aimed at investor protection, will remain until further information and compliance with disclosure obligations are met, with total obligations reaching USD 1.82 million and Rp 13.45 billion by October 20, 2025.

title: bank mandiri Enhances Digital Payments for Commuterline and LRT Jabodebek with QRIS Tap
body: Bank Mandiri has implemented its QRIS Tap In-Tap Out Livin’ Mandiri feature for Kereta Commuter Indonesia and LRT Jabodebek, allowing users to pay by tapping NFC-enabled phones at gates without manual scanning or top-ups. This innovation, available across all Commuterline and LRT Jabodebek gates, aims to provide faster, more efficient transactions and support digital payment acceleration in public transportation.

title: Oki Widjaja Increases Shareholding in PT galva technologies Tbk. (GLVA) for Investment
body: Oki Widjaja, Commissioner and controller of PT galva technologies Tbk. (GLVA), purchased 100,000 GLVA shares at Rp450 each on November 11, 2025, for investment, increasing his ownership from 2.45% to 2.46%. Concurrently, GLVA's share price fell 1.92% to Rp408 per share on the same day.

title: PT Trimegah Bangun Persada Tbk. (NCKL) Secures Saprolite Nickel Ore Sales with Affiliates to Strengthen Downstream Integration
body: PT Trimegah Bangun Persada Tbk. (NCKL), through its subsidiary PT Gane Tambang Sentosa, signed an agreement effective November 13, 2025, to sell saprolite nickel ore to affiliated entities PT Karunia Permai Sentosa and PT Halmahera Jaya Feronikel, reinforcing its internal supply chain for downstream processing. While the company stated no material impact, NCKL's shares dropped 1.3% to Rp980 today and 19.5% over the last month.

title: Noprian Fadli Buy Shares of PT Raja Roti Cemerlang Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Noprian Fadli bought shares of PT Raja Roti Cemerlang Tbk (BRRC.JK), increasing holdings from 289,000 to 50,289,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Granada Global Industri Sell Shares of PT Raja Roti Cemerlang Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Granada Global Industri sold shares of PT Raja Roti Cemerlang Tbk (BRRC.JK) at approximately IDR 150, decreasing holdings from 176,436,008 to 126,436,008. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Twinwood Family Holdings Limited Sell Shares of PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Twinwood Family Holdings Limited sold shares of PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk (SGRO.JK) at approximately IDR 7,903. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Noprian Fadli Buy Shares of PT Raja Roti Cemerlang Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Noprian Fadli bought shares of PT Raja Roti Cemerlang Tbk (BRRC.JK), increasing holdings from 289,000 to 50,289,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

body: According to the published announcement, Agpa Pte. Ltd buy shares of PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk (SGRO.JK) at approximately IDR 7,903. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

body: According to the published announcement, Agpa Pte. Ltd buy shares of PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk (SGRO.JK) at approximately IDR 7,903. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Pimsf Pulogadung Buy Shares of PT Geoprima Solusi Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Pimsf Pulogadung bought shares of PT Geoprima Solusi Tbk (GPSO.JK) at approximately IDR 650, increasing holdings from 303,033,800 to 316,368,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Shandong Heavy Industry (SDHI) showcases its mining equipment portfolio and signs strategic deals at Mining Expo 2025
body: SDHI displayed its brands Shantui, Shacman, Lovol, Weichai and Sinotruk at Mining Expo 2025, operating one of the largest booths (OS 27) and emphasizing a long‑term strategy for Indonesia’s mining sector, including the handover of advanced machines and new partnership agreements. MC Grup, the Indonesian distributor of Shacman, also exhibited the Shacman X3000 and F3000 dump trucks and reiterated its optimism about growing mining demand.

title: Analyst warns accelerated B50 biodiesel mandate could strain Indonesia’s palm oil market and fiscal balance
body: Julian Conway McGill of Glenauk Economics said the push to accelerate Indonesia’s biodiesel blend from B30/B40 to a mandatory B50 is driving up crude palm oil prices, raising financing costs and export levies, and threatening the sector’s competitiveness. He urges a flexible, demand‑linked mandate—similar to Brazil’s sugar‑ethanol model—rather than rapid implementation.

title: Indonesia's Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources urges synchronized mining licensing policy with provincial governments
body: The ministry held a coordination meeting with all provincial governments to align the implementation of recent regulations—including Law No. 2/2025, Government Regulation No. 39/2025 and Presidential Decree No. 55/2022—and to accelerate the delegation of mining licensing authority to provinces, supported by a risk‑based digital OSS system and new PP No. 28/2025. Officials said the integrated, transparent approach aims to improve sustainable mining governance and support national development.

title: Russian Sistema Group explores pulp-paper partnership with Danantara Indonesia
body: Danantara Indonesia CEO Rosan Roeslani said Russia’s Sistema Group, accompanied by ambassador Sergei Gennadievich Tolchenov, expressed strong interest in investing in Indonesia’s pulp‑paper sector, with the government ready to facilitate connections to local firms. The discussions, still at an early stage with no disclosed investment value, also covered potential cooperation in healthcare, electric vessels, hospitality and education.

title: Indonesia to Begin Construction of Seven Waste-to-Energy Plants in 2026 as First Phase of 33-Plant Goal
body: Coordinating Minister Airlangga Hartarto announced that seven waste-to-energy power plants, with a combined capacity of about 197.4 MW and able to process roughly 12,000 tons of waste daily, will start construction in 2026, marking the first phase toward the presidential target of 33 plants by 2029 under Regulation No. 109/2025. The projects will be located in Greater Yogyakarta, Greater Denpasar, Greater Bogor, Greater Bekasi, Greater Tangerang, Greater Medan and Greater Semarang.

title: Indonesia targets up to Rp6 trillion in revenue from new gold export duties
body: Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa said the government aims to raise between Rp2 trillion and Rp6 trillion in state revenue by imposing 7.5%‑15% duties on gold exports such as dore bars, granules, cast bars and minted bars. The policy also seeks to secure domestic gold supplies for entities like PT Pegadaian and Bank Syariah Indonesia and to capture gains from global gold prices above US$4,000 per ounce.

title: Indonesia Announces Ban on Online Sale of Imported Used Clothing to Curb Illegal Thrifting
body: The Communication and Digital Ministry, led by Meutya Hafid, will enforce a ban on online thrifting of imported used clothing, with e‑commerce platforms required to comply with Trade Regulation No. 31/2023 and the import prohibition under Trade Regulation No. 40/2022. The MSME ministry will rebrand former thrift markets and help sellers shift to local products.

title: Indonesia's Marine Affairs Ministry targets Rp2 trillion investment at fisheries business‑matching forum
body: The ministry hosted a one‑day investment‑matching event in Jakarta with around 300 domestic and international participants, aiming to mobilise Rp2 trillion for downstream fisheries projects. Aligned with National Fish Day, the forum highlighted investment opportunities, government policy support, and facilitated meetings among ministries, fishers, processors, logistics firms and foreign investors such as the Netherlands, Singapore, the European Investment Bank and JICA.

title: PT Adhi Kartiko Pratama Tbk reports resignation of three senior executives, stock rises 0.5%
body: PT Adhi Kartiko Pratama Tbk announced that director Sebin Kim, principal commissioner Hyek Seo Koo and director Gwanghee Hong have submitted resignations effective 20 November 2025, and the company said the departures will be processed under POJK 33/2004 with no impact on operations or financial condition. The company's shares (nice) rose 0.5% to Rp404, down 6.91% over the past month from Rp434 on 21 October 2025.

title: bank mandiri reaffirms its role as government’s strategic partner in accelerating the 3‑million‑house program through KPP credit outreach
body: On 20 November 2025, bank mandiri held a KPP credit socialisation in Tangerang attended by over 875 housing‑sector participants, including 315 supply‑side firms and 260 micro‑enterprises, to support the government’s target of building 3 million homes by 2029. The program, governed by Permenko No. 13/2025 and Permen PKP No. 13/2025, offers working‑capital and investment loans up to IDR 1 billion for micro, IDR 5 billion for small, and IDR 10 billion for medium enterprises.

title: Rudi Reksa Sutantra sells 16 million Atlantis Indonesia shares, raising about Rp1.05 billion and reducing his stake to 48.78%
body: Rudi Reksa Sutantra disposed of 11 million shares at Rp66 each on 17 Nov 2025 and 5 million shares at Rp64 each on 18 Nov 2025, generating roughly Rp1.05 billion. After the transactions, his holding in Atlantis Indonesia fell to 3.02 billion shares (48.78%), a 0.26‑percentage‑point dilution from before.

title: Amman Mineral Nusa Tenggara Secures Full Copper Mark Certification for Batu Hijau Mine
body: The subsidiary of amman mineral internasional has met all 32 criteria of the Copper Mark, confirming compliance with international ESG standards for responsible copper production. Executives said the award demonstrates the company's sustainability commitments and Indonesia’s contribution to global mining best practices.

title: Chengdong Investment Corp sells 3.71 billion Bumi Resources shares, cutting its stake to 7.99%
body: On 18 November 2025, Chengdong Investment Corporation divested a total of 3.71 billion Bumi Resources shares in two simultaneous transactions at prices ranging from Rp173.45 to Rp213.72 per share, generating approximately Rp646.11 billion in proceeds. The sale reduced Chengdong’s holding to 29.7 billion shares (7.99% of total), down from 33.4 billion shares (8.99%) prior to the transaction.

title: Sampoerna Agro completes Rp316.01 billion affiliate share sale to restructure and divest its sago HTI business
body: Sampoerna Agro transferred 99.66% of National Sago Prima shares (2,859,900 shares) from Sampoerna Bio Fuels to Sampoerna Agri Resources Pte Ltd for about Rp314.93 billion, and the remaining 0.34% (9,800 shares) from Sungai Menang to Sampoerna Strategic for Rp1.07 billion. The transaction, aimed at internal restructuring and focusing on more profitable palm‑oil operations, is expected to improve liquidity, cash flow and overall financial performance.

title: Budi Aditya Erna Mulyanto to Acquire 51% Stake in Trimitra Goldland from Trimitra Prawara
body: Budi Aditya Erna Mulyanto will purchase 637.5 million shares, representing 51% of Trimitra Goldland's paid‑up capital, from majority shareholder Trimitra Prawara, making him the new controller of the company. The transaction is non‑affiliated and does not constitute a related‑party or conflict‑of‑interest deal under OJK Regulation No. 42/POJK.04/2020.

title: PT sinar mas multiartha Tbk. (SMMA) Announces Leadership Changes at Extraordinary General Meeting
body: PT sinar mas multiartha Tbk. (SMMA) held an Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders on November 18, 2025, which was declared valid with 4,115,867,435 shares or 64.636% of voting rights present. The meeting approved the appointment of Eric Buntoro as Deputy President Director and accepted the resignation of Ferita as Director.

title: PT adi sarana armada Tbk signs IDR 500,000,000,000 term loan with PT bank qnb indonesia Tbk for fleet expansion
body: The company signed a term loan agreement for IDR 500 billion to support working‑capital needs, primarily to purchase rental vehicles and boost revenue. Following the announcement, PT adi sarana armada Tbk’s shares rose 1.85% to IDR 1,100 at Thursday’s close.

title: Komisaris Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara Increases Shareholding in PT dharma polimetal Tbk. (DRMA) for Investment
body: Komisaris Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara increased his direct ownership in PT dharma polimetal Tbk. (DRMA) by purchasing 3,000,000 shares at Rp1,000 each on November 19, 2025, for investment purposes, bringing his total stake to 74,897,600 shares or 1.592%. This transaction follows a previous repo transfer of 952,400 shares on November 4, 2025, while DRMA shares closed at Rp1,005 on Thursday.

title: Noprian Fadli Increases Stake in PT raja roti cemerlang Tbk. (BRRC) to Over 5% for Investment
body: Noprian Fadli acquired 50 million shares of PT raja roti cemerlang Tbk. (BRRC) for Rp7.5 billion on November 19, 2025, raising his direct ownership to 50,289,000 shares or 5.18% for investment purposes. Following this, BRRC's shares rose 20.94% to Rp156 on Thursday, contributing to significant gains of 87.6% in the last month and 174.5% over six months.

title: Indonesia Stock Exchange Reopens Trading for Three Suspended Stocks with Mixed Initial Performance
body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange (BEI) has reopened trading for PT Cahayasakti Investindo Sukses Tbk. (csis), PT Trimitra Prawara Goldland Tbk. (atap), and PT Sentra Food Indonesia Tbk. (food) starting Thursday, after deeming volatility and unusual transaction patterns sufficiently curbed. Post-reopening, atap surged 9.52% to auto rejection atas, while food fell 9.95% to auto-rejection bawah, and csis weakened 9.7%.

title: PT rajati alia kapital Reduces Stake in PT era digital media Tbk. (AWAN) Through Share Sales
body: PT rajati alia kapital, the controlling shareholder of PT era digital media Tbk., sold 24,096,385 shares of AWAN on November 14, 2025, at Rp250 per share, totaling Rp6.04 billion, following a previous sale on November 12, 2025. These transactions reduced its ownership from 77.32% to 76.62%.

title: twinwood family holdings limited Divests Entire Stake in PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk. (SGRO), POSCO International Corporation Becomes New Controller
body: twinwood family holdings limited sold its entire 65.721% stake, comprising 1,195,217,500 shares, in PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk. (SGRO) at Rp7,903 per share for a total of approximately Rp9.44 trillion on November 19, 2025. This transaction makes POSCO International Corporation, through its subsidiary AGPA Pte Ltd, the new controlling shareholder of PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk., with no significant impact expected on the company's operations or financial condition.

title: Director of PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk. Increases Stake in TPIA Through Share Purchase
body: Andre Khor Kah Hin, a director of PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk., acquired 136,200 shares of TPIA on November 18, 2025, at Rp7,450 per share, totaling Rp1.01 billion for investment purposes. This transaction increased his direct ownership to 432,000 shares, while TPIA's stock closed at Rp7,250 on Thursday, up 0.69%.

title: Garibaldi “Boy” Thohir Increases Stake in PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk, Citing Strong Prospects
body: National businessman Garibaldi “Boy” Thohir acquired 3,146,700 shares of PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk from November 17–19, 2025, expressing confidence in the company's prospects and the national capital market. This accumulation follows PT Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia Tbk reporting a net profit of Rp372.5 billion in Q3 2025, a significant increase from Rp151.7 billion in the prior year, with its shares rising 7.87 percent in the last week.

title: PT Triputra Agro Persada Tbk (TAPG) Receives Rp628.11 Billion in Interim Dividends from Subsidiaries
body: PT Triputra Agro Persada Tbk (TAPG) announced it received interim dividends totaling Rp628,111,000,000 from several subsidiaries on November 19, 2025. The company stated this receipt has no material impact on its financial condition, while its stock (TAPG) increased 1.76% to Rp1,735 per share on the trading day.

title: Ubs Group Ag Buy Shares of Bumi Resources Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Ubs Group Ag bought shares of Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI.JK) at approximately IDR 198, increasing holdings from 27,169,510,863 to 30,091,179,063. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Sentra Investa Maksima Sell Shares of PT Lavender Bina Cendikia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Sentra Investa Maksima sold shares of PT Lavender Bina Cendikia Tbk (BMBL.JK) at approximately IDR 48, decreasing holdings from 106,045,100 to 103,070,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Hasmoro Buy Shares of PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hasmoro bought shares of PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk (HEAL.JK) at approximately IDR 1,420, increasing holdings from 746,030,989 to 746,494,389. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Trimegah Sumber Mas Sell Shares of PT Trimegah Karya Pratama Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Trimegah Sumber Mas sold shares of PT Trimegah Karya Pratama Tbk (UVCR.JK) at approximately IDR 118, decreasing holdings from 924,557,467 to 862,057,467. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Agustin Widyawati Buy Shares of PT Trimegah Karya Pratama Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Agustin Widyawati bought shares of PT Trimegah Karya Pratama Tbk (UVCR.JK) at approximately IDR 118, increasing holdings from 37,808,400 to 100,308,400. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia Deemed Ready to Begin CPTPP Accession Process, Says Australia
body: Coordinating Minister Airlangga Hartarto announced that Australia has placed Indonesia on its list of countries ready to start the accession to the Comprehensive and Progressive Agreement for Trans‑Pacific Partnership, noting that Indonesia already aligns with most CPTPP provisions and only needs minor regulatory adjustments. He added that membership is expected to expand market access, boost exports and foreign investment, and strengthen Indonesia’s role in regional economic integration.

title: Indonesia's automotive sector sees growth potential amid low vehicle ownership and expanding low‑emission initiatives
body: The Ministry of Industry highlighted that Indonesia's large but under‑penetrated domestic market, with a car ownership ratio of 99 per 1,000 people, supports further expansion of the automotive subsector, which contributed 1.28% of GDP in Q3 2025 and produced 0.85 million four‑wheel vehicles and 5.25 million two‑/three‑wheel units. Government incentives and the low‑carbon vehicle program have attracted 15 manufacturers to invest 22.37 trillion rupiah in energy‑efficient, hybrid, and electric models, boosting sales and exports that now account for about 45% of total output.

title: Indonesia's state-owned ferry operator PT ASDP Indonesia Ferry announces up to 19% holiday fare discounts
body: PT ASDP Indonesia Ferry will apply discounts of up to 19% on ferry fares for travel between December 22, 2025 and January 10, 2026, covering major routes such as Merak‑Bakauheni and Padangbai‑Lembar, as part of a government transport stimulus funded with 180 billion rupiah. The program expects to serve about 227,000 passengers and 491,000 vehicles and is supported by the Ferizy online ticketing platform.

title: tbs energi utama to issue Rp448.5 billion 9‑year sukuk wakalah
body: tbs energi utama plans to raise Rp448.5 billion through a 9‑year sukuk wakalah, with issuance dated 25 November 2025 and agreements signed on 21 November 2025. The bond will pay periodic coupons every three months, the first on 21 February 2025, and the company states the issuance will not adversely affect its operations, legal standing, financial condition, or business continuity.

title: UBS Group AG purchases 2.92 billion Bumi Resources shares at a 10% discount, raising its stake to 8.1%
body: On 14 Nov 2025, UBS Group AG bought 2.92 billion Bumi Resources shares at Rp197,7218 each, a 10.12% discount to the closing price of Rp220, costing roughly Rp577.67 billion. The transaction increased UBS’s holding to 30.09 billion shares (8.1% of the company), up from 27.16 billion shares (7.32%).

title: Bank Mandiri hosts housing credit program socialization to support Indonesia's 3‑million‑house target
body: Bank Mandiri, as a strategic partner of the government, organized a KPP credit socialization in Tangerang attended by over 875 housing‑sector participants, including developers, contractors, material suppliers and micro‑enterprises. The program, governed by Permenko No.13/2025 and Permen PKP No.13/2025, aims to finance up to IDR 10 billion for UMKM and individuals to build, renovate or purchase homes, aligning with the 3 million‑house goal for 2025‑2029.

title: PT Buana Artha Anugerah Tbk shifts control to Singapore-based Calculus Investment after 32.19% stake sale
body: PT Kencana Selaras Sejahtera sold its entire 32.19% holding (1,544,925,000 shares) in PT Buana Artha Anugerah Tbk to Calculus Investment Pte. Ltd. for Rp88 per share, valuing the transaction at about Rp136 billion, and the new shareholder will comply with OJK Regulation No.9/POJK.04/2018, including a mandatory tender offer to minority shareholders; the stock closed up 9.09% at Rp96.

title: Ruby Mining Limited negotiates to acquire 86% of PT Puri Sentul Permai Tbk
body: Ruby Mining Limited of Hong Kong is in negotiations to purchase approximately 1,075 billion shares, equal to 86% of the issued and paid‑up capital of PT Puri Sentul Permai Tbk (kdtn), currently held by PT Putrasakti Mandiri, PT Intan Perdana Sukses, and PT Cahayasakti Investindo Sukses Tbk. The transaction, subject to due‑diligence and a mandatory tender offer, would change the control of PT Puri Sentul Permai Tbk and may influence its share price.

title: PT bank pembangunan daerah banten Tbk. announces resignation of Director of Business Bambang Widyatmoko
body: The company received Bambang Widyatmoko’s resignation letter on 19 November 2025 and will convene a shareholders meeting in accordance with POJK 33/2004, stating the departure will not affect operations, legal matters, financial condition, or business continuity. Its shares have been stable at Rp28, up 7.69% month‑on‑month but down 6.67% year‑to‑date from the January 2025 level of Rp30.

title: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk disburses 147.2 trillion IDR in micro‑SME credit, reaching 83.2% of its 2025 allocation
body: By the end of October 2025, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk has provided 147.2 trillion IDR in credit to 3.2 million micro, small and medium enterprise borrowers, representing 83.2% of its 2025 target of 177 trillion IDR. The bank retains a remaining quota of 29.8 trillion IDR to be allocated before year‑end, supporting sectors such as agriculture, trade and fisheries.

title: PT sigma energy compressindo Tbk. declares interim cash dividend of Rp3 per share for FY2025
body: The company announced an interim cash dividend of Rp3 per share, totaling Rp2.73 billion or 27.06% of its net profit of Rp10.09 billion as of 30 September 2025. Following the announcement, the stock rose 2.19% to Rp140, yielding an estimated dividend yield of 2.14%, with dividend dates set between 1 and 4 December 2025.

title: PT pimsf pulogadung raises its ownership in PT geoprima solusi Tbk to 47.45% after buying 13.3 million shares
body: On 20 November 2025, PT PIMSF Pulogadung purchased 13,334,800 shares of PT Geoprima Golusi Tbk at Rp650 per share, amounting to roughly Rp8.7 billion, increasing its direct stake from 45.45% to 47.45% (303,033,800 to 316,368,600 shares). Following the disclosure, PT Geoprima Solusi Tbk's stock jumped 21.31% to Rp740.

title: Bursa Efek Indonesia Lifts Suspension for PT Lotte Chemical Titan Tbk. and PT Agung Menjangan Mas Tbk., Moves Both to Full Call Auction
body: Bursa Efek Indonesia (BEI) officially lifted the trading suspension for PT Lotte Chemical Titan Tbk. (FPNI) and PT Agung Menjangan Mas Tbk. (AMMS) on Friday, subsequently placing both into the Full Call Auction mechanism for up to a week due to prior consecutive suspensions. Following the suspension lift, FPNI shares rose 9.38% to Rp700 and AMMS increased 5.69% to Rp520, with AMMS notably achieving a year-to-date gain of 1,168.29%.

title: PT xlsmart telecom sejahtera Tbk. (EXCL) Distributes Rp2.89 Trillion Additional Dividend
body: PT XLSmart Telecom Sejahtera Tbk. (EXCL) has officially distributed an additional dividend of Rp2.89 trillion, or Rp159 per share, sourced from retained earnings as of December 31, 2024, following approval at an extraordinary general meeting. This dividend represents 30.6% of the company's Rp9.46 trillion retained earnings, resulting in an estimated dividend yield of 5.82% based on Friday's share price of Rp2,730.

title: PT telkom indonesia Tbk. (TLKM) Announces Resignation of Independent Commissioner Yohanes Surya
body: PT Telkom Indonesia Tbk. (TLKM) received the resignation of its independent commissioner, Yohanes Surya, effective November 20, 2025. The company stated that this change will not have a material impact on its business continuity and will process the resignation according to applicable regulations.

title: bank tabungan negara Continues BTN Housingpreneur 2025 Roadshow to Innovate Future Housing Solutions
body: Bank Tabungan Negara (BTN) continued its BTN Housingpreneur 2025 roadshow at universitas sumatera utara (USU), Medan, aiming to find adaptive and sustainable housing solutions for urban communities and support President Prabowo Subianto's three million homes program. This annual competition, themed "Housing Nation: Smart, Green, Inclusive," encourages collaboration among various stakeholders and offers a total prize of Rp1.5 billion, with registration open until December 15, 2025.

title: PT Mandom Indonesia Tbk Announces Extension of Tender Offer Period for Mandom Corporation Acquisition
body: PT Mandom Indonesia Tbk provided an update on November 19, 2025, regarding the acquisition of its controlling shareholder, mandom corporation, by kalon holdings co., ltd. The tender offer period for mandom corporation has been extended from September 26, 2025 – November 19, 2025, to September 26, 2025 – December 4, 2025, with the completion date shifted to December 11, 2025, and no material impact on PT Mandom Indonesia Tbk's operations or financials.

title: PT Gowa Makassar Tourism Development Tbk denies land permit violation claims and reports no operational impact
body: Secretary Tubagus Syamsul Hidayat said allegations of irregularities in principle land permits for the Metro Tanjung Bunga area are unfounded, citing governor decisions and confirming all land use has solid legal basis, and noted that the company issued clarifications on 14, 17 and 18 November 2025 while reserving the right to take legal action.
