The stock fell 1.22% to Rp2,430, up 19% over the past month but down 47.5% year‑to‑date from early‑January levels.

title: PT tirta mahakam resources Tbk provides details on short‑term charter contracts and future expansion plans
body: The Indonesian stock exchange requested a comprehensive explanation of PT tirta mahakam resources Tbk's short‑term vessel charter contracts with PT guna harapan lestari (Oct 17‑Dec 31 2025) and PT lima srikandi jaya (Oct 17‑Dec 16 2025), and the company said revenue continuity is supported by potential extensions, contract transfers to users such as PT well harvest winning alumina refinery and PT marina bara lestari, and full fleet occupancy. Despite a significant rise in October 2025 revenue, the firm recorded gross, operating and net losses as operations only began that month, but management expects profitability as fleet utilization improves and new vessels are added.

title: PT Hensel Davest Indonesia Tbk divests PT Doeku Peduli Indonesia for IDR 18.5 billion to sharpen its core e‑commerce focus
body: The company’s corporate secretary Riska Handayani confirmed the sale of the P2P‑lending subsidiary PT Doeku Peduli Indonesia for IDR 18.5 billion, a transaction below the 20 % equity threshold and therefore not material under regulations. The divestment is part of a portfolio‑restructuring strategy aimed at concentrating on Hensel Davest’s core e‑commerce and digital product distribution business, with no expected impact on its operations.

title: PT gunung raja paksi Tbk schedules extraordinary shareholders meeting on Dec 15 2025 to approve conversion to a foreign investment company after PT apollo visintama putra acquires shares
body: The company will hold a RUPS Luar Biasa on 15 December 2025 to seek shareholder approval for changing its status from a domestic to a foreign investment entity following the entry of PT apollo visintama putra, a foreign‑capital investor. The announcement lifted ggrp shares by 24.76% (52 points) to the daily high of Rp262, though management gave no details on the impact on strategy, funding or long‑term expansion.

title: PT Supreme Cable Manufacturing & Commerce Tbk Announces Liquidation of Subsidiary PT Supreme Decoluxe
body: The company disclosed that PT Supreme Decoluxe will be dissolved and liquidated effective 19 November 2025 after failing to secure new business opportunities. The director, nicodermus, stated that the liquidation will not affect PT Supreme Cable Manufacturing & Commerce Tbk's operations, legal standing, financial condition, or its financial statements.

title: Grup Sampoerna exits Indonesian stock market as PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk sells 65.7% stake to AGPA Pte Ltd, a Posco International subsidiary
body: On 19 November 2025, PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk transferred 1,195,217,500 shares (65.721% of its paid‑up capital) from Twinwood Family Holdings Limited to AGPA Pte. Ltd., ending Grup Sampoerna’s three‑decade presence on the Indonesia Stock Exchange. The company said the change of control will not materially affect its operations, legal standing, financial condition or business continuity.

title: Bank Mandiri Highlights Digital Ecosystem Growth and Wins Indonesia’s Best Transaction Bank Award
body: Bank Mandiri’s digital platforms—livin’ Mandiri, livin’ merchant and Kopra Mandiri—now form an integrated closed‑loop ecosystem, with Kopra processing over Rp22 trillion in transactions (22% YoY) and 1.2 billion transactions (14% YoY), livin’ Mandiri serving 35.8 million users and handling Rp3.62 trillion in value (10% YoY), and livin’ merchant supporting more than 3 million merchants with transaction value up 166% and volume up 172% year‑on‑year. The bank was awarded Indonesia’s Best Transaction Bank at the Euromoney Transaction Banking Awards 2025, citing its strong financial performance, robust asset growth and sustained digital innovation aligned with government economic transformation goals.

title: Commissioner Hasmoro expands his holding in Medikaloka Hermina to 4.86% after a November 14 share purchase
body: On November 14, 2025, Hasmoro bought 463,400 Medikaloka Hermina shares at Rp1,420 each, costing about Rp658 million, adding to earlier purchases of 1.59 million shares on November 7, 10 and 11. The cumulative stake now totals 746.49 million shares, representing 4.86% of the company, a slight increase from the prior 4.86% holding.

title: Bank BTN to Issue Up to IDR 2.3 Trillion of Social and Subordinated Bonds
body: Bank BTN plans to issue bonds worth up to IDR 2.3 trillion, comprising a IDR 300 billion social bond and up to IDR 2 trillion subordinated bond with 3‑year and 5‑year maturities respectively and quarterly interest payments. All proceeds from the social bond will fund new or refinanced affordable‑housing, infrastructure, job‑creation and SME/micro‑finance projects, with issuance and allocation scheduled from 21 Nov to 15 Dec 2025.

title: Pt Nusantara Utamainvestama Buy Shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Nusantara Utamainvestama bought shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk (BUVA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,040, increasing holdings from 16,658,449,065 to 16,668,137,011. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Jhon Veter Firdaus Reagen Buy Shares of PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Jhon Veter Firdaus Reagen bought shares of PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk (FUJI.JK) at approximately IDR 829, increasing holdings from 165,319,600 to 171,389,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indosat Ooredoo plans to repay IDR56 billion maturing debt using internal cash
body: Indosat Ooredoo has IDR56 billion of bonds and sukuk maturing on 5 March 2026 (IDR45 billion bonds and IDR11 billion sukuk). The company intends to settle the debt with its IDR2.4 trillion cash balance as of September 2025, while an unused credit facility of IDR7.8 trillion remains available as a backup source.

title: Pt Nusantara Utamainvestama Buy Shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Nusantara Utamainvestama bought shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk (BUVA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,040, increasing holdings from 16,658,449,065 to 16,668,137,011. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia's MSME Minister Maman Abdurrahman pushes priority commodity clusters and collateral‑free financing for West Kalimantan SMEs
body: At a launch in Pontianak, the minister highlighted clusters such as kratom, arowana fish and processed tilapia as drivers of regional growth and announced a KUR credit scheme offering up to Rp100 million without collateral, backed by guarantee agencies like Jamkrindo and AskRindo; the governor praised the support for MSMEs that helped revive the post‑pandemic economy.

title: Indonesia cuts subsidized fertilizer prices by 20% to boost farmer productivity and food security
body: Deputy Agriculture Minister Sudaryono announced a 20% reduction in subsidized urea, NPK and Phonska fertilizers, with the government covering price differences and enforcing new retail ceilings through inspections. Ministry data, citing PT pupuk indonesia, show fertilizer sales rising to 180,000 tonnes of urea and 266,800 tonnes of NPK, while daily farmer purchases increased to roughly 72,000‑78,000.

title: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk aims for 105,700 oz gold production by 2027 as Mirae Asset upgrades to buy
body: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) is entering a strategic growth phase supported by strong global gold prices, an expanded 8,500‑tpd processing capacity and the development of a high‑grade underground mine, which Mirae Asset Securities expects to lift gold output to 91,900 oz in 2026 and 105,700 oz in 2027. The company’s revenue is projected to rise to US$267 million in 2025 and US$380 million in 2026 with net profit climbing to US$60 million and US$108 million respectively, prompting Mirae Asset to issue a buy rating with a target price of Rp1,100 per share (stock closed at Rp990, +4.21%).

title: MSCI Index Review Adds PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk and PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk, Triggering Anticipated Foreign Inflows
body: MSCI’s November 2025 review will make PT Barito Renewables Energy Tbk and PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk members of the MSCI Global Standard Index effective 25 Nov 2025, replacing PT Kalbe Farma Tbk and PT Indofood CBP Sukses Makmur Tbk. Analysts expect strong foreign inflows into the new constituents—especially bren, raja, wifi and brms—with brms having risen 9.68% on 5 Nov and attracting net foreign purchases of roughly Rp 3.19 trillion over the past three months, while the exiting stocks may offer value‑oriented opportunities.

title: PT Petrosea Tbk acquires 60% of Scan-Bilt Pte. Ltd. for SGD 10.3 million to expand EPC capabilities and regional reach
body: PT Petrosea Tbk, via its subsidiary Petrosea Services Solutions Ltd., completed the purchase of a 60% stake in Scan‑Bilt Pte. Ltd. (SBPL) from Tcal Engineering Pte. Ltd. on 21 November 2025 for SGD 10.3 million (≈USD 8.03 million), making Petrosea the majority shareholder. The deal is positioned as a strategic step to broaden Petrosea’s multidisciplinary EPC portfolio and extend its presence across the Asia‑Pacific and Oceania markets, leveraging SBPL’s long‑standing construction expertise.

title: Medco Energi Internasional’s ADRs Begin Trading on OTCQX Best Market under ticker MDCoy
body: The Indonesian energy firm announced that its American Depositary Receipts are now listed on the OTCQX Best Market with the symbol MDCoy, making it the first Indonesian issuer on this tier. CEO Roberto Lorato said the listing improves international investor visibility, liquidity, and supports the company’s long‑term value creation strategy.

title: Posco Group to Conduct Mandatory Tender Offer After Acquiring 65.7% of PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk
body: AGPA Pte. Ltd., a subsidiary of Posco International Corporation, purchased 1.19 billion shares (65.72%) of PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk from Twinwood Family Holding Limited for Rp7,903 per share, amounting to a transaction value of approximately Rp9.44 trillion, with CGS‑CIMB Sekuritas Indonesia and Maybank Sekuritas Indonesia acting as intermediaries. The sale ends the Sampoerna group's ownership in the Indonesian capital market, and the company affirmed that the change of control will not materially affect its operations, legal standing, financial condition, or business continuity.

title: PIMSf Pulogadung expands stake in Geoprima Solusi ahead of mandatory tender offer
body: PIMSf Pulogadung purchased an additional 13,334,800 shares at Rp650 each and 5,333,800 shares at Rp725‑730 each, raising its holding in Geoprima Solusi to 48.45% (323.03 million shares). The company has also committed to a mandatory tender offer for 54.55% of Geoprima Solusi’s shares at Rp436 per share, with the offer period running from 11 November to 11 December 2025.

title: Pesona Sukses Cemerlang sells 11 million Amman Mineral Internasional shares, cutting its holding to 6.29%
body: On 19 November 2025, Pesona Sukses Cemerlang divested 11 million Amman Mineral Internasional shares in a transaction valued at roughly Rp70.12 billion, assisted by Net Sekuritas, Bank Central Asia, Bank DBS Indonesia and Bank Maybank Indonesia. After the sale, its stake fell to 4.55 billion shares (6.29%), while the top shareholders as of 31 October 2025 were Sumber Gemilang (32.174%), Medco (20.915%), AP Investment (15.45%) and the public (18.571%).

title: Goto Group CEO Sugito Walujo Resigns and Hans Patuwo Is Nominated as Successor Ahead of a Dec. 17 Extraordinary Shareholders Meeting
body: Sugito Walujo submitted his resignation as chief executive officer of goto group on November 24, 2025, and the board will nominate Hans Patuwo as the new director and CEO at the extraordinary shareholders meeting scheduled for December 17, 2025. The meeting will also approve the resignations of directors Ade Mulyana, Pablo Malay, and commissioner Winato Kartono, and the appointments of Andre Soelistyo and Santoso Kartono as new commissioners, with the company stating the changes will not adversely affect its operations, legal standing, finances, or business continuity.

title: Director of PT daya intiguna yasa Tbk. Increases Stake in MDIY for Investment Restructuring
body: Edwin Cheah Yew Hong, Director of PT daya intiguna yasa Tbk., acquired 52,786,800 shares of MDIY at Rp1,100 per share on November 20, 2025, totaling Rp58 billion, with the purpose of restructuring personal investment. Following this transaction, his direct ownership in MDIY increased to 98.4 million shares (0.39%) from the previous 45.66 million shares (0.18%).

title: Bumi Resources Tbk. Announces Changes in Board of Directors and Commissioners
body: Bumi Resources Tbk. has announced management changes, with Christopher Fong replacing Ashok Mitra as director. Additionally, Yinbing Ian He has resigned from the board of directors, and Jinping Ma has stepped down from the board of commissioners.

title: Paragon Karya Perkasa Achieves Full 2025 Coal Production Target of 1.1 Million Tons
body: Paragon Karya Perkasa reported that its subsidiary Tri Oetama Persada produced and sold 1.1 million tons of coal in November 2025, meeting 100 % of its 2025 plan and contributing significantly to consolidated revenue. The company aims to double output in 2026 and is seeking approval for a 3 million‑ton budget from the Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources.

title: SLJ Global Plans Private Placement of 632.08 Million Shares
body: SLJ Global intends to issue 632,077,684 new shares with a nominal value of Rp100 each, representing up to 10% of its paid‑in capital, pending approval at an extraordinary shareholders meeting on 19 December 2025. The proceeds will be used to strengthen the capital structure, improve stock liquidity, diversify funding sources, and fund working‑capital needs, equipment upgrades, capacity expansion, and other business development activities.

title: Agustin Widyawati raises stake in Trimegah Karya Pratama to 5.02% through Rp7.37 billion share purchase
body: On 13 November 2025, Agustin Widyawati bought 62.5 million shares of Trimegah Karya Pratama at Rp118 per share, costing Rp7.37 billion and increasing his holding to 100.3 million shares (5.02% ownership). The transaction, described by Trimegah Karya Pratama CEO Hady Kuswanto as an investment for direct ownership, follows a prior stake of 1.89%.

title: Bank Jago approves IDR124.5 billion private placement via employee stock option program
body: Bank Jago will raise IDR124.52 billion by issuing 57.91 million new shares at IDR2,150 each under its Management Employee Stock Option Program (MESOP) from 1 December 2025 to 15 January 2026, split into two tranches of IDR119.15 billion and IDR5.37 billion. The plan, approved at the shareholders meeting on 14 May 2025, allows up to 200 million shares for the 2025‑2030 edition, with proceeds earmarked for business expansion and treated as core capital, and Trimegah Sekuritas has been appointed as the underwriter.

title: Triputra Agro Persada receives IDR 629.28 billion dividend from subsidiaries
body: On 20 November 2025, Triputra Agro Persada recorded a fresh capital injection of IDR 629.28 billion as dividend from its subsidiaries, including Agro Multi Persada, Tri Nur Cakrawala, Tripura Investindo Arya, and Daya Adicipta Mustika. The dividend distribution, approved by shareholders outside the annual general meeting, is not expected to have a material impact on the company's operations, financial condition, or legal standing.

title: PT Lion Metal Works Tbk Receives Director Cheng Zhi Wei’s Resignation, Effective 30 November 2025
body: The company announced that Cheng Zhi Wei submitted his resignation on 20 November 2025, which will take effect on 30 November 2025, pending shareholder approval in accordance with its articles of association and POJK 33/2014 and POJK 31/2015. The resignation is part of a planned board restructuring to be decided at a future RUPS.

title: Three Internal Shareholders of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk. (CYBR) Sell Shares Valued at Approximately Rp1 Billion
body: Between November 17-19, 2025, three internal shareholders of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk. (CYBR) — MB Investment Management Pte. Ltd., Commissioner Andri Hutama Putra, and Director Bambang Susilo — collectively sold shares worth approximately Rp1 billion, leading to a slight reduction in their respective ownerships.

title: PT Jaya Real Property Tbk. (JRPT) Reports Significant Net Profit and Revenue Growth for Q3-2025
body: PT Jaya Real Property Tbk. (JRPT) recorded a 15% increase in net profit attributable to the parent entity, reaching Rp903.10 billion, and a 10.8% rise in revenue to Rp2.26 trillion for Q3-2025. As of September 30, 2025, total assets grew 2.9% to Rp14.51 trillion while total liabilities decreased 3.0% to Rp3.86 trillion, with JRPT shares strengthening 3.12% on Friday.

title: Jhon Veter Firdaus Reagen Buy Shares of PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Jhon Veter Firdaus Reagen bought shares of PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk (FUJI.JK) at approximately IDR 829, increasing holdings from 165,319,600 to 171,389,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT abadi lestari indonesia Tbk (rlco) launches IPO of 625 million shares at Rp150‑168 per share
body: PT Abadi Lestari Indonesia Tbk announced a bookbuilding period from 24‑26 November 2025 for a 20 % primary offering of 625 million shares at Rp150‑Rp168, targeting proceeds of roughly Rp93.75‑105 billion. The net proceeds will be split 56.33 % for working‑capital to purchase bird‑nest raw material and 43.67 % to its subsidiary PT realfood winta asia, while audited figures to 31 May 2025 show assets of Rp685.8 billion, sales up 47.55 % to Rp231.3 billion and net profit soaring 579.4 % to Rp12.38 billion.

title: Jhon Veter Firdaus Reagen Buy Shares of PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Jhon Veter Firdaus Reagen bought shares of PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk (FUJI.JK) at approximately IDR 930, increasing holdings from 171,389,600 to 181,333,300. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Danatama Makmursekuritas, Pt Sell Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Danatama Makmursekuritas, Pt sold shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,460. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Sell Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya sold shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,460, decreasing holdings from 112,491,500 to 109,624,100. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Majukarya Mandiriindonesia, Pt Sell Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Majukarya Mandiriindonesia, Pt sold shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,460. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,460, increasing holdings from 109,624,100 to 110,638,200. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Sutadi Buy Shares of BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Sutadi bought shares of BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk (BFIN.JK) at approximately IDR 772, increasing holdings from 18,817,000 to 19,817,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Sutadi Buy Shares of BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Sutadi bought shares of BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk (BFIN.JK) at approximately IDR 772, increasing holdings from 18,817,000 to 19,817,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia’s Mineral and Coal Directorate Announces Governance Reforms to Boost Mining Efficiency and Energy Security
body: At a business forum in Jakarta, the Directorate General of Minerals and Coal (Ditjen Minerba) outlined reforms such as shifting to annual work‑plan and budget cycles, reducing required permits from 30 to 10, and launching the digital Minerba One platform to enhance transparency, compliance and approval speed. The measures aim to strengthen Indonesia’s energy resilience, support downstream nickel development, and address challenges including global geopolitical tensions, a 1.9% contraction in Q3 2025 mining output and persistent poverty in resource‑rich provinces.

title: PT United Tractors Tbk moves to acquire Doup gold mine for $540 million and eyes Australian assets as part of non‑coal diversification
body: PT United Tractors Tbk announced a conditional share purchase agreement to acquire 99,99996 % of PT J Resources Asia Pasifik Tbk’s Doup gold mine in North Sulawesi, valuing the transaction at USD 540 million with completion targeted by 23 December 2025. The company also opened a representative office in Perth to explore Australian gold assets and is expanding renewable‑energy projects, including an 18‑MW Aceh hydropower plant and the Besai Kemu mini‑hydro facility expected to generate up to 45 GWh by end‑2025, as it pursues a 50:50 coal‑non‑coal revenue mix by 2030.

title: Indonesia’s downstream investment reaches IDR 431.4 trillion in Jan‑Sept 2025, up 58% YoY, after raw‑material export ban
body: Deputy Minister Todotua Pasaribu said the surge, driven mainly by the mineral sector and backed by a roadmap covering 28 priority commodities, reflects the government’s push for domestic processing of natural resources. Major firms such as PT Vale Indonesia, PT Timah and PT Freeport Indonesia are expanding downstream projects in nickel, tin, copper and aluminum to boost added value, export potential and job creation.

title: PT Sun Energy rolls out solar and battery solutions to decarbonise Indonesia’s mining sector
body: PT Sun Energy announced integrated solar power plants, battery storage and roll‑up PV systems – in partnership with PT Cipta Kridatama and at PT Berau Coal – to cut diesel‑driven fuel costs that can reach 40‑50% of mining operating expenses. The company also plans fleet‑electrification and water‑reuse services, with pilot electric‑vehicle operations slated for late 2025, supporting Indonesia’s net‑zero 2060 target and Good Mining Practice law.

title: Indonesia's Weda Bay Industrial Park secures $8 billion for nickel, renewable energy and aluminium projects, spurring North Maluku growth
body: Indonesia's Weda Bay Industrial Park (iwip) announced a total of about USD 8 billion (IDR 133.76 trillion) in new downstream clean‑energy projects—including a USD 5 billion nickel‑battery ecosystem, a USD 2 billion 2‑GW solar/500‑MW wind and solar‑panel plant, and a USD 1 billion electrolytic aluminium smelter—targeting first production between late 2025 and early 2026. In Q2 2025, North Maluku’s investment reached IDR 19 trillion, its economy grew 32.09% YoY with manufacturing at 40.11% of GDP, and PT weda bay nickel and iwip employed over 81,000 direct workers, 80% from the region.

title: PT merdeka gold resources Tbk starts ore feeding at Pani Gold Mine, targeting first gold pour in Q1 2026
body: Ore feeding at the Pani Gold Mine in Gorontalo began on 12 November 2025, marking the transition from construction to operation for PT merdeka gold resources Tbk’s Emas project, which aims for its first gold pour in the first quarter of 2026. The mine holds 190 million tonnes of ore reserves containing approximately 4.8 million ounces of gold and will use renewable electricity under a PLN certificate scheme.

title: Mind ID expands alumina refinery and aluminum smelter capacity to boost Indonesia’s bauxite downstreaming
body: Mind ID announced the expansion of its Mempawah, West Kalimantan alumina refinery to 1 million tons per year and a Phase II SGAR project adding another 1 million tons of alumina plus a 600,000‑ton aluminum smelter, using domestic bauxite reserves. The company produced 1.33 million tons of bauxite in 2024 and says the downstream integration supports Indonesia’s energy‑transition goals.

title: PT Vale Indonesia Tbk showcases low-carbon nickel strategy and ESG progress at COP30, highlighting partnership with Huayou Indonesia
body: At the COP30 climate forum, PT Vale Indonesia Tbk outlined its plan to cut absolute emissions by 33% by 2030 and reduce nickel product‑carbon intensity by 50% through technologies such as heat recovery, off‑gas utilization and electrified processing, supported by downstream partner Huayou Indonesia’s hydrometallurgical innovations that can lower emissions by over 2 tCO₂e per ton of nickel. The company also announced its best‑ever Sustainalytics ESG risk rating of 23.7, positioning it among the top performers in the global diversified metals and mining sector.

title: Indonesia's Cesium-137 Task Force Temporarily Suspends PT Luckione Environmental Science Indonesia Over Contaminated Imports
body: The Cesium‑137 Task Force has temporarily halted operations of PT Luckione Environmental Science Indonesia after four shipments contaminated with cesium‑137 were discovered at Tanjung Priok port, including zinc powder from Angola and earlier shipments from the Philippines. The suspension will remain while the government completes an environmental audit, and the task force is also addressing other contaminated goods such as footwear from the United States and shrimp containers destined for export.

title: Indonesia finalizes export duty formula for gold, coal and other minerals, targeting up to Rp6 trillion in extra revenue
body: Indonesia's Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources announced a finalized export duty formula for coal, gold and other minerals, with gold duties set at 7.5‑15% and linked to global price movements. The government expects the new duties, to be codified in a 2026 finance regulation, to generate about Rp6 trillion in additional revenue.

title: Indonesia reaffirms rice import ban after seizure of 250‑ton illegal shipment in Sabang
body: Agriculture Minister Andi Amran Sulaiman said the government sealed a warehouse of PT MSG that imported 250 tons of rice from Thailand without a permit, reaffirming the ban because national stocks are projected at 34.77 million tons in 2025. He added that the ban has helped lower global rice prices and that Indonesia has no need to import.

title: Adhi Karya receives two PKPU applications filed by Interdesign Cipta Optima, PT Dinamika Prakarsa Mukti and PT Sinergi Karya Sejahtera, but asserts no negative impact pending court verification
body: Two PKPU requests (case numbers 373/2025 and 374/2025) were lodged at the Jakarta Central District Court against Adhi Karya, naming it as the respondent. The company says it has not yet received official court notification, will conduct a thorough verification once notified, and assures stakeholders that the matter will not adversely affect the firm.

title: Flying Fox Nusantara acquires 5.24% stake in DMS Propertindo for Rp20.45 billion
body: On 21 November 2025, Flying Fox Nusantara purchased 552.92 million shares (5.24%) of DMS Propertindo for approximately Rp20.45 billion, with the transaction facilitated by Sucor Sekuritas, OCBC Sekuritas Indonesia, and Bank Pembangunan Daerah Jawa Barat dan Banten. Prior to the deal, DMS Propertindo’s shareholding comprised DMS Investama (38%), Mohamad Prapanca (10,000 shares), and the public (62%).

title: Superbank plans Indonesia IPO targeting up to IDR 3.1 trillion
body: Superbank, formerly Bank Fama International, announced plans for an initial public offering on the Indonesia Stock Exchange, seeking IDR 2.3‑3.1 trillion by issuing up to 4.40 billion new shares at IDR 525‑695 each (13 % of paid‑up capital) with Mandiri Sekuritas, Suco Sekuritas, Trimegah Sekuritas and CLSA Sekuritas Indonesia as underwriters and a timeline from 25 Nov to 17 Dec 2025. Post‑IPO, major shareholders will include Elang Media Visitama (27.07 %), Kudo Teknologi Indonesia (16.67 %), A5–DB Holdings (10.03 %), GXS Bank (10.44 %), Kakaobank (8.66 %) and Singtel Alpha Investments (7.36 %), while the bank continues its digital transformation driven by Emtek Group, Grab Holdings Ltd and Singtel.

title: Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera denies takeover rumors by Widodo Makmur Unggas
body: Management of nusantara sawit sejahtera refuted reports that its subsidiary nsp is being targeted for acquisition by Widodo Makmur Unggas, stating the information is false and no corporate actions are planned. The company said the rumor has no negative impact on its operations, legal standing, financial condition, or business continuity.

title: Nusantara Utama Investama cuts Bukit Uluwatu stake by 1.49 billion shares, valuing the sale at roughly IDR 1.53 trillion
body: On 21 November 2025, Nusantara Utama Investama sold 1.49 billion Bukit Uluwatu shares through a silent transaction assisted by CGS International Sekuritas Indonesia, valuing the deal at about IDR 1.53 trillion based on a closing price of IDR 1,030 per share. The sale reduced its holding to 15.17 billion shares (61.64% of total), a 6.07% dilution from the prior 16.66 billion shares (67.71%).

title: UBS Group AG Reduces Stake in Bumi Resources by Selling 3.57 Billion Shares for Rp809.22 Billion
body: UBS Group AG sold 3.57 billion shares of Bumi Resources on November 17, 2025, at Rp226,5891 per share, generating Rp809.22 billion. This transaction reduced UBS Group AG's ownership in Bumi Resources by 0.96% to 7.09% (26.31 billion shares) and was conducted for client derivative hedging activities.

title: PT Sawit Sumbermas Sarana Tbk Completes Rp1.6 Trillion Acquisition of PT Sawit Mandiri Lestari from Parent Company PT Citra Borneo Indah
body: PT Sawit Sumbermas Sarana Tbk officially completed the Rp1.6 trillion acquisition of 98,328 shares of PT Sawit Mandiri Lestari from its parent company, PT Citra Borneo Indah, on November 24, 2025. This affiliated transaction, funded by a syndicated bank loan, aligns with PT Sawit Sumbermas Sarana Tbk's expansion strategy and follows its Q3 2025 net profit of Rp1.00 trillion.

title: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk Secures USD 625 Million Loan for Mine Development and Exploration
body: PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk (BRMS) secured a new long-term loan facility totaling USD 625 million, equivalent to Rp10.43 trillion, from a banking syndicate comprising bangkok bank public company limited, bank permata, bank mega, and bca. This funding will accelerate the development of the Palu underground gold mine, including increasing plant capacity and completing construction, and finance extensive mineral exploration in Gorontalo, Aceh, and Banten through its subsidiaries PT citra palu minerals, PT gorontalo minerals, PT linge mineral resources, and PT suma heksa sinergi.

title: PT Jaya Sukses Makmur Sentosa Tbk. (RISE) Plans to Distribute Up to 5.25 Billion Bonus Shares to Strengthen Capital and Liquidity
body: PT Jaya Sukses Makmur Sentosa Tbk. (Tanrise Property), with ticker RISE, plans to distribute up to 5.25 billion bonus shares from its additional paid-in capital, pending an EGMS on January 8, 2026, to strengthen its capital structure and boost stock liquidity. This corporate action, with a proposed 25:12 ratio, aims to provide investor value without dilution, while RISE's stock rose 0.64% to Rp11,750 today, despite recent declines.

title: PT Selamat Sempurna Tbk (SMSM) Receives Rp59.17 Billion in Dividends Amidst Declining Stock Performance
body: PT Selamat Sempurna Tbk (SMSM), an automotive parts issuer, received Rp59.17 billion in dividends from two subsidiaries on November 24, 2025, which strengthens the company's cash position. Despite this, SMSM's stock price fell 1.70% to Rp1,730 per share on Monday, reflecting a broader decline over the past week, month, six months, and year-to-date.

title: PT Sentra Investama Maksima Divests Shares in PT Lavender Bina Candika Tbk (BMBL) for Liquidity Needs
body: PT Sentra Investama Maksima, the controlling shareholder of PT Lavender Bina Candika Tbk (BMBL), sold a total of 5,264,500 shares in two tranches between October and November 2025 for liquidity purposes. This divestment reduced its ownership from 10.29% to 10.01%, even as BMBL's shares climbed 8.47% on Monday.

title: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BBRI) Drives Growth Through Diversification and 'Brivolution Reignite' While Reporting Strong Q3 2025 Results
body: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia Tbk (BRI) is expanding its business through diversification and the “Brivolution Reignite” program, focusing on strengthening its consumer segment, developing bullion services via Pegadaian, and leveraging subsidiaries like BRI Finance for vehicle financing. The company reported a solid financial performance for Q3 2025, with total third-party funds growing 8.2% YoY to Rp1,474.8 trillion, credit disbursement up 6.3% YoY to Rp1,438.1 trillion, and a net profit of Rp41.2 trillion.

title: PT Chandra Daya Investasi Tbk. (CDIA) Activates 4.7 MWp Solar Power Plant via PT Krakatau Chandra Energi, Boosting Total Capacity to 11 MWp
body: PT Chandra Daya Investasi Tbk. (CDIA) officially commenced operations of a 4.7 MWp solar power plant through its subsidiary, PT Krakatau Chandra Energi, on November 17, 2025, one week ahead of schedule, bringing the CDI Group's total installed solar capacity to 11 MWp. This facility, aimed at providing clean energy for national industry and reducing carbon emissions by 5,086.74 tons CO2eq annually, saw CDIA shares rise 3.05% to Rp1,860 per share.

title: Fuji Finance Indonesia Partners with energy absolute public company limited to Enter Green Financing for Electric Mobility
body: Fuji Finance Indonesia signed an MOU with energy absolute public company limited, a sustainable energy company from Thailand, to explore financing for electric vehicles and supporting infrastructure in Indonesia, marking fuji's first international partnership and a strategic shift towards green financing. This collaboration follows fuji's stable performance in the first nine months of 2025, with total assets reaching Rp187.57 miliar, exceeding annual targets.

title: Bank BCA to Distribute Interim Dividend of Rp6.77 Trillion for Fiscal Year 2025
body: Bank BCA plans to distribute an interim dividend of Rp6.77 trillion, or Rp55 per share, representing 15.6% of its Rp43.39 trillion net profit as of September 30, 2025. The cum dividend date for the regular and negotiation market is December 2, 2025, with the dividend payment scheduled for December 22, 2025.

title: Chandra Daya Investasi Injects USD 140 Million into Affiliates aster port terminal pte ltd and aster power pte ltd
body: Chandra Daya Investasi executed a USD 140 million capital injection into its affiliates, aster port terminal pte ltd (USD 80 million) and aster power pte ltd (USD 60 million), with agreements signed on November 20, 2025, and durations until November 30, 2035. This strategic funding aims to support the optimal and sustainable operations of these chandra asri pacific entities, which are developing businesses in hazardous goods storage and electricity in Singapore, while strengthening group synergy and expansion.

title: Merdeka Materials Plans Rp3.1 Trillion Debt Issuance Comprising Bonds and Sukuk Mudharabah
body: Merdeka Materials will issue Rp2.1 trillion in bonds and Rp1 trillion in sukuk mudharabah, with maturities up to five years and interest/profit-sharing rates ranging from 7.50% to 8.25% per annum. The bond proceeds will primarily repay a USD121.0 million debt facility, while sukuk funds will finance its subsidiary merdeka tsingshan indonesia for facility development and working capital.

title: Maulana Muhammad Resigns, Yuliana Appointed as President Director of PT batulicin nusantara maritim Tbk
body: PT Batulicin Nusantara Maritim Tbk reported that Maulana Muhammad resigned as President Director on November 22, 2025. Yuliana was subsequently appointed as the new President Director, effective November 24, 2025, replacing Maulana Muhammad, with changes also announced for the Board of Commissioners.

title: PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama Buy Shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Samuel Tumbuh Bersama bought shares of PT Nusantara Sawit Sejahtera Tbk (NSSS.JK) at approximately IDR 375, increasing holdings from 8,502,635,600 to 8,586,201,800. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Ubs Group Ag Sell Shares of Bumi Resources Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Ubs Group Ag sold shares of Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI.JK) at approximately IDR 227, decreasing holdings from 29,891,179,063 to 26,319,836,763. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Patrick Rudolf Dannacher Buy Shares of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk.
body: According to the published announcement, Patrick Rudolf Dannacher bought shares of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk. (CYBR.JK) at approximately IDR 1,417, increasing holdings from 191,129,122 to 191,196,222. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Dr. Pulung Peranginangin Buy Shares of Gema Grahasarana Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Dr. Pulung Peranginangin buy shares of Gema Grahasarana Tbk (GEMA.JK) at approximately IDR 94, increasing holdings from 7,660,000 to 7,760,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Bakrie Metalindustries, Pt Buy Shares of PT VKTR Teknologi Mobilitas Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Bakrie Metalindustries, Pt bought shares of PT VKTR Teknologi Mobilitas Tbk (VKTR.JK) at approximately IDR 496, increasing holdings from 6,852,846,600 to 6,858,620,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Nusantara Utamainvestama Sell Shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Nusantara Utamainvestama sold shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk (BUVA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,145, decreasing holdings from 16,668,137,011 to 15,173,281,772. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Bank Of Singaporelimited Buy Shares of Maskapai Reasuransi Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Bank Of Singaporelimited bought shares of Maskapai Reasuransi Indonesia Tbk (MREI.JK) at approximately IDR 980, increasing holdings from 32,702,655 to 32,752,655. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Astini Bernawati Oudang Buy Shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Astini Bernawati Oudang bought shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk (BUVA.JK) at approximately IDR 150. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Nusantara Utama Investama Sell Shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Nusantara Utama Investama sold shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk (BUVA.JK) at approximately IDR 150, decreasing holdings from 16,688,137,011 to 15,173,281,772. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Hendry Utomo Buy Shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hendry Utomo bought shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk (BUVA.JK) at approximately IDR 150. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Satrio Buy Shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Satrio bought shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk (BUVA.JK) at approximately IDR 150. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Andhesti Tungkas Pratama Sell Shares of Darma Henwa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Andhesti Tungkas Pratama sold shares of Darma Henwa Tbk (DEWA.JK) at approximately IDR 416, decreasing holdings from 4,785,666,666 to 4,140,782,066. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Cindy Budijono Buy Shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Cindy Budijono bought shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk (BUVA.JK) at approximately IDR 150. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Yos Effendi Susanto Buy Shares of PT Famon Awal Bros Sedaya Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Yos Effendi Susanto bought shares of PT Famon Awal Bros Sedaya Tbk (PRAY.JK) at approximately IDR 795, increasing holdings from 114,562,000 to 117,912,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Verah Wahyudi S. Wong Buy Shares of PT Remala Abadi Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Verah Wahyudi S. Wong buy shares of PT Remala Abadi Tbk (DATA.JK) at approximately IDR 4,245, increasing holdings from 551,133,100 to 551,233,100. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Olive Power Invest Buy Shares of PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Olive Power Invest bought shares of PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk (OLIV.JK) at approximately IDR 17,500,000,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Erwin Ciputra Buy Shares of PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Erwin Ciputra bought shares of PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (TPIA.JK) at approximately IDR 7,161, increasing holdings from 139,408,272 to 139,858,272. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Hendro Jap Sell Shares of PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hendro Jap sold shares of PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk (OLIV.JK) at approximately IDR 15,676,000,000, decreasing holdings from 1,175,000,000 to 19,842,888. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Cakra Bhakti Para Putra Buy Shares of PT Steel Pipe Industry of Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Cakra Bhakti Para Putra bought shares of PT Steel Pipe Industry of Indonesia Tbk (ISSP.JK) at approximately IDR 434, increasing holdings from 4,126,868,030 to 4,130,389,330. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Palm oil producers seek EU clarity on Deforestation Regulation amid Austrian timeline pause request
body: A high‑level delegation of the Council of Palm Oil Producing Countries (CPOPC) warned that the EU’s Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) timeline uncertainty, heightened by Austria’s call to stop the clock, could jeopardise compliance investments and market stability, despite progress such as Malaysia’s e‑MSPO platform and Indonesia’s ISPO certification covering over 6.2 million hectares. The European Commission rejected the pause, emphasizing the need for predictable rules before the 15 December deadline, while stakeholders highlighted technical challenges and potential impacts on smallholders.

title: Indonesia's Manpower Minister calls for new regulations to protect gig workers' rights and income
body: Minister Yassierli announced plans to revise Law No. 13 of 2003 to extend basic rights such as social security, fair wages and transparent contracts to the estimated 4.4 million gig workers in sectors like transport, logistics and digital services, and to require platforms to provide health insurance, training and timely payments. The proposal also seeks mechanisms for fair dispute resolution between workers and platforms.

title: Indonesia aims to sign a preferential trade agreement with Tunisia in January 2026
body: Indonesian Trade Minister Budi Santoso announced that the Indonesia–Tunisia Preferential Trade Agreement will be signed in January 2026, covering tariff reductions, non‑tariff measures and support for MSMEs. He highlighted 2024 trade with Tunisia at US$169.3 million and a 7.72% rise in Indonesia’s export value to US$185.13 billion for January–August 2025, alongside a US$29.14 billion trade surplus.

title: PT Indofood Sukses Makmur Tbk (INDF) reports Q3 2025 profit decline despite strong agribusiness revenue growth
body: PT Indofood Sukses Makmur Tbk (INDF) posted Q3‑2025 revenue of Rp 90.98 trillion, up 4.64% YoY, but net profit fell 10% to Rp 7.88 trillion, driven by a near‑doubling of financial costs and an unrealised foreign‑exchange loss. Analysts highlight the agribusiness segment’s 32.5% YoY revenue growth, higher CPO prices and project 2025 revenue of Rp 121.52 trillion and net profit of Rp 10.2 trillion, maintaining buy ratings with target prices between Rp 8,600 and Rp 11,200.

title: PT Sawit Sumbermas Sarana Tbk completes Rp1.6 trillion acquisition of PT Sawit Mandiri Lestari, funded by Rp5.2 trillion syndicated loan
body: PT Sawit Sumbermas Sarana Tbk (SSMS) finalized the purchase of 98,328 shares of PT Sawit Mandiri Lestari (SML) from its majority shareholder PT Citra Borneo Indah (CBI) on 24 Nov 2025 for Rp1.6 trillion, financing the deal through a new Rp5.2 trillion syndicated loan led by Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BBRI). The deal follows strong Q3 2025 results—net profit of Rp1 trillion (EPS Rp105.40) and revenue of Rp11.01 trillion, up from Rp609.3 billion and Rp7.38 trillion a year earlier—and analysts from Mirae Asset Sekuritas and MNC Sekuritas maintain bullish outlooks with price targets around Rp1,670‑1,710 per share.

title: Indonesia's IHSG dips 0.56% to 8,521.88 as banks fall, but analysts see a healthy correction within a bullish trend
body: The Jakarta Composite Index (IHSG) closed at 8,521.88, down 0.56% driven by banking stocks and profit‑taking after Monday’s rally, while technical analyst Reza Diofanda said the index remains above key support (8,440‑8,500) and could stay bullish. He highlighted upcoming US retail sales and PPI data as potential catalysts and recommended buying PT Solusi Sinergi Digital Tbk (WIFI), PT Merdeka Battery Materials Tbk (MBMA) and PT Apexindo Pratama Duta Tbk (APEX) within the specified price ranges.

title: PT Morris Capital Indonesia increases its stake in PT Multi Makmur Lemindo Tbk (PIPA) to 49.92% through a two‑tranche share purchase
body: On 24 November 2025, PT Morris Capital Indonesia bought an additional 2,000,000 shares of PT Multi Makmur Lemindo Tbk (PIPA) at Rp290 and Rp300 per share, raising its direct ownership from 49.86% to 49.92%. The purchase was reported to OJK in compliance with POJK No.4/2024, following earlier acquisitions that brought its total holding to just under 50%.

title: KPK detains two former PT PP executives over alleged Rp25 billion fictitious project procurement
body: The Corruption Eradication Commission (KPK) has detained former Senior Vice President Didik Mardiyanto and former Senior Finance Manager Herry Nurdy Nasution of PT PP on suspicion of a fictitious Rp25 billion EPC project procurement. PT PP said the investigation, which began in December 2024, has not materially affected its operations and no final court ruling has been issued.

title: PT Bank KB Indonesia Tbk adopts asset‑swap to replace write‑off loans with PT TBS Energi Utama Tbk sukuk, targeting Rp1 trillion asset recovery by 2025
body: PT Bank KB Indonesia Tbk (BBKP) announced it will swap non‑productive write‑off loan assets for sukuk issued by PT TBS Energi Utama Tbk (TOBA) as part of its asset‑quality improvement strategy. The bank aims to recover roughly Rp1 trillion of low‑quality assets by year‑end 2025, increase the share of productive assets and support higher net interest margin growth.

title: Pollux Hotels Group (POLI) to Issue idAAA‑Rated Sustainable 2025 Bond Backed by Full CGIF Guarantee
body: Pollux Hotels Group (POLI) is launching a Rp500 billion sustainable bond for 2025 that received an idAAA rating from Pemeringkat Efek Indonesia (Pefindo) and is fully guaranteed by the Credit Guarantee and Investment Facility (CGIF) of the Asian Development Bank (ADB), with coupons of 5.35‑5.85% for a 3‑year series and 5.75‑6.25% for a 5‑year series paid quarterly. The proceeds will be allocated 90% to refinance a Bank Mandiri loan and 10% to working capital, operational costs and a carbon‑reduction program, with the offering scheduled from 25 Nov to 11 Dec 2025.

title: Widodo Makmur Perkasa posts 87.2% revenue growth and records positive EBITDA in the first nine months of 2025
body: Widodo Makmur Perkasa (WMPP) reported revenue of Rp697.6 billion for the first nine months of 2025, up 87.2% year‑on‑year, with EBITDA turning positive at Rp5.6 billion after a loss of Rp87.8 billion in the same period last year; net loss narrowed 44% to Rp173.6 billion. The company attributes the turnaround to stronger poultry sales, efficiency measures, asset divestments and a post‑homologation PKPU plan to restructure cash flow and seek new working‑capital funding.

title: MD Entertainment (FILM) Secures Rp200 Billion Credit Facility from Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BBRI)
body: MD Entertainment (FILM) has secured a Rp200 billion credit facility from Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BBRI), with the agreement signed on November 21, 2025, for a one-year term. This facility is intended to enhance the company's financial capacity for business development and continuity, and is not expected to negatively impact its operations or financial condition.

title: Andhesti Tungkas Pratama Divests 644.84 Million Shares of Darma Henwa (DEWA) for Rp268.25 Billion
body: Andhesti Tungkas Pratama divested 644,849,600 shares of Darma Henwa (DEWA) on November 21, 2025, at Rp416 per share, generating Rp268.25 billion. This transaction reduced Andhesti's direct ownership in Darma Henwa to 10.18%, a 1.58% dilution from the prior 11.76%.

title: Lavender Bina Candika (BMBL) Stock Jumps After Controller Divestment, Amidst Active Acquisition Market
body: Sentra Investama Maksima (SIM) divested 2,974,500 shares of Lavender Bina Candika (BMBL), reducing its ownership to 10.01%, yet BMBL's stock unexpectedly surged 9.38% to Rp70 on November 25, 2025. This unusual market response, occurring amidst a trend of corporate acquisitions affecting companies like Buana Artha Anugerah (STAR) and Sampoerna Agro (SGRO), suggests potential changes in BMBL's ownership structure, especially given its current PBV of 1.03x.

title: Yupi Indo (YUPI) to Distribute Rp300 Billion Interim Dividend for Fiscal Year 2025
body: Yupi Indo (YUPI) announced an interim dividend distribution of Rp300 billion, translating to Rp35 per share, for the 2025 fiscal year. This amount represents approximately 63.44% of its Rp472.9 billion net profit as of September 30, 2025, with the recording date set for December 5, 2025, and payment on December 18, 2025.

title: Buma Internasional Grup (DOID) Subsidiary Plans USD500 Million Debt Issuance for Refinancing and Capital Expenditure
body: Bukit Makmur Mandiri Utama, a subsidiary of Buma Internasional Grup (DOID), intends to issue USD500 million in 5-year debt securities, listed on SGX-ST, to refinance USD223 million in existing debts and allocate USD150 million for capital expenditure and working capital. These securities, bearing a 10% annual interest and unconditionally guaranteed by Buma Australia, aim to enhance liquidity and debt maturity, backed by the group's orderbook exceeding USD8.5 billion as of June 2025.

title: Bukit Uluwatu (BUVA) Executives Acquire 26.66 Million Shares at Discounted Price While Controlling Shareholder Sells to Increase Free Float
body: On November 21, 2025, several executives of Bukit Uluwatu (BUVA), including Direktur Utama Satrio and Komisaris Utama Astini B Oudang, collectively purchased 26.66 million shares at Rp150 per share, representing an 85.43% discount from the market closing price, for a total of approximately Rp4 billion. Concurrently, the controlling shareholder, linked to Happy Hapsoro (Puan Maharani's investment company), divested 1.49 billion shares at the same Rp150 price to enhance free float liquidity in the market.

title: Hans Patuwo Appointed as New President Director and CEO of PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk
body: Hans Patuwo is slated to become the new President Director and CEO of PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk (GOTO), succeeding Patrick Walujo, with his appointment effective following Walujo's resignation on November 24, 2025. Patuwo brings nearly eight years of leadership experience within the Gojek–GoTo ecosystem, having held strategic roles including Director and Chief Operating Officer of GoTo, and various positions in entities such as PT Indah Kiat Pulp & Paper Tbk, PT Dompet Karya Anak Bangsa, and PT Midtrans.

title: PT Global Digital Niaga Tbk. (BELI) to Simultaneously Open MESOP III Tahap III and MESOP IV Tahap I from December 2025 to January 2026.
body: PT Global Digital Niaga Tbk. (BELI) will open its Management and Employee Stock Option Plan (MESOP) III Tahap III and MESOP IV Tahap I concurrently from December 15, 2025, to January 13, 2026. Both programs have an exercise price of Rp436 per share, with 1,283,677,301 shares available for MESOP III Tahap III and up to 1,300,000,000 shares for MESOP IV Tahap I.

title: Rudi Reksa Sutantra Reduces Stake in PT Atlantis Subsea Indonesia Tbk. (ATLA) Through Share Sales Valued at Rp1.17 Billion
body: Rudi Reksa Sutantra, the controlling shareholder of PT Atlantis Subsea Indonesia Tbk. (ATLA), sold a total of 18,400,000 ATLA shares between November 17-20, 2025, for an aggregate value of Rp1,1736 billion. This transaction decreased his ownership from 48.78% to 48.74%, with ATLA shares closing at Rp62 on November 25.

title: PT Teknologi Karya Digital Nusa Tbk. Partners with National Battery Research Institute to Accelerate EV and BESS Infrastructure Development
body: PT Teknologi Karya Digital Nusa Tbk. (TRON) has officially partnered with National Battery Research Institute (NBRI) through an MoU to accelerate the development of digitalized electric vehicle infrastructure and Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS) in Indonesia. This collaboration encompasses smart mobility technology, battery research and development, testing, and human resource capacity building, aiming to strengthen Indonesia's EV ecosystem and support TRON's vision as a leader in Battery as a Service and Green Mobility.

title: Jhon Veter Firdaus Reagen Increases Stake in PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk. (FUJI)
body: Jhon Veter Firdaus Reagen purchased 9,943,700 shares of PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk. (FUJI) for Rp9,247,641,000 on November 24, 2025, increasing his total ownership to 181,333,300 shares or 13.94%. This latest acquisition follows several previous investments in FUJI and he also holds a 5.01% stake in PT Brigit Biofarmaka Teknologi Tbk (OBAT).

title: PT Sarimelati Kencana Tbk. (PZZA) Conducts Board and Commissioner Reshuffle
body: PT Sarimelati Kencana Tbk (PZZA), the operator of Pizza Hut Indonesia outlets, held an Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders (RUPSLB) on November 20, 2025, to approve changes in its board of directors and commissioners. The meeting accepted the resignation of Emireza Mohammad Arifin, appointed Hadian Iswara and Stephen James McCarthy as Commissioners, and named Boy Ardhitya Lukito as the new President Director, with all appointments effective until the Annual GMS 2027.

title: Hioe Mie Tjen Sell Shares of PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hioe Mie Tjen sold shares of PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk (OLIV.JK) at approximately IDR 1,824,000,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Cakra Bhakti Para Putra Buy Shares of PT Steel Pipe Industry of Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Cakra Bhakti Para Putra bought shares of PT Steel Pipe Industry of Indonesia Tbk (ISSP.JK) at approximately IDR 434, increasing holdings from 4,126,868,030 to 4,130,389,330. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Arkora Hydro Tbk. Unveils Hydropower Expansion Plans and Reports Significant Q3 2025 Growth
body: PT Arkora Hydro Tbk presented its hydropower project development and expansion plans, targeting staged operations from late 2025 to 2026 and aiming to develop over 300 MW. The company reported a 61% revenue increase to Rp247 billion and a net profit of Rp47.7 billion for Q3 2025, driven by increased electricity production and construction services.

title: Controlling Shareholder PT Cakra Bhakti Para Putra Boosts Ownership in PT Steel Pipe Industry of Indonesia Tbk. (ISSP)
body: PT Cakra Bhakti Para Putra, the controlling shareholder of PT Steel Pipe Industry of Indonesia Tbk. (ISSP), increased its ownership by acquiring 3,521,300 shares for Rp1.53 billion on November 25, 2025, following a previous purchase of 6,262,200 shares for Rp2.59 billion on November 11, 2025. These two transactions, totaling approximately Rp4.12 billion, were made for investment purposes, raising PT Cakra Bhakti Para Putra's stake in ISSP to 57.48% from 57.43%.

title: Yos Effendi Susanto Increases Stake in PT Famon Awal Bros Sedaya Tbk. (PRAY) with Rp2.66 Billion Share Purchase
body: Yos Effendi Susanto, the controlling shareholder of PT Famon Awal Bros Sedaya Tbk. (PRAY), purchased 3,350,000 shares on November 18, 2025, at Rp795 per share, totaling Rp2,663,250,000. This personal transaction increased his direct ownership in PRAY from 0.82% to 0.84%.

title: PT Raharja Energi Cepu Tbk. (RATU) Secures Future Profitability Through Stable Blok Cepu Production and Strategic Acquisitions
body: PT Raharja Energi Cepu Tbk (RATU) affirmed that Blok Cepu's production, which increased to 156,000 barel per hari in 2025 and has surpassed 600 million cumulative barrels, is expected to maintain profitability until 2027-2028. Concurrently, RATU is actively pursuing strategic acquisitions of domestic and international oil and gas fields, with one participating interest acquisition in its final stages, while also exploring early-stage clean energy initiatives.

title: Hendro Jap Sell Shares of PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hendro Jap sold shares of PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk (OLIV.JK) at approximately IDR 15,676,000,000, decreasing holdings from 1,175,000,000 to 19,842,888. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Apratima Sejahtera - Junita Ciputra Buy Shares of Metropolitan Kentjana Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Apratima Sejahtera - Junita Ciputra bought shares of Metropolitan Kentjana Tbk (MKPI.JK) at approximately IDR 24,000, increasing holdings from 74,857,527 to 74,858,027. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Hapsoro Sell Shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hapsoro sold shares of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk (BUVA.JK) at approximately IDR 839, decreasing holdings from 110,845,049 to 60,845,049. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Tunggal Jaya Investama Sell Shares of PT Impack Pratama Industri Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Tunggal Jaya Investama sold shares of PT Impack Pratama Industri Tbk (IMPC.JK) at approximately IDR 2,300, decreasing holdings from 21,459,426,194 to 20,759,426,194. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Febyan Buy Shares of PT Indonesia Pondasi Raya Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Febyan bought shares of PT Indonesia Pondasi Raya Tbk (IDPR.JK) at approximately IDR 288, increasing holdings from 55,199,600 to 55,476,900. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesian President Prabowo orders a 1‑MW solar plant for every village to boost the clean‑energy transition
body: President Prabowo Subianto directed the construction of 1‑megawatt solar power plants in each Indonesian village, with the Energy and Mineral Resources Ministry finalising financing and implementation plans and targeting a total capacity of 100 GW. The programme is designed to support village cooperatives, improve energy security and stimulate domestic battery‑industry investment by storing excess solar output for night‑time use.

title: Indonesia announces toll, transport and airline discounts for Christmas 2025 and New Year 2026 holidays
body: The Indonesian government will provide 10‑20% toll road discounts on Dec 22, 23 and 31 2025 across 26 sections, and 20% discounts on sea travel with PT Pelayaran Nasional Indonesia (Pelni) and PT ASDP Indonesia Ferry from Dec 17 2025 to Jan 10 2026. Economy‑class train tickets will be cut by about 30% and air‑fare by 13‑14% for the same period, as announced by Coordinating Minister for Economic Affairs Airlangga Hartarto and Deputy Minister of Transportation Suntana.

title: Indonesia's Agrarian Minister backs Bali's plan to halt productive land conversion and create new rice fields
body: Minister Nusron Wahid endorsed Bali's proposal to stop converting productive land for commercial use and called for the creation of roughly 6,000 hectares of new rice fields to meet the LP2B and KP2B thresholds, warning that illegal conversion can carry up to five years in prison. He also stressed the need to balance agriculture, tourism and housing, urging vertical development on unproductive land.

title: Indonesia pushes nature-based solutions in forestry to monetize carbon and boost green-economy transition
body: Deputy Forestry Minister Rohmat Marzuki announced at the Global Carbon Summit Indonesia 2025 that the country aims to sequester carbon on about 12 million hectares through afforestation, reforestation and revegetation, with the Social Forestry program covering 8.3 million hectares and plans to recognize 1.4 million hectares of customary forests. He highlighted a potential carbon value of 13.4 billion tonnes CO₂e from 2024‑2050 and cited new regulations and partnerships with the Integrity Council for the Voluntary Carbon Market (ICVCM) and the International Emissions Trading Association (IETA) to ensure transparent carbon trading.

title: Indonesia's BEI lifts suspension on PT Arkora Hydro Tbk., PT Janu Putra Sejahtera Tbk., and PT Nusa Raya Cipta Tbk., with stocks surging on resumption
body: The Indonesia Stock Exchange (BEI) resumed trading of PT Arkora Hydro Tbk. (ARKO), PT Janu Putra Sejahtera Tbk. (AYAM), and PT Nusa Raya Cipta Tbk. (NRCA) on Thursday, 27 November 2025, after a one‑day suspension intended to curb price volatility. On the opening, NRCA jumped 15.38% to Rp1,800, ARKO rose 24.37% to Rp4,900, while AYAM slipped 3.59% to Rp322, following prior aggressive gains of 105.26% (NRCA), 151.13% (AYAM) and 328.26% (ARKO) year‑to‑date.

title: Indonesia Stock Exchange suspends trading of PT Bintang Oto Global Tbk., PT Bhakti Mulia Artha Tbk., and PT Personel Alih Daya Tbk. after aggressive price spikes
body: On 27 November 2025, the Bursa Efek Indonesia halted trading of PT Bintang Oto Global Tbk. (BOGA), PT Bhakti Mulia Artha Tbk. (BHAT) and PT Personel Alih Daya Tbk. (PADA) because their shares hit the Auto‑Rejection Atas level following large intraday gains (BHAT +24.91% to Rp1,780, BOGA +24.14% to Rp1,260, PADA +9.88% to Rp178). The suspension is expected to last one day, after which normal trading may resume.

title: Bakrie & Brothers (BNBR) reports a 97.74% drop in Q3 2025 profit
body: For the quarter ended 30 September 2025, Bakrie & Brothers (BNBR) posted net profit of Rp11.65 billion, down 97.74% year‑on‑year, with basic EPS falling to Rp0.07 from Rp11.48. Net revenue slipped 2.57% to Rp2.65 trillion while total assets rose to Rp7.19 trillion.

title: Ifishdeco (IFSH) executes Rp92 billion affiliated loan transactions involving LSM, SDM, IJM, and IML
body: On 25 November 2025, Ifishdeco (IFSH) completed a series of affiliated loan arrangements totaling Rp92 billion, including a Rp22 billion capital injection to Ifishdeco Jaya Makmur (IJM) for use by Lingke Sulawesi Mineral (LSM) and a Rp70 billion facility to Ifishdeco Mineral Lestari (IML) for Sulawesi Damai Mineral (SDM) working capital. The transactions, driven by 49% shareholdings in LSM and SDM, are disclosed as non‑material to the company's operations, financial condition, or continuity.

title: Waskita Karya signs Rp147.4 billion cash‑collateral affiliate transaction with Bank Rakyat Indonesia
body: On 26 November 2025, Waskita Karya (WSKT) entered an affiliate cash‑collateral transaction of Rp147.4 billion with Bank Rakyat Indonesia (BBRI), deposited into BRI’s Jakarta Otista branch account. The deal, exempt under OJK Regulation No. 42/POJK.04/2020, supports a bank guarantee for the 500 kV Sumatra transmission project (Peranap‑Perawang) and aims to enhance the company’s operational performance.

title: Longping Investasi Indonesia sells 46.66 million Green Power Group shares, raising up to Rp10.08 billion
body: Longping Investasi Indonesia divested 46,6693 million shares of Green Power Group in nine transactions priced between Rp200 and Rp216 per share, generating roughly Rp9.33‑10.08 billion. After the sales, its holding fell to 193.33 million shares (17.521% of the company), down 4.23 percentage points from the pre‑sale level.

title: UNSGSA’s Queen Máxima praises BTN’s ‘Pay Your Installment With Your Trash’ program as a green financial inclusion initiative
body: During a visit to BTN’s Gran Harmoni Cibitung housing, Queen Máxima highlighted the bank’s ‘Pay Your Installment‑With‑Your‑Trash’ scheme, which lets low‑income borrowers convert household waste into savings that can cut monthly mortgage payments by about 10‑15% (≈Rp100‑200 k). BTN plans to roll the program out to 100 sites by end‑2026 and to deliver 150,000 low‑emission homes by 2029, emphasizing financial literacy and emergency‑fund savings.

title: Bumi Resources Minerals (BRMS) and Subsidiary Secure USD625 Million Loan Facility for Capital Expenditure and Working Capital
body: Bumi Resources Minerals (BRMS) and its subsidiary Citra Palu Minerals (CPM) secured a USD625 million loan facility on November 24, 2025, from a consortium including Bangkok Bank Public Company Limited (BBL), Bank Permata (BNLI), Bank BCA (BBCA), and Bank Mega (MEGA). The facility, comprising USD200 million for BRMS and USD425 million for CPM, is designated for capital expenditure, working capital, and repayment of existing loans, with maturity set for December 31, 2031, and secured by pledges of shares including Linge Mineral Resources (LMR), Sukma Heksa Sinergi (SHS), and CPM.

title: Tunggal Jaya Investama Sells 700 Million IMPC Shares at a Discount, Raising Rp1.61 Trillion
body: Tunggal Jaya Investama divested 700 million shares of Impack Pratama Industri (IMPC) on November 25, 2025, at Rp2,300 per share, a 27.44% discount from the closing price, generating Rp1.61 trillion. Following this transaction, Tunggal Jaya Investama's ownership in Impack Pratama Industri decreased by 1.27% to 37.83%, with 20.75 million shares remaining.

title: Agung Podomoro Land (APLN) Reports Increased Losses in Q3 2025 Despite Strategic Asset Sale
body: Agung Podomoro Land (APLN) recorded a net loss of Rp57.94 billion as of September 30, 2025, a 40.15% increase from the previous year, with sales and business revenue correcting 4.7% to Rp2.64 trillion and recurring income from hotels and malls decreasing 13.3%. Despite this, the company strengthened its fundamentals through the sale of Pullman Ciawi Vimala Hills, reducing interest expenses by nearly 38% to Rp311.37 billion, and remains optimistic for positive growth in Q4 2025 driven by adaptive sales strategies and holiday momentum.

title: PT Jembo Cable Company Tbk (JECC) to See Shareholder Structure Shift as FUJIKURA GROUP Exits Cable Industry
body: PT Jembo Cable Company Tbk (JECC) will undergo a significant shareholder structure change by December 2025, involving a buyback of 6.4% of shares from FUJIKURA GROUP as it exits the global cable industry. The remaining FUJIKURA GROUP shares will be acquired by PT Monaspermata Persada, increasing its ownership to over 60% from 59%, a move expected to strengthen majority shareholder control.

title: PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk.'s President Director, Erwin Ciputra, Increases Shareholding for Investment.
body: Erwin Ciputra, President Director of PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk. (TPIA), purchased a total of 450,000 TPIA shares across multiple transactions on November 19 and 25, 2025, accumulating a total investment of Rp3,222,500,000 for investment purposes. Following these purchases, his direct ownership in PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk. increased to 139,858,272 shares, maintaining a 0.16% ownership percentage.

title: PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk.'s Controlling Shareholder Divests 50 Million Shares Valued at Rp41.95 Billion to Increase Free Float
body: Hapsoro, the controlling shareholder of PT Bukit Uluwatu Villa Tbk. (BUVA), sold 50 million shares for approximately Rp41.95 billion at Rp839 per share on November 24, 2025, reducing his ownership from 0.45% to 0.25%. This divestment aims to realize profit and increase the public free float, occurring as BUVA's shares strengthened 20.75% on November 25 before being suspended.

title: PT Bundamedik Tbk (BMHS) Reports Strong Q3 2025 Performance Driven by High-Complexity Services and Asset Maximization
body: PT Bundamedik Tbk (BMHS) announced robust Q3 2025 results, with revenue growing 5% QoQ to Rp397 billion and net profit surging 950% QoQ to Rp13.6 billion, alongside an EBITDA margin increase to 17%. This growth is attributed to its strategy of deepening high-complexity maternal and child health services, maximizing existing assets, and expanding innovations like robotic surgery and NICU capacity, with plans to increase hospital beds to 1,000 by 2026-2027.

title: PT Olive Power Invest Acquires 67.90039% Stake in PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk., Becoming Its New Controller
body: PT Olive Power Invest (OPI) completed the acquisition of 1,290,157,112 shares, representing 67,90039% ownership, from Hendro Jap and Hieo Mie Tje in PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk. (OLIV) on November 25, 2025. This transaction officially establishes PT Olive Power Invest as the new controlling shareholder of OLIV.

title: PT Sentra Investa Maksima Sell Shares of PT Lavender Bina Cendikia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Sentra Investa Maksima sold shares of PT Lavender Bina Cendikia Tbk (BMBL.JK) at approximately IDR 62, decreasing holdings from 103,070,600 to 102,270,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Vincent Ie Kok Hui Buy Shares of PT Futura Energi Global Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Vincent Ie Kok Hui bought shares of PT Futura Energi Global Tbk (FUTR.JK) at approximately IDR 633, increasing holdings from 368,410,700 to 376,290,700. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Ubs Group Ag Sell Shares of Bumi Resources Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Ubs Group Ag sold shares of Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI.JK) at approximately IDR 228,994, decreasing holdings from 26,384,420,663 to 25,614,966,563. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Dr. Pulung Peranginangin Buy Shares of Gema Grahasarana Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Dr. Pulung Peranginangin buy shares of Gema Grahasarana Tbk (GEMA.JK) at approximately IDR 92, increasing holdings from 7,760,000 to 7,860,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Komatsu Remanufacturing Asia (KRA) signs MoU with Institut Teknologi Sepuluh Nopember and PKS with Institut Teknologi Kalimantan to boost industry‑education collaboration
body: On 19 September 2025, PT Komatsu Remanufacturing Asia (KRA), a unit of PT United Tractors Tbk (UT), signed a Memorandum of Understanding with Institut Teknologi Sepuluh Nopember (ITS) in Surabaya, and on 17 September 2025 it signed a Perjanjian Kerja Sama with Institut Teknologi Kalimantan (ITK) in Balikpapan. The agreements aim to develop human‑resource competencies, applied research, internships and training programs aligned with the remanufacturing and technology needs of the industry, supporting sustainable development in East Kalimantan and broader national competitiveness.

title: Indonesia's move to a 50% palm‑oil biodiesel mandate is expected to lift palm oil prices and curb exports
body: Indonesia plans to raise its biodiesel blend requirement from 40% (B40) to 50% (B50) by the second half of next year, a shift that could push palm oil prices to around MYR5,000‑5,500 (US$1,210‑1,331) per ton and reduce total palm oil exports to about 26 million tons in 2026. The higher blend would increase biodiesel demand by roughly 1.7 million tons, representing about 18% of global palm oil use, and may force major importers such as India and China to seek alternative vegetable‑oil supplies.

title: Indonesia and China launch the Two Countries Twin Parks project to boost industrial cooperation and investment
body: Coordinating Minister for Economic Affairs Airlangga Hartarto announced the Two Countries Twin Parks (TCTP) initiative, which will build two industrial complexes in twin cities to deepen Indonesia‑China value‑chain synergies and support Indonesia’s 8% growth target by 2029. At the China‑Indonesia Economic and Trade Exchange, Fuzhou City signed 16 investment commitments worth about US$2.1 billion for projects slated to start in 2026, covering sectors such as steel, nickel, seafood processing, solar energy, AI research and industrial supply chains.

title: Indonesia to raise subsidized 3‑kg LPG supply to up to 8.5 million metric tons for the Christmas 2025 and New Year 2026 holidays
body: The government approved an increase in subsidized 3‑kg LPG volume from about 8.2 million to 8.4‑8.5 million metric tons after a coordination meeting with Finance Minister Purbaya Yudhi Sadewa and BP BUMN chief Dony Oskaria. Despite the higher quota, subsidies will not rise because LPG prices remain below the state budget reference level.

title: Vincent Ie Kok Hui purchases 7.88 million shares of Futura Energi Global, raising his stake to 5.67%
body: On 26 November 2025, Vincent Ie Kok Hui bought a total of 7,880,000 shares of Futura Energi Global in two transactions at Rp630‑635 per share, spending roughly Rp4.98 billion. The acquisition increased his holding to 376.29 million shares, representing a 5.671% ownership stake, up 0,119 percentage points.

title: Petrosea prepares Rp85.2 billion to redeem 2024 sustainable bond and sukuk due Dec 2025
body: Petrosea will allocate Rp85.2 billion to repay a Rp47 billion sustainable bond at 6.5% interest and a Rp33 billion sustainable sukuk ijarah, both maturing on 20 December 2025, with payments to be made through KSEI by 19 December 2025. The company states the transaction will not negatively affect its operations, legal standing, financial condition, or business continuity.

title: GMF Asia (GMFI) posts 44.7% YoY increase in Q3 net profit to USD22.31 million despite slight revenue decline
body: For the quarter ended 30 September 2025, GMF Asia (GMFI) reported net profit of USD22.31 million, a 44.68% rise year‑over‑year, with basic diluted EPS of USD0,00060. Revenue fell 2.1% to USD307.13 million, while assets grew to USD519.54 million and liabilities to USD755.24 million.

title: UBS Group AG sells 769.45 million Bumi Resources shares, cutting its stake to 6.9%
body: On 20 November 2025, UBS Group AG completed the sale of 769.45 million Bumi Resources shares at Rp228,994 per share, generating roughly Rp176.2 billion in proceeds. The divestment reduced UBS's holding to 25.61 billion shares (6.9% of Bumi Resources), down from 26.38 billion shares (7.11%) before the transaction.

title: Summarecon Agung posts 41% drop in net profit for the first nine months of 2025
body: Summarecon Agung (SMRA) reported net profit of Rp549.57 billion for the first nine months of 2025, a 41.39% decline from Rp937.75 billion a year earlier, while net revenue fell 14.87% to Rp6.41 trillion. Total assets rose to Rp37.03 trillion and liabilities increased to Rp22.1 trillion, bringing equity to Rp14.93 trillion.

title: Golden Energy Mines (GEMS) reports Q3 2025 net profit of $199.31 million, down 49.84% year‑over‑year
body: The company posted a net profit of $199.31 million for Q3 2025, a 49.84% decline from $397.39 million a year earlier, with earnings per share falling to $0,034 from $0,068. Revenue dropped 13.93% to $1.73 billion and total assets decreased to $1.12 billion, while liabilities fell to $475.86 million.

title: PT ChemStar Indonesia Tbk. Prioritizes Efficiency and Organic Growth Over Aggressive Capex in 2025
body: PT ChemStar Indonesia Tbk (CHEM) reported minimal capital expenditure of less than Rp5 billion this year, stating 2025 is a phase of efficiency and margin improvement rather than aggressive expansion. The company is prioritizing production efficiency and cost structure improvements, leveraging its 50-60% factory utilization to facilitate organic business expansion without significant new investment.

title: Leyand International plans restructuring and potential entry into energy and silica mining as JSI Sinergi Mas becomes controlling shareholder
body: JSI Sinergi Mas has acquired 51% of Leyand International (LAPD), holding 2,022,838,500 shares, and is evaluating restructuring or divestiture of Rusindo Eka Raya (RER) while exploring energy and silica‑mining projects, subject to POJK No. 17/POJK.04/2020 and shareholder approval. The company confirmed it will remain listed, will follow mandatory tender offer rules, and will disclose any material corporate actions publicly.

title: Merdeka Gold Resources (EMAS) posts USD22.27 million loss for Q3 2025 amid sharp revenue decline and higher expenses
body: For the quarter ended 30 September 2025, Merdeka Gold Resources (EMAS) recorded a net loss of USD22.27 million, with revenue falling 93.7% to USD113.4 thousand and operating expenses, especially general and administrative costs, rising nearly 300%. Despite the loss, total equity rose to USD386.22 million and assets increased to USD712.47 million.

title: PT Global Sukses Digital Tbk launches DOSS PLAY, DOSS Creator+ and Artisan Finder to broaden its creative ecosystem
body: The company introduced three new platforms—DOSS PLAY, a low‑cost pay‑per‑view short‑film streaming service; DOSS Creator+, an affiliate program that lets creators earn commissions from gear reviews; and Artisan Finder, a job‑matching marketplace—for creators, coinciding with the DOSS Vaganza 2025 event. These initiatives aim to generate new revenue streams for creators and strengthen the integrated, sustainable creative ecosystem that PT Global Sukses Digital Tbk (DOSS) is building across Indonesia.

title: PT Perintis Triniti Properti Tbk. (TRIN) Details Recovery Strategy and Addresses Corporate Action Delays Amidst Stock Suspension
body: PT Perintis Triniti Properti Tbk (TRIN) is undertaking performance recovery steps, including sales evaluation and efficiency strategies, while its strategic corporate actions are delayed due to stock suspension since November 21, 2025. The company reported Rp946 billion in realized marketing revenue as of September 2025, representing 53% of its total target, and plans an Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders (RUPSLB) on December 2, 2025, to discuss options like subsidiary divestment.

title: PT Gozco Capital Increases Stake in PT Bank Neo Commerce Tbk. (BBYB) Amidst Strong Financial Performance
body: PT Gozco Capital acquired 72,452,676 shares of PT Bank Neo Commerce Tbk. (BBYB), raising its ownership from 6.61% to 7.16%, which BBYB views as a positive signal of confidence. This accumulation aligns with BBYB's significant Q3-2025 profit of Rp464 billion, a nearly 100-fold increase, alongside improved ROA to 3.45%, ROE to 16.96%, and reduced NPLs.

title: PT Pelayaran Jaya Hidup Baru Tbk Begins Construction of First LCT Ship Funded by IPO Proceeds
