body: PT Pelayaran Jaya Hidup Baru Tbk (PJHB) has commenced the initial stage of construction for its first Landing Craft Tank (LCT) ship, utilizing Rp158.4 billion from its IPO proceeds, with material procurement and preparation already completed. This project, in collaboration with PT Untung Brawijaya Sejahtera, aims to strengthen domestic sea transport capacity for sectors like mining, energy, and logistics, with each LCT designed to carry approximately 2,500 DWT.

title: PT Jaya Sukses Makmur Sentosa Tbk. (RISE) Secures Rights Issue Approval and Appoints New Directors
body: PT Jaya Sukses Makmur Sentosa Tbk (RISE) shareholders approved a rights issue of up to 1,330,000,000 new shares at Rp100 each to strengthen capital and increase recurring income. The Extraordinary General Meeting also accepted Belinda Natalia's resignation and appointed Budi Agusti as the new President Director, along with Samuel Adhiputra Bunjamin as Director.

title: PT Digital Mediatama Maxima Tbk. (DMMX) Approves Board and Commissioner Composition Changes at EGMS
body: PT Digital Mediatama Maxima Tbk (DMMX) concluded its Extraordinary General Meeting of Shareholders (RUPSLB) on November 25, 2025, where shareholders approved changes to the composition of the board of directors and commissioners. The meeting achieved a quorum with 88.61% of valid voting shares present, and the new board structure will be effective until the closing of the 2029 Annual General Meeting of Shareholders.

title: PT Bangun Karya Perkasa Jaya Tbk. Divests Subsidiary PT Karya Artha Sinergi to Focus on EV Sector
body: PT Bangun Karya Perkasa Jaya Tbk (KRYA) officially sold its 51% stake in PT Karya Artha Sinergi (PT KAS) to Budi Herlambang for Rp1.02 billion, aligning with its new strategy to strengthen its business in the electric vehicle (EV) sector. This divestment, which represents 0.97% of KRYA's equity, means PT KAS is no longer a subsidiary and has no material impact on KRYA's operations or financial condition.

title: Gloriajaya Gempita Buy Shares of Suparma Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Gloriajaya Gempita bought shares of Suparma Tbk (SPMA.JK) at approximately IDR 242, increasing holdings from 992,959,457 to 1,290,847,294. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pacific Star Synergy Buy Shares of Suparma Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pacific Star Synergy bought shares of Suparma Tbk (SPMA.JK) at approximately IDR 242, increasing holdings from 1,576,497,000 to 2,049,446,100. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Graha Inti Harapan Buy Shares of Suparma Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Graha Inti Harapan bought shares of Suparma Tbk (SPMA.JK) at approximately IDR 242, increasing holdings from 336,734,079 to 437,754,302. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Sell Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya sold shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,510, decreasing holdings from 110,672,000 to 101,390,800. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,510, increasing holdings from 110,638,200 to 110,672,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Hengtong Optic Electricinternational Co., Limited Sell Shares of Voksel Electric Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hengtong Optic Electricinternational Co., Limited sell shares of Voksel Electric Tbk (VOKS.JK) at approximately IDR 252, decreasing holdings from 2,275,050,320 to 2,275,040,220. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Sekar Laut Tbk Sell Shares of Sekar Laut Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Sekar Laut Tbk sold shares of Sekar Laut Tbk (SKLT.JK) at approximately IDR 234, decreasing holdings from 672,108,600 to 671,108,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Alexander Ramlie Sell Shares of PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk.
body: According to the published announcement, Alexander Ramlie sold shares of PT Amman Mineral Internasional Tbk. (AMMN.JK) at approximately IDR 6,475, decreasing holdings from 259,922,860 to 253,422,860. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Bahtera Bintang Nusantara Sell Shares of PT Ketrosden Triasmitra Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Bahtera Bintang Nusantara sold shares of PT Ketrosden Triasmitra Tbk (KETR.JK) at approximately IDR 558, decreasing holdings from 139,443,300 to 110,570,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Candrakarya Multikreasi Sell Shares of PT Mora Telematika Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Candrakarya Multikreasi sold shares of PT Mora Telematika Indonesia Tbk (MORA.JK) at approximately IDR 420, decreasing holdings from 9,653,884,260 to 8,510,884,260. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Mitra Investindo Tbk Sell Shares of Bumi Citra Permai Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Mitra Investindo Tbk sold shares of Bumi Citra Permai Tbk (BCIP.JK) at approximately IDR 108, decreasing holdings from 532,192,100 to 526,327,100. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Sudjono Buy Shares of BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Sudjono bought shares of BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk (BFIN.JK) at approximately IDR 758, increasing holdings from 26,310,800 to 30,000,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Johannes Sell Shares of PT Adiwarna Anugerah Abadi Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Johannes sold shares of PT Adiwarna Anugerah Abadi Tbk (NAIK.JK) at approximately IDR 100, decreasing holdings from 342,808,200 to 322,808,200. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Patrick Rudolf Dannacher Buy Shares of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk.
body: According to the published announcement, Patrick Rudolf Dannacher bought shares of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk. (CYBR.JK) at approximately IDR 1,415, increasing holdings from 191,196,222 to 192,709,622. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Andri Hutama Putra Sell Shares of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk.
body: According to the published announcement, Andri Hutama Putra sold shares of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk. (CYBR.JK) at approximately IDR 1,495, decreasing holdings from 20,332,100 to 20,232,100. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara Sell Shares of PT Dharma Polimetal Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Noel Aelyo Laras Kusuma Negara sold shares of PT Dharma Polimetal Tbk (DRMA.JK) at approximately IDR 1,057, decreasing holdings from 71,897,600 to 71,747,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Yulisar Khiat Buy Shares of PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Yulisar Khiat bought shares of PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk (HEAL.JK) at approximately IDR 1,432, increasing holdings from 954,969,966 to 975,554,966. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Omni Inovasi Indonesia Tbk Sell Shares of PT Green Power Group Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Omni Inovasi Indonesia Tbk sold shares of PT Green Power Group Tbk (LABA.JK) at approximately IDR 193, decreasing holdings from 193,330,700 to 130,240,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia and Germany launch InCircular project to boost circular economy in pilot provinces
body: The National Development Planning Ministry/National Development Planning Agency (PPN/Bappenas) and Deutsche Gesellschaft für Internationale Zusammenarbeit (GIZ) GmbH signed a cooperation agreement to implement the five‑year InCircular project, targeting packaging in East Java, residue in Bali, and e‑waste in Jakarta. The initiative aims to strengthen regulations, build capacity, improve waste‑management systems and create green jobs as part of Indonesia’s Circular Economy Roadmap & Action Plan 2025‑2045.

title: Indonesia's IHSG reaches all‑time high as PT Barito Renewables Energy leads market‑cap rankings and BPI Danantara plans 2026 investments
body: On 26 November 2025 the Indeks Harga Saham Gabungan (IHSG) closed at 8,602.13 points, up 0.94%, marking an all‑time high, with PT Barito Renewables Energy (BREN) overtaking Bank Central Asia (BBCA) to become the largest market‑cap company at Rp1,318 trillion. The article highlights upcoming 2026 challenges, notably the investment rollout by Badan Pengelola Investasi Daya Anagata Nusantara (BPI Danantara) and the continued growth of a young, predominantly individual investor base now exceeding 18 million people.

title: Candrakarya Multikreasi sells 1.14 billion Mora Telematika Indonesia shares, raising Rp480 billion and cutting its stake to 35.99%
body: Candrakarya Multikreasi divested 1.14 billion shares of Mora Telematika Indonesia at an execution price of Rp420 per share, generating fresh capital of roughly Rp480 billion while the stock was frozen by the Bursa Efek Indonesia (BEI). The transaction diluted Candrakarya’s holding to 8.51 billion shares, equivalent to 35.99% of the company, a 4.83% decline from the pre‑sale position.

title: Amman Minerals commissioner Alexander Ramlie sells 6.5 million shares, cutting his stake to 0.349%
body: On 21 November 2025, Alexander Ramlie divested 6.5 million Amman Minerals (AMMN) shares at Rp6,475 per share, generating Rp2.08 billion in proceeds. The transaction reduced his holding from 259.92 million shares (0.358%) to 253.42 million shares (0.349%), a decline of 0,009 percentage points.

title: Bank Tabungan Negara (BBTN) oversubscribes its subordinated and social bond issuances to boost capital for housing finance
body: On 28 November 2025, Bank Tabungan Negara (BBTN) received 1.47‑times oversubscription for a Rp2 trillion, 5‑year subordinated bond and 5.47‑times oversubscription for a Rp300 billion, 3‑year social bond, with coupon ranges of 5.85‑6.75% and 5.25‑5.75% respectively. The proceeds are aimed at strengthening Tier 2 capital, raising the bank's CAR to 18.16% and supporting its KPR expansion and the government’s three‑million‑house program.

title: Sawit Sumbermas Sarana provides Rp600 billion loan to affiliate Sawit Mandiri Lestari
body: Sawit Sumbermas Sarana (SSMS) signed a loan agreement on 28 November 2025 to inject Rp600 billion into its 63.40%‑owned affiliate Sawit Mandiri Lestari (SML) for capital expenditures, working capital and operational needs. The funding is intended to streamline group liquidity management and support SML’s activities in agriculture, forestry, fisheries, processing, electricity procurement and trade.

title: PT Sinergi Inti Andalan Prima Tbk reports near‑tripling revenue and 819% net profit surge in Q3 2025
body: PT Sinergi Inti Andalan Prima Tbk posted revenue of Rp68.60 billion for the quarter ended 30 September 2025, a 195% increase from the prior year, with net profit rising 819% to Rp19.37 billion and EBITDA climbing to Rp35.35 billion. Total assets nearly doubled to Rp454.59 billion while the debt‑to‑equity ratio remained low at 0.26×, indicating a healthy capital structure amid rapid expansion.

title: PT Mandiri Manajemen Investasi partners with PT Mandiri Sekuritas to launch Indonesia's first Shariah Gold ETF in Q1 2026
body: PT Mandiri Manajemen Investasi (Mandiri Investasi) signed an MoU with PT Mandiri Sekuritas on 28 Nov, appointing the latter as Indonesia's first ETF Gold Shariah dealer participant and targeting a launch in the first quarter of 2026. The product will be backed by physical gold stored with an LJK‑licensed custodian, follow the DSN‑MUI Fatwa No. 163/2025, and is expected to help grow Mandiri Investasi's AUM, which already exceeds Rp 70 trillion across 54 funds.

title: Chief Commissioner of PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk resigns, pending approval at the Jan 13, 2026 extraordinary shareholders meeting
body: Eka Dharmajanto Kasih submitted his resignation as Chief Commissioner of PT Sampoerna Agro Tbk on 18 November 2025, which will become effective after the resolution is approved at the extraordinary shareholders meeting scheduled for 13 January 2026. The company indicated that the resignation will not impact SGRO's operations, legal status, financial condition, or business continuity.

title: PT Petrosea Tbk. completes acquisition of 60% of Scan‑Bilt Pte. Ltd. for S$10.3 million
body: PT Petrosea Tbk., via its subsidiary Petrosea Services Solutions Ltd., acquired a 60% stake in Scan‑Bilt Pte. Ltd. from TCAL Engineering Pte. Ltd. for S$10.3 million (approximately US$8.03 million). The purchase is intended to broaden Petrosea's multidisciplinary EPC capabilities and serve as a business hub for expansion into Singapore, Papua New Guinea, Australia and Indonesia, supporting its diversification strategy.

title: Asuransi Tugu Pratama Indonesia (TUGU) Reports Strong Premium Growth and Superior Financial Solvency
body: Asuransi Tugu Pratama Indonesia (TUGU) announced premium growth of 5.6% by September 2025, exceeding the industry average of 3.4%, and significantly improved its market ranking in the Fire & Property segment while maintaining leadership in Marine Hull and Aviation. The company also demonstrated robust financial health with a Risk Based Capital (RBC) of 360.9% and an Investment Adequacy Ratio (RKI) of 272.6%, both well above industry averages.

title: Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (TPIA) President Director Erwin Ciputra Increases Share Ownership for Investment
body: Erwin Ciputra, President Director of Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (TPIA), acquired 200,000 shares of TPIA on November 24, 2025, at an average price of Rp7,200 per share, totaling approximately Rp1.4 billion. This transaction, intended for investment, increased his direct ownership to approximately 0.16%.

title: Hasmoro Buy Shares of PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hasmoro bought shares of PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk (HEAL.JK) at approximately IDR 1,415, increasing holdings from 746,494,389 to 747,553,489. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Jhon Veter Firdaus Reagen Buy Shares of PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Jhon Veter Firdaus Reagen bought shares of PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk (FUJI.JK) at approximately IDR 935, increasing holdings from 181,333,300 to 199,033,300. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Hengtong Optic Electricinternational Co., Limited Sell Shares of Voksel Electric Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hengtong Optic Electricinternational Co., Limited sell shares of Voksel Electric Tbk (VOKS.JK) at approximately IDR 252, decreasing holdings from 2,275,040,220 to 2,274,447,320. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Erwin Ciputra Buy Shares of PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Erwin Ciputra bought shares of PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (TPIA.JK) at approximately IDR 7,200, increasing holdings from 139,858,272 to 140,058,272. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Garuda Indonesia (Persero) Tbk. announces a Rp23.67 trillion private placement with PT Danantara Asset Management (Persero) to boost working capital and support Citilink operations
body: Garuda will issue 315,610,920,000 Series D shares at Rp75 each, all to be subscribed by PT Danantara Asset Management (Persero) in a transaction valued at Rp23.67 trillion, comprising a Rp6.65 trillion shareholder loan conversion and a Rp17.02 trillion cash injection. The proceeds will be allocated 36.78% to Garuda’s working capital and aircraft maintenance and 63.22% to strengthen Citilink’s working capital, including fuel debt repayment to Pertamina, with share distribution on 5 December 2025 and listing on the BEI on 8 December 2025.

title: PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk. insiders increase shareholdings in late November 2025
body: Commissioner Hasmoro purchased about 1.1 million shares at an average price of Rp1,415, raising his direct stake to 4.87%, while CEO Yulisar Khiat bought 20.53 million shares at Rp1,431‑Rp1,432, increasing his ownership to 6.35%; the transactions total roughly Rp16.2 billion. HEAL’s stock closed at Rp1,400 on 28 November 2025, down 0.71%.

title: PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk. (OLIV) appoints new board and commissioners after change of control at 2025 extraordinary shareholders meeting
body: PT Oscar Mitra Sukses Sejahtera Tbk. (OLIV) held an extraordinary shareholders meeting on 26 November 2025, approving the resignation of its previous directors and commissioners and appointing a new board led by Wang Xiaoxuan as President Director, with Chen Chunming, Chen Chaoying and Habibi Simorangkir as directors. The new board of commissioners includes An Shaohong as Chairman (who also chairs PT Green Power Group Tbk. (LABA)), Qiu Shaohua and Sandra Perkasa as independent commissioner, all serving until 26 November 2030, following OLIV’s acquisition by Olive Power Invest (OPI).

title: Dharma Polimetal (DRMA) acquires 82% of Mah Sing Indonesia for Rp41 billion
body: Dharma Polimetal (DRMA) completed the acquisition of 82% of Mah Sing Indonesia, purchasing 65% (3.25 million shares) from VitalRoutes Sdn Bhd and 17% (850,000 shares) from Kingsanindo Perkasa Indah for a total consideration of Rp41 billion. The transaction is below the materiality threshold under Otoritas Jasa Keuangan Regulation No. 17/POJK.04/2020 and does not adversely affect the company's operations, legal standing, financial condition, or business continuity.

title: Summarecon Agung raises Rp536.28 billion from divesting its stake in Bukit Permai Properti
body: Summarecon Agung sold a total of 478.88 million shares of Bukit Permai Properti for Rp536.28 billion, with Summarecon Bali Indah disposing 335.27 million shares to Bukit Uluwatu and Bali Indah Development selling 143.56 million shares to Bukit Uluwatu and 50 thousand shares to Nusantara Bali Realti. The transactions, completed on 28 November 2025, are not material to the company's operations, legal standing, financial condition, or continuity.

title: BUMA International Group reports stronger Q3 2025 operations and EBITDA of $63 million amid ongoing YTD challenges
body: In the third quarter of 2025, BUMA Internasional Grup (DOID) increased overburden removal by 18% and coal production by 12% quarter‑on‑quarter, driving revenue to $400 million and EBITDA to $63 million (19% margin) while net loss narrowed to $1 million; however, year‑to‑date results remain pressured by extreme weather, a Q1 safety incident and contract ramp‑downs, with revenue down 16% YoY to $1,131 billion. The group also diversified funding by issuing a $53.7 million bond in October and completing an early repayment of $212.25 million senior notes in November.

title: DANA Indonesia launches Premium Mini digital wallet for teenagers to boost financial inclusion
body: DANA Indonesia introduced Premium Mini, a verified digital wallet for users aged 13 to 17 that requires parental approval and caps balances at $120 (IDR 2 million), aiming to close the 74% financial inclusion gap among 15‑17‑year‑olds and support the national goal of 93% inclusion by 2029. The product offers top‑ups, mobile payments and peer‑to‑peer transfers while allowing parents to monitor activity, differentiating it from the standard DANA Premium wallet with a $1,198 (IDR 20 million) limit.

title: PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA), PT Yupi Indo Jelly Gum Tbk (YUPI), PT Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa Tbk (KMDS) and PT Sigma Energy Compressindo Tbk (SICO) announce interim dividend cum dates in early December 2025
body: PT Bank Central Asia Tbk (BBCA) will pay an interim dividend of Rp 6.77 trillion (15.6% of Q3 2025 net profit) with a cum date of 2 December 2025, followed by PT Yupi Indo Jelly Gum Tbk (YUPI) on the same day (Rp 300 billion, 63.44% of nine‑month profit), PT Kurniamitra Duta Sentosa Tbk (KMDS) on 4 December 2025 (Rp 12.8 billion) and PT Sigma Energy Compressindo Tbk (SICO) on 3 December 2025 (Rp 2.73 trillion). Analyst Chory Agung Ramdhani of BRI Danareksa Sekuritas warned investors to watch key dates, fundamentals, price reactions and liquidity, noting BBCA’s 0.6% yield remains attractive given its solid fundamentals.

title: PT Summarecon Agung Tbk reports 41% YoY profit decline in Q3 2025 amid falling revenue but 31% growth in marketing sales
body: In Q3 2025 PT Summarecon Agung Tbk (SMRA) posted net profit of Rp 549.57 billion, a 41.39% YoY drop, as net revenue fell 14.86% to Rp 6.41 trillion, while earnings per share fell to Rp 33.29. Despite the downturn, marketing sales rose 31% YoY to Rp 3.57 trillion, representing 71% of the annual target, and analysts note a PER of 8.7×, PBV of 0.56× and a target price of Rp 422 per share.

title: PT Mirae Asset Sekuritas Indonesia launches internal investigation after a client alleges loss of Rp71 billion
body: A 70‑year‑old client, Irman, filed a police report claiming a loss of approximately Rp71 billion in his Mirae Asset Sekuritas account, prompting PT Mirae Asset Sekuritas Indonesia to begin an internal investigation and coordinate with OJK, SRO and PPATK. The firm noted early findings that the client may have shared his password and access credentials, warned all customers to keep such information confidential, and said it will pursue legal action if wrongdoing is confirmed.

title: Happy Hapsoro reduces stake in Rukun Raharja through a 5‑million share divestment
body: On 27 November 2025, Hapsoro Sukmonohadi alias Happy Hapsoro sold 5 million Rukun Raharja shares and an additional 1 million shares via Sentosa Bersama Mitra, with assistance from Mandiri Sekuritas, Henan Putihrai Sekuritas, Maybank Sekuritas, Minna Padi Investama Sekuritas, CGS International Sekuritas Indonesia and Trimegah Sekuritas Indonesia (TRIM). After the transactions, his direct holding fell to 1.17 billion shares (27.81% of Rukun Raharja) and his indirect holding through Sentosa Bersama Mitra to 1.49 billion shares (35.28%).

title: Autopedia Sukses Lestari posts 15% Q3 revenue rise and outlines double‑digit growth plan for 2026
body: In Q3 2025, Autopedia Sukses Lestari (ASLC) recorded revenue of Rp713.0 billion, a 15 % increase, and net profit of Rp33.9 billion, with Caroline.id contributing 71 % of revenue and selling over 3,000 used cars (up 26 % YoY). The company expects double‑digit revenue growth in 2026, driven by digital integration, new Caroline.id showrooms in Bandung and Cibubur (total 17 locations), and continued expansion of its JBA auction and MotoGadai services.

title: CEO Johannes sells 20 million Adiwarna Anugerah Abadi shares at a discount, reducing his stake to 9.7%
body: Johannes disposed of 20 million Adiwarna Anugerah Abadi shares on 20 Nov 2025 at an exercise price of Rp100, about 19 % below the closing price of Rp124, generating roughly Rp2 billion and lowering his holding to 322.8 million shares (9.7%). The sale, described as for internal purposes, comes as the company's stock has fallen 28 % over the past six months.

title: Adhi Karya (ADHI) resolves two PKPU petitions through amicable settlements
body: Two debt‑payment suspension petitions filed by Interdesign Cipta Optima, PT Dinamika Prakarsa Mukti and PT Sinergi Karya Sejahtera were settled out of court, ending the PKPU proceedings for Adhi Karya (ADHI). Corporate Secretary Rozi Sparta confirmed the legal process has concluded.

title: Pakuan (UANG) reports Q3 2025 net profit decline to Rp66.17 billion despite higher sales and asset growth
body: For the quarter ended 30 September 2025, Pakuan (UANG) posted a net profit of Rp66.17 billion, down 10.77% year‑on‑year and reducing basic EPS to Rp54.69. Sales rose 5.43% to Rp266.98 billion, total assets increased to Rp1.71 trillion, liabilities fell to Rp1.12 trillion, and equity grew to Rp593.43 billion.

title: Multi Garam Utama (FOLK) plans private placement of 394.81 million shares to raise capital
body: Multi Garam Utama (FOLK) intends to issue 394,814,146 new shares at a nominal value of Rp20 each, representing up to 10 % of its paid‑up capital, with proceeds earmarked for business expansion, working capital and general corporate purposes. The transaction is expected to boost assets by about 8.86 % and equity by 10.04 %, while non‑participating shareholders could face up to 9.09 % dilution, pending shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting on 12 December 2025.

title: CLEO posts 6% sales growth in the first nine months of 2025 but profit falls 7.8%
body: CLEO reported net sales of Rp2.09 trillion for the first nine months of 2025, a 6% YoY increase, with the bottled segment driving most of the growth, while attributable profit slipped 7.8% to Rp305.12 billion. The company also announced the launch of CLEO Eco Lite and plans to add three new factories in Palu, Pontianak and Pekanbaru by 2026, bringing its total to 35 plants.

title: Bakrie Sumatera Plantations (UNSP) posts Rp63.46 billion loss for Q3 2025, assets rise to Rp3.5 trillion
body: For the quarter ended 30 September 2025, Bakrie Sumatera Plantations (UNSP) reported a net loss of Rp63.46 billion, turning a previous surplus of Rp812.64 per share into a loss of Rp25.39 per share. Revenue rose 4.5% to Rp1.86 trillion and gross profit increased 28.2% to Rp575.83 billion, while total assets grew to Rp3.5 trillion and liabilities to Rp8.84 trillion, widening the capital deficiency to Rp5.34 trillion.

title: PT Longping Investasi Indonesia sells 5.71% stake in PT Green Power Group Tbk. (LABA) in multi‑day divestment
body: Between 25‑27 November 2025, PT Longping Investasi Indonesia sold 63,090,100 shares (about 5.71% of capital) of PT Green Power Group Tbk. (LABA) at prices ranging from Rp175 to Rp208, generating roughly Rp13.38 billion in proceeds. After the sales, its holding fell to 130,240,600 shares (11.80%) and LABA’s share price closed down 2.23% at Rp175.

title: PT Optima Prima Metal Sinergi Tbk shares jump 11.2% ahead of Dec 1 AGM amid diversification plans and acquisition rumors
body: PT Optima Prima Metal Sinergi Tbk (OPMS) rose 11.21% on Nov 28, 2025, after announcing a plan to add 16 new business lines covering food, beverage and dairy products. Analysts cite large institutional buying and speculation of strategic investors, making the shareholders’ meeting on Dec 1 a potentially decisive moment for the company.

title: PT Pertamina Geothermal Energy Tbk. announces CEO Jufi Hadi's resignation, shares slip 2.11%
body: PT Pertamina Geothermal Energy Tbk. (PGEO) disclosed that Jufi Hadi submitted his resignation as President Director on 25 November 2025, with a shareholders meeting to be held within 90 days to confirm its effectiveness. The resignation is said to have no impact on operations or finances, but the stock fell 2.11% to Rp1,160 on 28 November.

title: PT Sinar Mas Multiartha Tbk. injects Rp880 billion capital into PT Arthamas Solusindo via debt‑to‑equity swap
body: PT Sinar Mas Multiartha Tbk. added Rp880 billion capital to its subsidiary PT Arthamas Solusindo through a debt‑to‑equity swap on 28 Nov 2025, maintaining its 99.99% ownership while PT Sinarmas Sekuritas holds about 0,000003%. The transaction, approved at an extraordinary shareholders meeting, is said to have no material impact on SMMA’s operations, financial condition, business outlook, legal aspects, or long‑term continuity.

title: Controlling Shareholder of PT Asri Karya Lestari Tbk. (ASLI) Plans Divestment to PT Wahana Konstruksi Mandiri
body: Sudjatmiko, the controlling shareholder of PT Asri Karya Lestari Tbk. (ASLI), plans to divest 4,345,000,000 shares (69.52%) to PT Wahana Konstruksi Mandiri, a transaction that could trigger a change of control. Following the official announcement of the takeover plan on November 28, 2025, ASLI's shares surged 24.68% to Rp394, reaching Auto-Rejection Atas.

title: PT Waskita Karya Tbk completes Rp3.28 trillion divestment of 35% stake in PT Cimanggis Cibitung Tollways to PT Bakrie Toll Indonesia
body: PT Waskita Karya Tbk (WSKT) sold its 35% share in PT Cimanggis Cibitung Tollways (CCT) through subsidiary PT Waskita Toll Road (WTR) to PT Bakrie Toll Indonesia for Rp3.28 trillion, using the proceeds to meet creditor obligations and boost operational liquidity as part of its Master Restructuring Agreement. The company said it will continue divesting other toll assets to refocus on core construction businesses.

title: Rondy Yunanda Buy Shares of PT Ecocare Indo Pasifik Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Rondy Yunanda bought shares of PT Ecocare Indo Pasifik Tbk (HYGN.JK) at approximately IDR 160, increasing holdings from 202,800,000 to 203,000,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Raymond Buy Shares of PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Raymond bought shares of PT Chandra Asri Pacific Tbk (TPIA.JK) at approximately IDR 7,138, increasing holdings from 2,600,000 to 2,800,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Hengtong Optic Electricinternational Co., Limited Sell Shares of Voksel Electric Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hengtong Optic Electricinternational Co., Limited sell shares of Voksel Electric Tbk (VOKS.JK), decreasing holdings from 2,274,447,320 to 2,274,205,120. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Sentra Investa Maksima Sell Shares of PT Lavender Bina Cendikia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Sentra Investa Maksima sold shares of PT Lavender Bina Cendikia Tbk (BMBL.JK) at approximately IDR 1,375, decreasing holdings from 102,270,600 to 101,270,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Lee Suharlie Sell Shares of PT Megapower Makmur Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Lee Suharlie sold shares of PT Megapower Makmur Tbk (MPOW.JK), decreasing holdings from 49,056,100 to 47,356,100. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Doni Mora Sell Shares of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk.
body: According to the published announcement, Doni Mora sold shares of PT ITSEC Asia Tbk. (CYBR.JK) at approximately IDR 1,450, decreasing holdings from 3,037,100 to 2,987,100. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Celebes Mining Resources Buy Shares of PT Harapan Duta Pertiwi Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Celebes Mining Resources bought shares of PT Harapan Duta Pertiwi Tbk (HOPE.JK) at approximately IDR 112, increasing holdings from 390,781,500 to 470,781,500. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Harapan Group Sukses Sell Shares of PT Harapan Duta Pertiwi Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Harapan Group Sukses sold shares of PT Harapan Duta Pertiwi Tbk (HOPE.JK) at approximately IDR 112, decreasing holdings from 767,450,400 to 687,450,400. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Sentra Investa Maksima Sell Shares of PT Lavender Bina Cendikia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Sentra Investa Maksima sold shares of PT Lavender Bina Cendikia Tbk (BMBL.JK) at approximately IDR 74, decreasing holdings from 102,270,600 to 101,270,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Multi Sarana Nasional Buy Shares of PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Multi Sarana Nasional bought shares of PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk (HATM.JK) at approximately IDR 384, increasing holdings from 1,738,909,861 to 1,745,409,861. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT United Tractors Tbk expands disaster resilience programs across East Java, from pre‑disaster mitigation to post‑eruption recovery
body: PT United Tractors Tbk (UT) is implementing a comprehensive disaster management system in East Java, including pre‑disaster initiatives such as the KATANA program, forest revegetation, and waste‑to‑resource projects that have restored 567 hectares, planted over 110,000 trees and doubled farmers’ monthly incomes. The company also conducted disaster‑response simulations with local authorities, launched the “Red Code” emergency app, and is supporting long‑term recovery after the November 2025 Semeru eruption through housing, school construction, logistics aid and community training.

title: Indonesia launches a palm oil extensification program targeting 3 million hectares to reach 100 million tons of CPO by 2045
body: The government will add up to 3 million hectares of oil palm plantations over the next two decades, starting with 600,000 hectares—400,000 for smallholder plasma farms and 200,000 for state‑owned PT Agrinas and PalmCo—aiming for 100 million tons of crude palm oil by 2045 and expanding downstream processing to create jobs. The expansion will focus on regions such as Jambi, South Sumatra, Bangka Belitung, Kalimantan and South Sulawesi while adhering to sustainability principles.

title: Indonesia’s Directorate General of Mineral and Coal and Directorate General of Tax Hold Hybrid Socialisation on RKAB and IUP Compliance
body: The two directorates hosted a hybrid event in Jakarta with 800 on‑site and 1,000 online participants to explain the new Ministerial Regulation No. 17/2025 on electronic RKAB submission via the Minerba One platform and to outline penalties for non‑compliance. They also highlighted tax‑collection growth, noting that mining taxpayers rose from 6,321 in 2021 to 7,128 in 2025 and sector revenue jumped from Rp 4 trillion in 2016 to Rp 45 trillion in 2024.

title: Indonesia aims to sign EU Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement by January 2026
body: Trade Minister Budi Santoso said the Indonesia‑EU Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (IEU‑CEPA) is slated for signing in January 2026, after legal drafting and translation, with ratification by the House of Representatives expected by mid‑year. The deal is projected to boost Indonesia’s growth to 8% by 2029 by granting zero tariffs on key agricultural, fisheries, forestry, textile and electronic exports to the EU and easing visa procedures.

title: OJK updates AI ethics code to address fintech risks and adds fairness principle
body: At the OECD Asia forum in Bali, the Financial Services Authority (OJK) unveiled revised AI ethics guidelines that expand the original six principles with a new fairness requirement and strengthen consumer protection, data reliability, financial inclusion, data protection, and cyber resilience, particularly for generative AI. The updated code, which supersedes the 2023 version, received full support from the OECD after a review and input process.

title: Bumi Resources launches Rp780 billion 9% sustainable bond to fund acquisitions and Wolfram project
body: Bumi Resources (BUMI) announced a Rp780 billion 9% fixed‑rate, five‑year sustainable bond, bringing its total bond program to Rp1.07 trillion, with quarterly interest payments starting March 2026 and principal due December 2030. Proceeds will be used for the acquisition of Jubilee Metals Limited (Rp340.88 billion) and Laman Mining (Rp333.60 billion), a loan to Wolfram Limited (Rp97.50 billion), and further capital and working‑capital expenditures for Wolfram.

title: Telkom Indonesia signs a Rp35.78 trillion conditional spin‑off agreement with Telkom Infrastruktur Indonesia to separate its wholesale fiber connectivity assets.
body: The agreement, signed on 20 October 2025, will transfer 99,9999997 % of Telkom Infrastruktur Indonesia (TIF) shares to a separate entity, aiming to sharpen Telkom Indonesia’s focus, improve efficiency and support Indonesia’s digitalization agenda; the transaction, valued at Rp35.78 trillion, is not expected to materially affect the company’s finances and requires shareholder approval at an extraordinary meeting on 12 December 2025.

title: Pantai Indah Kapuk Dua (PANI) announces a Rp15.73 trillion right issue with Multi Artha Pratama (MAP) committing to the majority of the subscription
body: PANI will issue 1,212 billion new shares (6.69% of equity) at Rp12,975 per share, giving MAP, which holds 87.78% of existing shares, rights to 1,064 billion HMETD and a commitment to purchase 36.21% of those rights worth Rp4.99 trillion, with additional optional orders up to Rp5.84 trillion. Proceeds of roughly Rp15.12 trillion will be used to inject capital into Bangun Kosambi Sukses (BKS) via acquisitions of Agung Sedayu (AS) and Tunas Mekar Jaya (TMJ), and the remainder will fund new shares of subsidiaries Cahaya Inti Sentosa (CISN), Karunia Utama Selaras (KUS) and Panorama Eka Tunggal (PET).

title: Bumi Serpong Damai (BSDE) to Issue Rp1.75 trillion of Sustainable Bonds and Sukuk Ijarah
body: BSDE will offer a total of Rp1.75 trillion comprising Rp1.25 trillion of sustainable bonds in four series and Rp500 billion of sustainable Sukuk Ijarah in two series, with the bond proceeds mainly used to prepay term loans from Bank Tabungan Negara and Bank Permata and the Sukuk proceeds allocated to working capital. The public offering runs from 10‑12 December 2025, with allocation on 15 December and electronic distribution on 17 December, followed by listing on the Indonesia Stock Exchange on 18 December 2025.

title: Energi Mega Persada Announces New Gas Discovery in Sengkang, South Sulawesi
body: Energi Mega Persada (ENRG) announced a new gas discovery on December 1, 2025, at the East Walanga Structure within the KKS Sengkang, South Sulawesi, with the EWL-1 exploration well showing a production rate of 25-36 million cubic feet of gas per day and a confirmed 0.2 trillion cubic feet of gas. The company, in collaboration with SKK Migas, plans further drilling to convert this discovery into commercial reserves, expecting a positive impact on future financial performance and shareholder value.

title: PT Raharja Energi Cepu Tbk. (RATU) Wins Bid for SMS Development Limited Shares, Securing Indirect Stake in Husky–CNOOC Madura Limited.
body: PT Raharja Energi Cepu Tbk. (RATU) has been declared the winner in the sale process for shares of SMS Development Limited (SMSDL), which holds a 20% indirect participation in Husky–CNOOC Madura Limited (HCML), a natural gas operator. This designation currently has no material impact on RATU's financials, with further transaction details pending a binding agreement; RATU shares experienced volatility, initially rising 3.09% to Rp12,500 before correcting 3.30% to Rp11,700 due to UMA.

title: UBS Group AG Reduces Stake in Bumi Resources (BUMI) by Transferring 4.66 Billion Shares
body: UBS Group AG divested 4.66 billion shares of Bumi Resources (BUMI) on November 25, 2025, in an internal transfer estimated to be worth around Rp1.1 trillion. This transaction reduced UBS Group AG's holding in Bumi Resources (BUMI) to 20.87 billion shares, equivalent to 5.62%, from a previous 6.88%.

title: Merdeka Copper Gold Injects USD50 Million into Subsidiary Merdeka Gold Resources
body: Merdeka Copper Gold (MDKA) has injected USD50 million into its subsidiary, Merdeka Gold Resources (EMAS), to support general corporate purposes including capital expenditure, operations, and working capital. This financing carries an interest rate of 3-month term SOFR plus a 5.76% annual margin and matures on December 31, 2026.

title: Goto Group (GOTO) Faces Rp 43.2 Billion Lawsuit from Former Employee
body: Goto Group (GOTO) is facing a Rp 43.2 billion lawsuit from former Chief Product Officer Gojek, Bruce McRae Haldane, over an industrial relations dispute concerning the interpretation of employment termination terms. The company asserts this individual dispute has no material impact on its operations and is preparing to follow the legal process, including appointing legal counsel.

title: PT XL Axiata Tbk and PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk Merger Forms PT XLSmart Telecom Sejahtera Tbk, Driving Telecom Sector Growth and Benefiting PT Prima Multi Usaha Indonesia Tbk
body: The merger of PT XL Axiata Tbk and PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk into PT XLSmart Telecom Sejahtera Tbk, effective April 16, 2025, creates a telecom powerhouse with over 94.5 million customers, targeting 38-40% EBITDA margins and driving overall industry growth. This consolidation is a significant catalyst for PT Prima Multi Usaha Indonesia Tbk, the largest distributor of EXCL products, which reported 25.98% YoY net profit growth in Q3 2025 and is diversifying into FMCG through PT Graha Prima Mentari Tbk.

title: INET Records Strong Q3 2025 Growth and Plans Major Digital Infrastructure Expansion via Rp3.2 Trillion Rights Issue and Strategic Acquisitions
body: PT Sinergi Inti Andalan Prima Tbk (INET) posted impressive financial results for the period ending September 30, 2025, with revenue surging 195% to Rp68.60 billion and net profit rising 819% to Rp19.37 billion, alongside significant growth in assets and EBITDA. To accelerate digital infrastructure expansion and strengthen its ecosystem, INET plans a Rp3.2 trillion rights issue to fund projects by PT Garuda Prima Internetindo, PT Pusat Fiber Indonesia, and PT Internet Anak Bangsa, and will acquire controlling stakes in PT Trans Hybrid Communication and PT Personel Alih Daya Tbk.

title: Energi Mega Persada (ENRG) Offers Rp500 Billion Bonds as Part of a Sustainable Program to Fund Debt and Working Capital
body: Energi Mega Persada (ENRG) is issuing Rp500 billion in bonds, part of its Rp4 trillion sustainable bond I program, to cover debt payments and working capital needs. These bonds, rated A+ by Pemeringkat Efek Indonesia (Pefindo) and set to be listed on Bursa Efek Indonesia (BEI), underscore the company's robust financial performance, strong operational track record in oil and gas, and optimistic future business prospects.

title: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara Tbk. (TOWR) Plans Interim Cash Dividend of Rp6.87 per Share for Fiscal Year 2025.
body: PT Sarana Menara Nusantara Tbk. (TOWR) has approved an interim cash dividend distribution of Rp6.87 per share, totaling Rp400 billion, for the 2025 fiscal year. Based on financial data as of September 30, 2025, this dividend reflects a payout ratio of approximately 15.66% and offers an estimated dividend yield of 1.26%.

title: PT Kustodian Sentral Efek Indonesia Reports New Institutional Investor Acquires 5.01% Stake in PT Esta Indonesia Tbk.
body: PT Kustodian Sentral Efek Indonesia reported that Citibank Hong Kong S/A PBG Clients SG, a Singaporean investor, acquired 206 million shares of PT Esta Indonesia Tbk. (NEST) from Hoo Anton Siswanto. This transaction, dated November 21, 2025, establishes PBG Clients SG as a new institutional investor with a 5.01% stake in NEST.

title: PT Janu Putra Sejahtera Tbk (AYAM) Strengthens Fundamentals with Asset Growth and Outlines 2026 Expansion Plans
body: PT Janu Putra Sejahtera Tbk (AYAM) reported a 36% increase in biological assets to Rp217.95 billion by September 30, 2025, with total assets reaching Rp478.21 billion, bolstered by stable 2024 operational performance and increased demand from the Makan Bergizi Gratis (MBG) program. The company plans 2026 expansion including layer farms in Kalimantan and enhanced breeding capacity, supported by national food investment.

title: PT Chandra Daya Investasi Tbk. (CDIA) Subsidiary PT Redeco Petrolin Utama (RPU) Breaks Ground on 12,000 Cubic Meter Bitumen Tank Facility
body: PT Chandra Daya Investasi Tbk. (CDIA), via its subsidiary PT Redeco Petrolin Utama (RPU), has begun construction on a 12,000 cubic meter bitumen tank facility, slated for operation in Q3/2026. This initiative strengthens the CDI Group's industrial infrastructure integration and expands RPU's total storage capacity to 75 tanks, supported by the Chandra Asri Group supply chain and Aster Chemicals and Energy Pte. Ltd.

title: PT Indomobil Multi Jasa Tbk. Plans Rights Issue (PUT IV) to Raise Rp504.83 Billion for Subsidiary Capital Injection
body: PT Indomobil Multi Jasa Tbk. (IMJS) plans a Rights Issue (PUT IV) to offer up to 2,194,937,500 new shares at Rp230 each, aiming to raise Rp504.83 billion. The net proceeds will be used as a capital injection for its 99.99% owned subsidiary, CSM, with PT Indomobil Sukses Internasional Tbk, holding 91.97% of IMJS, confirming it will exercise its rights.

title: PT BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk. (BFIN) Sets Interim Cash Dividend of Rp35 Per Share for End of 2025 Fiscal Year
body: PT BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk. (BFIN) will distribute an interim cash dividend totaling Rp519.73 billion, or Rp35 per share, for the end of the 2025 fiscal year, as decided on November 28, 2025. This dividend represents a 44.5% payout ratio from its Rp1,167 trillion net profit as of September 30, 2025, and offers a 4.64% dividend yield.

title: PT Citra Marga Nusaphala Persada Tbk. (CMNP) Targets Rp6.1 Trillion Capex in 2026, Doubling from 2025 Estimate.
body: PT Citra Marga Nusaphala Persada Tbk. (CMNP) projects a capital expenditure (capex) of Rp6.1 trillion for 2026, a significant increase from the estimated Rp3.22 trillion for 2025. The 2026 capex will primarily fund strategic projects like HBR 2-CMNP (Rp3.73 trillion) and Cisumdawu contractor debt (Rp1.09 trillion), with funding from operational cash, bank loans, and a potential rights issue.

title: PT Abadi Lestari Indonesia Tbk (RLCO) Sets IPO Price at Rp168, Targets Rp105 Billion
body: PT Abadi Lestari Indonesia Tbk (RLCO) has finalized its IPO price at Rp168 per share, the upper limit of its bookbuilding range, aiming to raise up to Rp105 billion with significant investor interest already noted. The company plans to allocate the IPO proceeds primarily for working capital to purchase bird's nest raw materials and as a capital injection into PT Realfood Winta Asia for similar procurement.

title: PT Multi Sarana Nasional Increases Shareholding in PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk.
body: PT Multi Sarana Nasional (MSN) acquired an additional 6,500,000 shares of PT Habco Trans Maritima Tbk. (HATM) on November 28, 2025, for approximately Rp2.52 billion, following a previous purchase on November 14, 2025. These transactions increased MSN's total ownership in HATM from 20.03% (1.73 billion shares) to 20.11% (1.74 billion shares).

title: Majukarya Mandiriindonesia Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Majukarya Mandiriindonesia bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK). This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Sell Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya sold shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK), decreasing holdings from 114,290,200 to 108,070,500. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Chemical Asia Corporation Pte, Ltd Sell Shares of Bank Victoria International Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Chemical Asia Corporation Pte, Ltd sold shares of Bank Victoria International Tbk (BVIC.JK) at approximately IDR 115, decreasing holdings from 2,978,334,404 to 2,256,924,404. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Francis Lay Sioe Ho Sell Shares of BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Francis Lay Sioe Ho sold shares of BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk (BFIN.JK) at approximately IDR 750, decreasing holdings from 343,908,180 to 338,908,180. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Hasmoro Buy Shares of PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Hasmoro bought shares of PT Medikaloka Hermina Tbk (HEAL.JK) at approximately IDR 1,410, increasing holdings from 747,553,489 to 748,766,889. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Iwan Buy Shares of BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Iwan bought shares of BFI Finance Indonesia Tbk (BFIN.JK) at approximately IDR 750, increasing holdings from 1,650,000 to 2,000,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK), increasing holdings from 101,390,800 to 107,730,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK), increasing holdings from 114,286,400 to 114,290,200. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Trukindo Persadasejahtera Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Trukindo Persadasejahtera bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK). This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Pt Fersindo Nusa Jaya bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK), increasing holdings from 107,730,000 to 114,286,400. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Trukindo Persadasejahtera Buy Shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Trukindo Persadasejahtera bought shares of Sumber Energi Andalan Tbk (ITMA.JK), increasing holdings from 60,043,500 to 62,593,500. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Darma Henwa Tbk’s subsidiary PT Mahadaya Imajinasi Nusantara becomes controlling shareholder of PT Gayo Mineral Resources
body: PT Mahadaya Imajinasi Nusantara (MIN) now holds 99.75% of PT Gayo Mineral Resources (GMR) after Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources approval, making GMR’s financials consolidable into PT Darma Henwa Tbk (DEWA). DEWA’s shares rose 1.41% to IDR 430, and the company targets IDR 1.7 trillion EBITDA and IDR 490 billion net profit in 2025.

title: PT Timah Tbk seeks Danantara Indonesia investment to fund downstream expansion at PT Timah Industri
body: PT Timah Tbk is preparing a business proposal for a capital injection from Danantara Indonesia, which would be directed to its subsidiary PT Timah Industri to support downstream tin‑chemical production, including new products such as Ethylhexyl Thioglycolate and TMP Solid. The company also received seized smelter units and heavy equipment transferred through the Attorney General, the Deputy Minister of Finance, and BPI Danantara to PT Timah.

title: PT PP Presisi Tbk secures new mining service contract in Maba Village, East Halmahera
body: PT PP Presisi Tbk (PPRE) won a contract to provide integrated mining services—including clearing, topsoil and waste removal, and ore production—for PT Position's Mining Development and Operations Work in Maba Village, East Halmahera, supporting its expansion in Eastern Indonesia. The company said the award aligns with its strategy to enhance mining operations safely and sustainably.

title: Macmohan Holdingsrise shares jump 3.7% after winning AUD 36 million underground mining contract in Indonesia
body: Macmohan Holdingsrise (MAH) rose up to 3.7% to AUD 0,555, its biggest intraday gain since Nov 17, after being awarded a 12‑month underground mining contract for the Kucing Liar mine in Central Papua, Indonesia by a Freeport‑McMoRan unit. The contract, covering development of all underground fixed facilities, is valued at AUD 36 million (≈USD 23.27 million).

title: Indonesia considers raising the domestic coal supply quota as it plans to lower 2026 production targets
body: The Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources is evaluating a cut to the 2026 coal production target to below 700 million tonnes and may increase the mandatory domestic market obligation (DMO) percentage to keep domestic supply around 200‑230 million tonnes, which analysts say is needed to meet electricity, cement and fertilizer demand. Industry experts note that domestic coal consumption has been steady at roughly 220‑233 million tonnes and that any DMO adjustment must be proportional to ensure fairness and market absorption.

title: PT Kereta Api Indonesia works to restore flood‑damaged rail lines in northern Sumatra
body: PT Kereta Api Indonesia (KAI) is deploying teams from its North Sumatra regional division and Aceh subdivision to repair tracks washed out by severe floods and landslides, with President Director Bobby Rasyidin overseeing the operation. The damage includes multiple washouts on the Medan–Binjai line and the Krueng–Geukueh–Bungkaih stretch, while the National Disaster Mitigation Agency reports hundreds of deaths and thousands injured in the affected provinces.

title: PT Mastersystem Infotama Tbk Announces 16 IDR Interim Dividend Yielding About 1.12%
body: The company will distribute an interim dividend of Rp 16 per share, totaling Rp 50.23 billion (16.18% of net profit as of 30 Sept 2025), approved by the board and scheduled for payment in December 2025. The dividend corresponds to a yield of roughly 1.12% based on the closing price of Rp 1,425 per share on 2 Dec 2025.

title: PT Archi Indonesia Tbk Announces US$30 Million Interim Dividend as Stock Surges 428% YTD
body: PT Archi Indonesia Tbk (ARCI) announced an interim dividend for 2025 of US$30 million (≈Rp 499.93 billion), which translates to roughly Rp 20.23 per share based on its 24.84 billion shares outstanding. The company's shares have risen about 428% YTD in 2025, closing at Rp 1,290 on 2 December, with the dividend payable between 10 December and 15 December 2025.

title: PT Raharja Energi Cepu Tbk (RATU) unveils a multi‑horizon upstream acquisition strategy and receives a speculative‑buy recommendation with a Rp20,000 target price
body: RATU plans seven block acquisitions worth US$10‑150 million each over the next three years, adopting short‑, medium‑ and long‑term investment phases while new Barito Group‑linked management and a US$9.4 million (5%) stake by Chandra Daya Investasi (CDIA) bolster its capital base; analysts at Samuel Sekuritas Indonesia recommend a speculative buy with a target of Rp20,000 per share, citing MSCI Indonesia Small Cap Index inclusion and projected 2025‑2026 revenues of US$52 million and US$59 million, respectively, though lower oil prices and acquisition delays pose risks.

title: Hasmoro increases stake in Medikaloka Hermina (HEAL) by buying 1.21 million shares at a discount
body: On 25 November 2025, Hasmoro purchased 1.21 million shares of Medikaloka Hermina (HEAL) at Rp1,410 per share, a 1.74% discount to the closing price of Rp1,435, costing about Rp1.71 billion. The acquisition raised Hasmoro’s total holding to 748.76 million shares, representing 4.87% of the company.

title: Jaya Sukses Makmur (RISE) posts Q3 2025 net profit of Rp51.92 billion, up 102% YoY
body: In Q3 2025, Jaya Sukses Makmur (RISE) reported a net profit of Rp51.92 billion, a 102.49% increase from the same period last year, with earnings per share rising to Rp4.74. The company’s revenue grew 30.78% to Rp294.14 billion, while total assets reached Rp3.5 trillion and equity rose to Rp2.73 trillion.

title: BFI Finance Indonesia (BFIN) director Iwan buys 350,000 shares while commissioner Francis Lay Sioe Ho sells 5 million shares ahead of a large dividend payout
body: On 1 December 2025, BFI Finance Indonesia (BFIN) director Iwan acquired 350,000 shares at Rp750 each, spending Rp262.5 million and raising his holding to 2 million shares (0.01%). At the same time, commissioner Francis Lay Sioe Ho divested 5 million shares worth Rp3.75 billion, leaving him with 338.9 million shares (2.25%), as the company announced an interim dividend of Rp35 per share totaling Rp519.72 billion payable on 18 December 2025.

title: Mastersystem Infotama (MSTI) to Distribute Interim Dividend of Rp16 Per Share
body: Mastersystem Infotama (MSTI) will distribute an interim dividend totaling Rp50.23 billion, or Rp16 per share, representing 16.18% of its Rp310.33 billion net profit as of September 30, 2025. The cum dividend date for the regular and negotiation market is December 10, 2025, with the payment scheduled for December 29, 2025.

title: Dian Swastatika (DSSA) Reports 27.31% Decline in Net Profit and 10.26% Drop in Revenue for First Nine Months of 2025
body: Dian Swastatika (DSSA) recorded a net profit of USD177.24 million for the first nine months of 2025, marking a 27.31% decrease from the previous year, alongside a 10.26% reduction in revenue to USD2.01 billion. Despite the drop in profitability, the company's total assets increased to USD4.17 billion and total equity rose to USD2.15 billion.

title: Archi Indonesia (ARCI) to Distribute USD30 Million Interim Dividend for 2025
body: Archi Indonesia (ARCI) plans to distribute an interim dividend of USD30 million (Rp499.83 billion) for the 2025 financial year, translating to Rp20,1260 per share. The cum dividend date for regular and negotiation markets is December 10, 2025, with payment scheduled for December 16, 2025, supported by a net profit of USD70.47 million as of September 30, 2025.

title: Rukun Raharja (RAJA) Reports Mixed Q3 2025 Financials with Net Profit Decline Amidst Revenue Growth
body: Rukun Raharja (RAJA) recorded a net profit of USD17.75 million for the period ending September 30, 2025, marking an 8.31% decrease from the previous year, despite net revenue increasing by 3.36% to USD196.04 million. The company's gross profit rose 9.47% to USD56.28 million, and total assets significantly grew to USD446.1 million from USD331.35 million at the end of the previous year.

title: Chengdong Investment Corporation Sell Shares of Bumi Resources Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Chengdong Investment Corporation sold shares of Bumi Resources Tbk (BUMI.JK) at approximately IDR 238, decreasing holdings from 29,703,117,930 to 25,989,764,030. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Andry Hakim Buy Shares of PT Cakra Buana Resources Energi Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Andry Hakim bought shares of PT Cakra Buana Resources Energi Tbk (CBRE.JK) at approximately IDR 1,020, increasing holdings from 226,903,374 to 227,000,074. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Pimsf Pulogadung Buy Shares of PT Geoprima Solusi Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Pimsf Pulogadung bought shares of PT Geoprima Solusi Tbk (GPSO.JK), increasing holdings from 323,035,900 to 326,369,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia considers using I‑UAE CEPA to lower food costs for Hajj and Umrah pilgrims
body: Coordinating Minister for the Economy and Trade Zulkifli Hasan said the Indonesia‑UAE Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement could enable duty‑free shipments to the UAE and onward to Saudi Arabia, reducing logistics costs for Hajj pilgrims. He has instructed the National Food and Drug Agency (BPOM) to coordinate with Saudi authorities to remove regulatory barriers, highlighting a potential market of up to Rp60 trillion (about US$3.6 billion) for Indonesian food supplies currently dominated by foreign providers.

title: Indonesia projects IEU-CEPA will raise EU-bound exports by more than 50 percent
body: The Coordinating Ministry for Economic Affairs says the Indonesia–EU Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement, slated for ratification in January 2026, will remove duties on 98% of Indonesian products and grant zero‑tariff treatment to key commodities such as palm oil, coffee and textiles, potentially increasing exports to the EU by over 50%. The pact is also positioned as a tool to help achieve the government's 8% economic growth target by 2029.

title: Mayora Indah (MYOR) to Issue Rp827.54 billion Sustainable Bond in Two Tranches
body: Mayora Indah (MYOR) will offer a total of Rp827.54 billion of bonds, split into a Rp363.52 billion Series A at 5.85% interest for 5 years and a Rp464.02 billion Series B at 6.15% interest for 7 years, with quarterly interest payments starting March 18 2026 and principal repayment on December 18 2030 and December 18 2032 respectively. The net proceeds will be loaned to its subsidiary Torabika Eka Semesta (TES) to fund working‑capital needs such as raw material and packaging purchases, and the public offering runs from December 12‑15 2025 with listing on the Bursa Eek Indonesia on December 19 2025.

title: Chengdong Investment Corporation raises Rp884.07 billion from Bumi Resources share divestment while retaining a 6.99% stake
body: Chengdong Investment Corporation sold a total of 3.71 billion Bumi Resources shares in two transactions on 28 November 2025, generating Rp20.4 billion from 84.76 million shares at Rp240.72 each and Rp863.67 billion from 3.62 billion shares at Rp238.02 each. After the sales, Chengdong’s holding fell to 25.98 billion shares, representing 6.99% of Bumi Resources, down from 7.99% (29.7 billion shares) before the divestment.

title: Surya Semesta Internusa executes Rp1.66 trillion in‑kind share and land contribution to consolidate its hotel units
body: Surya Semesta Internusa (SSIA) executed a Rp1.66 trillion in‑kind transaction on 1 December 2025, contributing shares of Sitiagung Makmur (SAM) (493.2 million shares, Rp366.93 billion), Surya Internusa Hotels (SIH) (823,329 shares, Rp379.05 billion) and Surya Semesta Realti (SSR) (85.42 million shares, Rp117.66 billion) as well as land from TCP Internusa (TCP) valued at Rp803.55 billion to Suryalaya Anindita International (SAI), which compensated with 17,102 new shares. The move consolidates SSIA’s hotel units under SAI to enable unified reporting, improve asset utilization and support future financing and growth of its hospitality business.

title: Adhi Karya (ADHI) Secures Rp14.1 Trillion in New Contracts, an 80.8% Increase from October 2025
body: As of 30 November 2025, Adhi Karya (ADHI) won new contracts worth Rp14.1 trillion, up 80.76% from the Rp7.8 trillion recorded in October, with 65% of the value coming from building projects and 74% of funding sourced from the government. The contracts, dominated by engineering & construction work, include major government infrastructure projects such as legislative and judicial buildings in Ibu Kota Nusantara, strengthening the company's project pipeline and financial outlook.

title: Bakrie Telecom posts a net loss of Rp3.11 billion in the first nine months of 2025
body: The company’s net loss narrowed 94% year‑on‑year to Rp3.11 billion, with earnings per share falling to Rp0.08, while operating profit rose to Rp15.18 billion and profit for the year turned positive at Rp13.01 billion. Revenue declined 12.8% to Rp85.92 billion and total assets fell to Rp57.77 billion.

title: Paradise Indonesia expects holiday-season boost to hospitality and commercial revenues, targeting double‑digit growth in FY2025
body: Paradise Indonesia (INPP) forecasts strong performance in its hospitality and commercial segments during the 2025 holiday season, citing a 5 % rise in foreign tourist arrivals to 5.3 million by September and Q3 revenues of Rp457.1 billion (+7.7 % YoY) for hotels and Rp417.8 billion (+14.4 % YoY) for its shopping centers. The company aims to achieve double‑digit growth for FY2025, leveraging flagship assets such as Sheraton Bali Kuta Resort, Beachwalk Shopping Center and the expanded 23 Paskal Shopping Center with the Skyward rooftop amusement park.

title: BTN launches Housingpreneur 2025 roadshow in Makassar to accelerate youth‑driven housing innovation
body: On 3 December 2025, BTN held the BTN Housingpreneur 2025 roadshow in Makassar, inviting students, creators and young entrepreneurs to turn housing ideas into bankable businesses, with a total prize pool of Rp1.5 billion and three‑month business incubation. The event highlighted successful alumni such as Moh. Rozaqul Anam of Paktukang and emphasized the city’s role as a “natural laboratory” for addressing Indonesia’s housing demand of over 91,000 units per year.

title: Mohamad Jusuf Hamka sells 100 million shares of Citra Marga Nusaphala, cutting his stake to 7.34%
body: On 2 December 2025, Mohamad Jusuf Hamka disposed of 100 million Citra Marga Nusaphala (CMNP) shares through Profindo Sekuritas Indonesia and Bank HSBC Indonesia, a transaction valued at roughly Rp133 billion based on the closing price of Rp1,330. After the sale, his holding fell to 491.48 million shares (7.34% of CMNP), down from 591.48 million shares (8.83%).

title: Waskita Beton Precast (WSBP) holds bondholders meeting to seek covenant waiver and outline recovery strategy for 2025‑2026
body: On 2 December 2025, Waskita Beton Precast (WSBP) convened a Rapat Umum Pemegang Obligasi (RUPO) where bondholders approved temporary suspension of financial covenants—including a minimum current ratio of 1.0×, a maximum debt‑to‑equity ratio of 2.5×, and a 100% debt service coverage ratio—for the 2025 and 2026 reporting periods, while the company presented its post‑restructuring performance, market‑share growth plans, and operational efficiency initiatives.

title: Sampoerna Agro (SGRO) posts net profit of Rp1 trillion in Q3 2025, up 178% YoY
body: Sampoerna Agro (SGRO) reported a net profit of Rp1 trillion for the quarter ended 30 September 2025, a 177.77% increase from Rp367.89 billion a year earlier. Revenue rose 36.79% to Rp4.61 trillion, gross profit to Rp1.61 trillion, operating profit to Rp1.36 trillion, while equity grew to Rp6.43 trillion and liabilities fell to Rp44.2 trillion.

title: All PT Bank Negara Indonesia (Persero) Tbk. (BBNI) Directors Pass OJK Fit‑and‑Proper Test, Munadi Herlambang Appointed Human Capital and Compliance Director
body: The OJK confirmed that every member of PT Bank Negara Indonesia (Persero) Tbk. (BBNI)’s board has passed its fit‑and‑proper test and approved the appointment of Munadi Herlambang as Director of Human Capital and Compliance, effective 1 December 2025. The approval is documented in OJK Letter No. SR491/PB.02/2025 dated 28 November 2025 and follows the annual shareholders’ meeting on 26 March 2025.

title: CEO Konosuke Mizuta resigns from PT Mizuho Leasing Indonesia Tbk (VRNA) ahead of extraordinary shareholders meeting
body: Konosuke Mizuta submitted his resignation as President Director of PT Mizuho Leasing Indonesia Tbk (VRNA), which was received on 1 December 2025 and will be processed according to OJK Regulation No. 33/POJK.04/2014, with the resignation to be considered at an extraordinary general meeting scheduled for 23 December 2025. The company, founded in 1993 and listed on the IDX since its 2008 IPO of 460 million shares at Rp100 each, had previously appointed Mizuta for the 2025‑2027 term.

title: PT MPX Logistics International Tbk. Subsidiary Changes Name and Expands Business Scope
body: PT MPX Logistics International Tbk. (MPXL) announced that its subsidiary, PT MPX Indorental Gemilang, officially changed its name to PT MPX Indo Gemilang (MIG) on November 27, 2025. This change also includes the addition of new business activities such as rental of mining and energy machinery, car repair, and wholesale trade of automotive parts, with no material impact on MPXL's operations or financial condition.

title: UNTR Forms PT Nusantara Industri Nikel Lestari (NINL) for Nickel Business Diversification
body: PT United Tractors Tbk (UNTR), through its subsidiaries PT Danusa Tambang Nusantara (DTN) and PT Energia Prima Nusantara (EPN), established PT Nusantara Industri Nikel Lestari (NINL) on December 2, 2025. This new entity, with DTN holding 99.90% and EPN 0.10% of its shares, aims to diversify UNTR's business into non-ferrous basic metal manufacturing and metal trading, with no material impact on UNTR's current operations or financial condition.

title: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia (Persero) Tbk. (BBRI) Reports Continued UMKM Business Expansion and Increased Optimism for Q4-2025
body: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia (Persero) Tbk. (BBRI) announced its UMKM Business Index at 101.9 for Q3-2025, indicating continued expansion, with the Business Expectation Index rising to 120.7 due to stable input prices, rising commodity prices, and government projects. Despite a recorded decline in business rentability impacting loan repayment ability, investment activities are increasing, and the UMKM Business Sentiment Index for Q3-2025 stood at 111.9, with expectations for Q4-2025 strengthening to 134.8 driven by holiday demand and government spending.

title: PT Infiniti Wahana Buy Shares of Steady Safe Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Infiniti Wahana bought shares of Steady Safe Tbk (SAFE.JK) at approximately IDR 280, increasing holdings from 459,170,813 to 459,570,813. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Turniady Widjaja Buy Shares of Roda Vivatex Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Turniady Widjaja bought shares of Roda Vivatex Tbk (RDTX.JK) at approximately IDR 11,181, increasing holdings from 50,393,300 to 50,394,600. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Bahtera Bintang Nusantara Sell Shares of PT Ketrosden Triasmitra Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Bahtera Bintang Nusantara sold shares of PT Ketrosden Triasmitra Tbk (KETR.JK) at approximately IDR 577, decreasing holdings from 110,300,000 to 77,709,400. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Handriyanto Sell Shares of Sidomulyo Selaras Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Handriyanto sold shares of Sidomulyo Selaras Tbk (SDMU.JK) at approximately IDR 117, decreasing holdings from 460,000 to 100,000. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Jemmy Kurniawan Sell Shares of PT Hetzer Medical Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Jemmy Kurniawan sold shares of PT Hetzer Medical Indonesia Tbk (MEDS.JK) at approximately IDR 89, decreasing holdings from 1,047,500,000 to 1,046,135,300. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Arkora Bakti Indonesia Sell Shares of PT Arkora Hydro Tbk.
body: According to the published announcement, PT Arkora Bakti Indonesia sold shares of PT Arkora Hydro Tbk. (ARKO.JK) at approximately IDR 1,025, decreasing holdings from 1,391,760,000 to 1,226,692,775. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Andhesti Tungkas Pratama Sell Shares of Darma Henwa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Andhesti Tungkas Pratama sold shares of Darma Henwa Tbk (DEWA.JK) at approximately IDR 422, decreasing holdings from 4,140,782,066 to 3,805,013,366. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Indonesia seeks to broaden Turkey preferential trade pact to boost bilateral trade
body: Indonesia’s Deputy Minister of Trade Dyah Roro Esti met Turkey’s Deputy Minister of Trade Mustafa Tuzcu in Cairo to push for an expanded scope of the Indonesia‑Turkey Preferential Trade Agreement, aiming to deepen economic ties and lay groundwork for a comprehensive partnership. Bilateral trade reached $2.4 billion in 2024, with Indonesia’s exports to Turkey up 26 % to $1.9 billion, and both sides are considering broader product coverage and cooperation in sectors such as textiles, footwear, iron‑steel and construction.

title: Indonesia to declare food self-sufficiency for rice and corn on December 31, 2025
body: Agriculture Minister Andi Amran Sulaiman said Indonesia will announce self‑sufficiency for strategic commodities such as rice and corn at noon on December 31, citing a record 3.8 million‑ton national rice reserve stored by state‑run logistics firm Bulog and a 13.6% rise in projected 2025 rice production to 34.79 million tons. The increase is driven by higher yields across Java, Sumatra, Sulawesi, Nusa Tenggara and Kalimantan, supporting the government's food‑security target.

title: Indonesia's Coordinating Minister Airlangga Hartarto announces measures to mitigate flood‑induced economic slowdown in Sumatra
body: Airlangga Hartarto said the government will activate credit‑restructuring and debt‑relief regulations for MSMEs, and launch infrastructure rehabilitation in North Sumatra, Aceh and West Sumatra to prevent a dip in the national growth target of 5.6 percent after floods and landslides killed 776 people, left 564 missing and affected 3.2 million residents. President Prabowo Subianto has visited the disaster zones and ordered air‑lifted aid to isolated areas.

title: PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia (Persero) Tbk adds BRI credit card as a QRIS payment source in the BRImo app
body: The bank announced that BRI credit cards can now be selected as the primary funding source for QRIS transactions on BRImo, covering both merchant‑presented and online payments. New users can apply for a virtual BRI credit card through the app and may receive vouchers up to Rp150,000 along with exclusive merchant discounts.

title: Batu Investasi Indonesia raises its Bahtera Bumi Raya stake to 82.30% with a silent purchase of 161.9 million shares
body: On 3 December 2025, Batu Investasi Indonesia bought 161,899,350 shares of Bahtera Bumi Raya (PGJO) with the assistance of BCA Sekuritas, increasing its holding to 654.98 million shares (82.30%). Based on the 3 December closing price of Rp1,115, the transaction is valued at roughly Rp180.51 billion.

title: Victoria Insurance (VINS) to raise Rp13 billion through a private placement of 92.85 million shares
body: Victoria Insurance (VINS) will issue 92.85 million new shares at an exercise price of Rp140 each, totaling Rp13 billion, with the placement approved on 20 Oct 2025 and the shares to be absorbed by Victoria Investama (VICO) and listed on the Bursa Efek Indonesia (BEI) by 15 Dec 2025. The proceeds will be used to meet minimum equity requirements, strengthen capital structure, and fund working‑capital needs, including investments in deposits, mutual funds, and bonds.

title: Rahayu Saraswati D Djojohadikusumo to acquire up to 20% of Perintis Triniti Properti
body: On 2 December 2025, a share purchase agreement was signed for Rahayu Saraswati D Djojohadikusumo, niece of President Prabowo Subianto, to acquire an initial 5% and potentially up to 20% of Perintis Triniti Properti from its main shareholders Kunci Daud Indonesia (KDI) and Intan Investama Internasional (III). The transaction, overseen by Director Ishak Chandra, is stated to have no material impact on the company's operations, legal standing, financial condition, or continuity.

title: Andhesti Tungkas Pratama sells 335.76 million Darma Henwa shares at a discount, cutting its stake to 9.35%
body: Andhesti Tungkas Pratama divested 335.76 million Darma Henwa (DEWA) shares under the Grup Bakrie banner at Rp422 per share, generating approximately Rp141.69 billion in proceeds, which is below the closing price of Rp432 per share on 28 November 2025. The transaction diluted Andhesti’s holding to 3.8 billion shares, or 9.35%, down from 4.14 billion shares (10.18%) prior to the sale.

title: Raharja Energi Cepu injects USD5.1 million to support PT Petrogas Jatim Utama Cendana's cash‑call for Blok Cepu
body: Raharja Energi Cepu (RATU) will provide a USD5.1 million cash‑call financing in 2026 to PT Petrogas Jatim Utama Cendana (PJUC) to help it meet its project funding obligations under the KKS and JOA for Blok Cepu. The injection aims to secure Petrogas Jatim’s participation, sustain operations in the Cepu Working Area, and may generate consolidated interest income and economic value for Raharja Cepu, which holds a 49% stake.

title: Triniti Dinamik (TRUE) plans a 757.11‑million‑share private placement to strengthen capital and fund operations
body: Triniti Dinamik alias True Land (TRUE) intends to issue 757,110,786 new shares with a Rp25 nominal value, representing up to 10% of its placed capital, and will use net proceeds for working capital, general business and project development. An extraordinary shareholders meeting is scheduled for 12 January 2026 to approve the placement, subject to OJK Regulation No. 42/2020.

title: Arkora Bakti Indonesia sells 165.06 million Arkora Hydro shares at deep discount, raising only Rp169.25 billion
body: Arkora Bakti Indonesia divested 165.06 million Arkora Hydro shares on 1‑3 December 2025 at prices ranging from Rp950 to Rp1,800 per share, generating Rp169.25 billion—about 61.7% below the market price of Rp4,700. The Bursa Efek Indonesia froze Arkora Hydro stock after a 246.86% one‑month surge, with the year‑to‑date rise reaching 410.87%.

title: Unilever Indonesia Announces Rp3.3 Trillion Interim Dividend for FY 2025
body: Unilever Indonesia (UNVR) approved an interim dividend of Rp3.3 trillion, representing 99.3 % of its net profit of Rp3.33 trillion for the nine months ended 30 September 2025, resulting in a dividend of Rp87 per share. The dividend will be paid on 30 December 2025, with the ex‑dividend date on 15 December and the record date on 16 December.

title: PT Kencana Energi Lestari Tbk wins 10 MW solar IPP tender with PLN for Tobelo project
body: PT Kencana Energi Lestari Tbk (KEEN) was selected by PLN to develop a 10 MW photovoltaic plant with an 8.4 MWh battery in Tobelo Utara, scheduled to start construction in Q2 2026 and generate about 20.4 GWh annually. The company also is bidding on additional IPP projects totaling 180 MW, aiming to expand its renewable energy footprint.

title: PT Solusi Sinergi Digital Tbk (WIFI) signs addendum with PT Kereta Api Indonesia (Persero) to expand fiber‑optic network to Sumatra
body: On 3 December 2025, PT Solusi Sinergi Digital Tbk (WIFI), operating as Surge, signed an addendum to its existing long‑term agreement with PT Kereta Api Indonesia (Persero) to build, install, operate and manage fiber‑optic cables along railway corridors in Sumatra, extending the project that previously covered Java. The expansion aims to improve high‑speed internet access, support digital services, education, logistics and the broader digital economy across the island.

title: Andry Hakim Increases Stake in PT Cakra Buana Resources Energi Tbk (CBRE) Amid Positive Corporate Developments
body: Andry Hakim acquired an additional 96,700 shares of PT Cakra Buana Resources Energi Tbk (CBRE) at Rp1,020 per share on December 1, 2025, raising his total ownership to 5.002%. This purchase, along with the impending RUPSLB for a right issue, the arrival of the Hai Long 106 vessel, and a Rp4.3 trillion contract with Guna Nusa Utama Fabricator (GUF), contributed to a 16% rise in CBRE's stock price.

title: PT Hua Yuan New Energi Indonesia Negotiates Acquisition of 51.18% Stake in PT Andalan Sakti Primaindo Tbk (ASPI)
body: PT Hua Yuan New Energi Indonesia is negotiating to acquire 51.18% of PT Andalan Sakti Primaindo Tbk (ASPI) shares, totaling 349,955,000 units, from its current controlling shareholder, PT Andalan Sakti Inti, with the transaction value still under discussion. This acquisition, intended to support the new controller's investment and business development in Indonesia, is contingent on due diligence and will trigger a mandatory tender offer.

title: PT Asuransi Tugu Pratama Indonesia Tbk (TUGU) Demonstrates Robust Financial Health Exceeding Industry Averages
body: PT Asuransi Tugu Pratama Indonesia Tbk (TUGU) exhibits strong financial health, with its Risk Based Capital (RBC) at 360.9% per September 2025 and Investment Adequacy Ratio (RKI) at 272.6% per July 2025, both significantly surpassing industry averages. Analyst Kharel Devin from Trimegah Sekuritas affirms TUGU's solid financial position, noting its optimal RBC level, 21% year-on-year growth in investment income to Rp509 billion by September 2025, and consistent 'a-' rating from AM Best since 2016.

title: PT Celebes Mining Resources Reduces Stake in PT Harapan Duta Pertiwi Tbk. (HOPE)
