body: PT Celebes Mining Resources divested 130 million shares of PT Harapan Duta Pertiwi Tbk. (HOPE) at Rp155 per share, totaling Rp20.15 billion, reducing its ownership from 22.1% to 16%. Following this divestment, HOPE's shares increased by 11.11% to Rp210 on Thursday.

title: PT Benteng Api Technic Tbk (BATR) Targets Significant Growth in Indonesia's Expanding Refractory Market, Supported by Strategic Investments and Share Buyback.
body: PT Benteng Api Technic Tbk (BATR) is poised for substantial growth in Indonesia's refractory market, driven by increasing demand from heavy industries, strategic investments funded by IPO proceeds, and targeting Rp 150 billion in revenue and Rp 14.8 billion in net profit for 2025. The company also completed a Rp 4 billion share buyback to strengthen ownership and investor confidence, with analysts from Kiwoom Sekuritas Indonesia highlighting BATR's bright prospects in a market currently dominated by imports.

title: Leyand International (LAPD) Plans Capital Strengthening for Business Alignment with PT JSI Sinergi Mas's Energy Expansion
body: Leyand International (LAPD) is planning corporate actions to strengthen its capital structure, aiming to align with its new controller, PT JSI Sinergi Mas, which is expanding into the energy and mining sectors, including silica. The company's equity stood at Rp26.7 billion as of September 30, 2025, a decrease from Rp28.3 billion at the end of 2024 due to a Rp21.1 billion capital deficiency.

title: PT Celebes Mining Resources Sell Shares of PT Harapan Duta Pertiwi Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Celebes Mining Resources sold shares of PT Harapan Duta Pertiwi Tbk (HOPE.JK) at approximately IDR 155, decreasing holdings from 470,781,500 to 340,781,500. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Daya Kemilau Nusantara Investama Sell Shares of PT Teknologi Karya Digital Nusa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Daya Kemilau Nusantara Investama sold shares of PT Teknologi Karya Digital Nusa Tbk (TRON.JK) at approximately IDR 90, decreasing holdings from 1,851,380,000 to 1,764,046,400. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Charnic Capital Tbk Sell Shares of PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT Charnic Capital Tbk sold shares of PT Fuji Finance Indonesia Tbk (FUJI.JK) at approximately IDR 790, decreasing holdings from 94,863,400 to 85,532,400. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: Kundy Wijaya Sell Shares of PT Kusuma Kemindo Sentosa Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, Kundy Wijaya sold shares of PT Kusuma Kemindo Sentosa Tbk (KKES.JK) at approximately IDR 31, decreasing holdings from 81,640,800 to 78,830,200. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT XLSMART Telecom Sejahtera Tbk Sell Shares of PT Mora Telematika Indonesia Tbk
body: According to the published announcement, PT XLSMART Telecom Sejahtera Tbk sold shares of PT Mora Telematika Indonesia Tbk (MORA.JK) at approximately IDR 432. This filing was disclosed by the exchange.

title: PT Daya Kemilau Nusantara Investama Sells Shares of PT Teknologi Karya Digital Nusa Tbk
body: PT Daya Kemilau Nusantara Investama, an insider, sold 53,333,400 shares of PT Teknologi Karya Digital Nusa Tbk, reducing its holdings from 1,817,379,800 to 1,764,046,400. The transaction was for collateral purposes.

title: PT Daya Kemilau Nusantara Investama Sells Shares of PT Teknologi Karya Digital Nusa Tbk
body: PT Daya Kemilau Nusantara Investama, an insider, sold 53,333,400 shares of PT Teknologi Karya Digital Nusa Tbk, a technology company, for collateral purposes. The transaction reduced the holder's ownership from 1,817,379,800 to 1,764,046,400 shares.

title: Indonesia confirms $3.1 billion of JETP funds available and $5.5 billion under negotiation for clean‑energy projects
body: The government said $3.1 billion of the original $20 billion Just Energy Transition Partnership (JETP) pledge is now mobilised, with an additional $5.5 billion being negotiated for pipeline projects such as floating solar, geothermal, waste‑to‑energy and transmission upgrades. JETP funding has risen to $21.4 billion, split between the International Partners Group ($11 billion) and the Glasgow Financial Alliance for Net Zero ($10 billion), and the United States' withdrawal will not affect the programme.

title: Indonesia's Deputy Housing Minister calls on sharia developers to lead the 3 Million Houses Program
body: Deputy Minister of Housing and Settlement Areas Fahri Hamzah urged sharia‑based property developers to become drivers of the 3 Million Houses Program, emphasizing the need to protect social housing from speculation and ensure fairness under President Prabowo Subianto's leadership. He said collaboration with the sharia property sector would accelerate the delivery of decent homes for low‑income Indonesians and help narrow urban‑rural disparities.

title: Indonesia reviews mining permits in Aceh and North Sumatra after deadly Sumatra floods
body: The Ministry of Energy and Mineral Resources has sent a team to re‑evaluate mining business permits (IUP) in Aceh and North Sumatra following flash floods and landslides that killed 836 people and left 518 missing, and the minister said any permits found to be in violation will be revoked. He also confirmed that the Martabe gold mine in South Tapanuli has ceased operations and is assisting in disaster response while its future status is under review.

title: PT GTS Internasional Tbk. secures Rp1.19 trillion credit facility from PT Bank Negara Indonesia (Persero) Tbk.
body: PT GTS Internasional Tbk. (GTSI) signed a Rp1.19 trillion credit facility with PT Bank Negara Indonesia (Persero) Tbk. (BBNI) that includes a Rp365 billion ship‑refinancing loan at 8% interest for 60 months and a USD50 million standby L/C facility, with 20% collateral requirements. The material transaction, exceeding 20% of GTSI’s equity, is disclosed without shareholder approval and is intended to strengthen the company’s working capital and liquidity.

title: PT Unilever Indonesia Tbk. declares Rp87 per‑share interim dividend for FY 2025
body: PT Unilever Indonesia Tbk. announced an interim dividend of Rp87 per share, totaling roughly Rp3.3 trillion for 37.98 billion eligible shares, with a record date of 16 December 2025 and payment on 30 December 2025. The company also reported Q3 2025 net profit of Rp3.33 trillion (up 10.81% YoY) and sales of Rp27.61 trillion, a modest year‑on‑year increase.

title: PT Pulau Subur Tbk commits to continued high dividend payouts and announces a Rp43 billion palm oil mill expansion
body: The company declared two dividend payments this year totaling Rp6.5 per share, aligning with its prospectus‑mandated minimum 30% payout and forecasting a 5% profit growth in 2026 driven by higher fresh fruit bunch prices and volumes. It also disclosed that the environmental permit for a new palm oil mill is in final stages, with a Rp43 billion capex expected to generate about Rp180 billion in annual revenue and an additional Rp5‑7 billion in profit.

title: PT XLSmart Telecom Sejahtera Tbk sells its 18.32% stake in PT Mora Telematika Indonesia Tbk at a deep discount
body: On 4 December 2025, PT XLSmart Telecom Sejahtera Tbk (EXCL) divested its entire 4,331,835,710 shares (18.32%) in PT Mora Telematika Indonesia Tbk (MORA) for an average price of Rp432 per share, totaling about Rp1.87 trillion—roughly 95% below the closing price of Rp8,850. Despite the large sell‑off, MORA’s shares rose 9.89% on 5 December, reaching Rp9,725.

title: PT PP Tbk Receives Bankruptcy Petition Summons from Jakarta Commercial Court Over Subcontractor Claims
body: PT PP Tbk was summoned by the Central Jakarta Commercial Court regarding bankruptcy petitions filed by subcontractors PT Atap Perkasa (claiming Rp4.03 billion) and CV Citra Pratama (claiming Rp6 billion) related to the KCBN Museum project. The company said the filings have not materially affected its legal standing, financial condition, or business continuity and will cooperate with legal proceedings.

title: Bank Mandiri wins five Bank Indonesia Awards in 2025, highlighting strong credit growth and expanded FX and digital payment operations
body: At the Bank Indonesia Award 2025, Bank Mandiri received five honors for its macro‑prudential, monetary and payment‑system contributions, including best sustainable finance and best foreign‑exchange dealer. The bank reported 9.38% year‑on‑year credit growth to IDR 1,403.79 trillion, a 32.06% rise in interbank FX transactions and a 28% increase in BI‑FAST transactions to 564 million moves in October 2025.

title: PT Perintis Triniti Properti Tbk appoints Rahayu Saraswati Djojohadikusumo as President Commissioner after extraordinary shareholders meeting
body: On 2 December 2025, shareholders representing 76.36% of voting rights approved the appointment of Rahayu Saraswati Djojohadikusumo, the nephew of President Prabowo Subianto, as President Commissioner of PT Perintis Triniti Properti Tbk (TRIN). The meeting also accepted the resignations of two directors, reassigned Septian Starlin to Commissioner, and appointed Gregorius Seloko Uyanto as Director, with the new board serving until the 2029 annual meeting.

title: PT PP (Persero) Tbk faces bankruptcy petitions from two subcontractors on the Museum KCBN Muarajambi project
body: The Central Commercial Court of Jakarta has summoned PT PP (Persero) Tbk after PT Atap Perkasa and CV Citra Pratama filed bankruptcy petitions over unpaid claims of Rp4.03 billion and Rp6.00 billion respectively for work on the Museum KCBN Muarajambi construction. PT PP (Persero) Tbk said it will cooperate and follow legal procedures.

title: Indonesia’s Low Interest Rate Environment Persists as BI Holds Rate and Deposit Yields Decline
body: Bank Indonesia kept the BI‑Rate at 4.75% in November 2025, prompting a gradual fall in average Rupiah deposit rates to 3.85% and foreign‑currency deposit rates to 1.82%, while credit rates also slipped, with OJK reporting a 50‑bp drop in investment loan rates. LPS projects further deposit‑rate reductions and stronger credit growth, and an OJK survey shows banking optimism with an IBP of 66 in Q4‑2025.

title: PT XLSMART Telecom Sejahtera Tbk Sells Shares of PT Mora Telematika Indonesia Tbk
body: PT XLSMART Telecom Sejahtera Tbk sold 4,331,835,710 shares of PT Mora Telematika Indonesia Tbk, reducing its holding to 0. The transaction type was a sell, with a purpose of divestment, and was completed at a price of 432.
